quotes.text: Then let yourself experience. If you are used to terms like meditation, try to forget the term during this procedure. Do not use any name. Free yourself from concepts, and experience the being of yourself and the motion of your own vitality. Do not question, "Is this right? Am I doing it correctly? Am I feeling what I should feel?" This is the book's first exercise for you. You are not to use other people's criteria. There are no standards but your own feelings.
quotes.translation: 然后让自己去体验。若你习惯使用"冥想"这类术语，在此过程中请忘记它。不要使用任何名称。从概念中解放自己，体验你自身的存有与生命力的流动。不要质疑："这样对吗？我做得正确吗？我感受到应该感受的吗？"这是本书给你的第一个练习。你无需使用他人的标准。除了你自己的感受，没有其他标准。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Emotions are a quite natural portion of action, and left to themselves are fluid. They have electrical validity, and shape. When an attempt is made to reject an emotion, this does not affect the emotion half as much as it affects the individual involved. The act of rejection in itself is detrimental and doomed to failure.
quotes.translation: 情绪是行动中相当自然的部分，若任其自然，它们具有流动性。它们具有电性效力与形态。当试图拒绝某种情绪时，这种拒绝行为对情绪本身的影响远不及对当事人的影响。拒绝行为本身具有破坏性且注定失败。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: I do not think you are pushing her, nor am I. It is known however that a feeling of affirmation and confidence is indispensable in any such circumstances. The tricky thing here is that a plus expectancy and inner confidence always brings the best results with the least effort. Ruburt is attracted by and yet doubtful of his own abilities. This is based upon the personal feelings of Ruburt, who is a woman now and does not wish to appear flighty or hysteric.
quotes.translation: 我不认为你在逼迫她，我也不是。但众所周知，在任何此类情况下，肯定与自信的感受不可或缺。棘手之处在于，正向期待与内在自信总能以最少努力带来最佳结果。鲁柏被自身能力吸引却又对其存疑。这基于鲁柏的个人感受——她现在是女性，不愿显得轻浮或歇斯底里。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I told you that we do not experience your time sequence. We travel through various intensities. Our work, development, and experience all takes place within what I term the "moment point." Here, within the moment point, the smallest thought is brought to fruition, the slightest possibility explored, the probabilities thoroughly examined, the least or the most forceful feeling entertained. It is difficult to explain this clearly, and yet the moment point is the framework within which we have our psychological experience. Within it, simultaneous actions follow "freely" through associative patterns. For example, pretend that I think of you, Joseph. In so doing I immediately experience -- and fully -- your past, present, and future (in your terms), and all of those strong or determining emotions and motivations that have ruled you. I can travel through those experiences with you, if I choose. We can follow a consciousness through all of its forms, for example, and in your terms, within the flicker of an eye.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你，我们不经历你们的时间序列。我们穿越各种强度。我们的工作、发展与经验都发生在我所称的"瞬间点"内。在此瞬间点中，最微小的念头得以实现，最细微的可能性被探索，概率被彻底检视，最轻微或最强烈的情感被容纳。这很难清楚解释，然而瞬间点正是我们拥有心理经验的框架。在其中，同时发生的行动通过联想模式"自由"延续。例如，假设我想起你约瑟夫。如此做时，我立即且完整地体验你的过去、现在与未来（以你们的时间观），以及所有支配你的强烈或决定性情绪与动机。若我选择，我可与你共同穿越那些经验。例如，我们可跟随意识穿越其所有形态——以你们的时间观而言，这发生在眨眼之间。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: What happens to a thought when it leaves your conscious mind? It does not simply disappear. You can learn to follow it, but you are usually frightened of turning your attention away from its intense focus in three-dimensional existence. Therefore, it seems that the thought disappears. It seems also that your subjectivity has a mysterious unknown quality about it, and that even your mental life has a sort of insidious dropping-off point, a subjective cliff over which thoughts and memories fall, to disappear into nothingness. Therefore to protect yourself, to protect your subjectivity from drifting, you erect various psychological barriers at what you suppose to be the danger points. Instead, you see, you can follow these thoughts and emotions simply by realizing that your own reality continues in another direction, beside the one with which you mainly identify. For these thoughts and emotions that have left your conscious mind will lead you into other environments.
quotes.translation: 当念头离开你的意识心智时，它发生了什么？它不会简单消失。你可以学习追随它，但通常你害怕将注意力从三维存在的强烈焦点移开。因此，念头看似消失了。你的主观性似乎带有神秘未知的特质，甚至你的精神生活也有某种阴险的断崖点——一个主观悬崖，念头与记忆坠入其中，消失于虚无。因此为保护自己，为保护主观性不漂流，你在自以为的危险点竖立各种心理屏障。但你看，你其实只需认识到：你的实相在另一个方向持续存在——与你主要认同的方向并行——便能追随这些念头与情绪。因为这些离开你意识心智的念头与情绪，将引领你进入其他环境。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "You" presently have a once-centered consciousness, in that "you" close off from your experience these other stages of consciousness in which other portions of your entire identity are intimately involved. These other stages of consciousness create their own realities as you create your own. The realities are, therefore, byproducts of consciousness itself. If you could become aware of these, they might appear to be other places to you, rather than realms or fields of different kinds of activities. If you probe into these realms you will be forced to perceive them with the root assumptions of your own system, translating feelings of warmth and comfort, for example, into images of warm shelters or buildings, or feelings of fear into images of demons.
quotes.translation: 你目前拥有一个以自我为中心的意识，因为你将自己完整身份中其他部分密切参与的其他意识阶段从经验中隔绝开来。这些其他意识阶段创造了自己的实相，正如你创造你的实相一样。因此，实相是意识本身的副产品。如果你能意识到它们，它们对你而言可能看起来像是其他地方，而非不同活动的领域或场域。如果你探索这些领域，你将被迫用自己系统的根本假设来感知它们，例如将温暖舒适的感觉转化为温暖庇护所或建筑物的意象，或将恐惧的感觉转化为恶魔的意象。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now there is a reason why these lessons must be learned in just this way. Elementally there is only creativity. Destruction is merely the changing of form. A cloudburst or a tornado knows nothing of destruction. This same energy encased within a human form is something else. There are different kinds of creativity, then, to learn, and a specialization in energy is focus and feelings that emerge; elemental energy becoming conscious of itself, and aware of issues that did not exist for it earlier; millions of molecules momentarily united with the living consciousness (Pause), filled with primal energy, now learning love, and forming highly sensitive psychic patterns, electrical charges that now form emotions instead of clouds; the innocent chaos of undifferentiated personality that exists behind the highly specified and truly sophisticated mechanism of one thought. And all of this before an individual is born within your system. In terms of time this is behind us all.
quotes.translation: 这些课程必须以这种方式学习是有原因的。从根本上说，只有创造力。毁灭仅仅是形式的改变。一场暴雨或龙卷风对毁灭一无所知。同样的能量包裹在人类形态中则是另一回事。因此，需要学习不同类型的创造力，而能量的专门化是焦点和涌现的感受；基本能量开始意识到自身，并意识到先前对它而言不存在的问题；数百万个分子瞬间与活生生的意识结合（停顿），充满原始能量，现在学习爱，并形成高度敏感的心灵模式、电荷，这些电荷现在形成情绪而非云朵；存在于一个思想的高度专门化且真正复杂机制背后的未分化人格的无辜混沌。所有这一切都发生在一个人在你的系统内出生之前。从时间角度来看，这在我们所有人之后。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Unfortunately, the fundamentalists accept literal interpretations of intuitive realities in such a way that they further narrow the channels through which their psychic abilities can flow. The fundamental framework, in this period of time, for all of its fervor, is not rich -- as for example Christianity was in the past, with its numerous saints. It is instead a fanatical Puritan vein, peculiarly American in character, and restrictive rather than expansive, for the bursts of emotion are highly structured -- that is, the emotions are limited in most areas of life, permitted only an explosive religious expression under certain conditions, when they are not so much spontaneously expressed as suddenly released from the dam of usual repression.
quotes.translation: 不幸的是，原教旨主义者以这样一种方式接受直觉实相的字面解释，以至于他们进一步缩小了其心灵能力可以流动的渠道。在这个时期，基本框架尽管充满热情，但并不丰富——例如过去的基督教，拥有众多圣徒。相反，它是一种狂热的清教徒脉络，具有独特的美国特征，且限制性而非扩展性，因为情绪的爆发是高度结构化的——也就是说，情绪在生活的大多数领域受到限制，只允许在某些条件下爆发性的宗教表达，这时它们并非自发表达，而是从通常压抑的堤坝中突然释放。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 829, MARCH 22, 1978 
9:30 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: You are beginning to form this psychological bridge. Now when you form it you do so with the inner realization that it reaches from you outward and you expect something to be on the other side, or the bridge would be useless. Do not, however, identify the bridge with what waits on the other side. The bridge is a living psychological bridge because in this type of communication feelings and emotions are involved. A steel bridge would get you nowhere. You are weaving threads of psychic energy, insights, and realizations, and also certain personality characteristics to form this bridge outward. Now you are beginning at your end and in doing so you form the initial version of your Adam. As this, Adam is highly legitimate and an aid.
quotes.translation: 你正在开始构建这座心理桥梁。现在，当你构建它时，你带着内在的认知，即它从你向外延伸，并且你期望另一边有东西存在，否则这座桥将毫无用处。然而，不要将这座桥与另一边等待的东西等同起来。这座桥是一座活生生的心理桥梁，因为在这种沟通中涉及感受和情绪。一座钢桥将无法带你到任何地方。你正在编织心灵能量、洞见和认知的线索，以及某些人格特质，以形成这座向外延伸的桥梁。现在，你从你的这一端开始，并在此过程中形成你的亚当的初始版本。作为这样，亚当是高度合理且有益的辅助。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Some individuals can with ease and exuberance imagine themselves in a fistfight, a brawl, unmercifully beating "the devil" out of an adversary. The same thoughts may fill another man with intense terror and grave feelings of guilt. This same man, however, who would not purposely entertain fantasies of such nature under normal conditions, may in time of war imagine himself killing the enemy with the greatest feelings of holy joy and righteousness.
quotes.translation: 有些人可以轻松而充满活力地想象自己在拳斗、斗殴中，无情地将“魔鬼”从对手身上打出来。同样的想法可能让另一个人充满强烈的恐惧和深重的罪恶感。然而，这个在正常情况下不会故意沉溺于此类性质幻想的人，可能在战争时期想象自己以最大的神圣喜悦和正义感杀死敌人。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Spontaneous talking out on Ruburt's part for this particular incident or times, to elicit the full release of pent-up Emotions that lay behind the uncovering, you see. In some such instances, proper suggestions will help. This is excellent therapy incidentally, for the release of the Emotions automatically releases the symptoms. A discharge of this type is required when unpleasant material of an Emotional kind is uncovered through the pendulum.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏方面针对这一特定事件或时刻的自发倾诉，是为了引发隐藏在揭示背后的压抑情绪的完全释放，你看。在某些此类情况下，适当的建议会有所帮助。顺便说一句，这是极好的疗法，因为情绪的释放会自动释放症状。当通过摆锤揭示出令人不快的情绪材料时，需要这种类型的释放。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: There are here also thrusts in all directions, with certain identifying pulsations that underlie all, and that have their origins within the inner self. As the vitality of the universe forms, of itself, the boundaries of the various fields of activity, and as vitality itself takes on the coloration of the various fields and forms the camouflage patterns within them, so also vitality, in the form of emotions that the camouflage patterns of the personality, even while this vitality forms both emotions and personality for every individual, every consciousness, may be thought of as a separate field of activity; and all the data relating to fields of activity may be seen to apply to any consciousness as well.
quotes.translation: 此处同样存在向各个方向的冲力，伴随着某些贯穿一切的基础脉动，这些脉动源于内在自体。正如宇宙的生命力自行形成各种活动领域的边界，正如生命力本身染上不同领域的色彩并形成其中的伪装模式，同样地，生命力以情感的形式——即使这种生命力为每个个体、每个意识同时形成情感与人格——也可被视为一个独立的活动领域；而所有与活动领域相关的资料，同样适用于任何意识。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: By recognizing these differences and honestly following the feelings through -- in other words, by riding the emotions -- you will be led to the beliefs behind them. A series of self-revelations will inevitably result, each leading you to further creative psychological activity. At each stage you will be closer to the reality of your experience than you have ever been.
quotes.translation: 通过辨识这些差异并诚实地跟随感受走到底——换言之，驾驭情绪——你将被引向它们背后的信念。一系列自我启示将不可避免地随之而来，每一步都将引领你进入更富创造性的心理活动。在每个阶段，你都将比以往任何时候更接近你经验的实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Perhaps I may be able to make mental enzymes clearer afterwards since they have a basic part to play in the universe, at least as I know it so far. Let me say though in your own experience you are familiar with steam, water and ice. These are all manifestations of the same thing. So can a seemingly physical chlorophyll be also a part of a seemingly immaterial emotion or feeling, but in a different form; and of course directed into this form or caused to take various forms as response to certain laws, as of course ice will not exist of itself in the middle of your summertime. And if I am not to be compared to a symphony, Joseph, you must admit I do well with a figurative baton.
quotes.translation: 或许我之后能更清晰地解释精神酵素，因为它们在宇宙中扮演着基础角色——至少就我目前所知而言。不过让我这样说：在你的经验中，你熟悉蒸汽、水和冰。这些都是同一事物的不同显化。同样地，看似物质的叶绿素，也可能是看似非物质的情感或感受的一部分，只是以不同形式存在；当然，它被导向这种形式，或根据某些法则被塑造成各种形态，正如冰当然不会在盛夏时节自行存在。约瑟，即便我不该被比作交响乐，你也必须承认我挥舞比喻的指挥棒还算得心应手。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: In daily language, objects have certain names. Obviously the names are not the objects, but symbols for them. Even these symbols, however, divide you as the perceiver from the rest of the world, which becomes objectified. You can yourself understand far more about the nature of the psyche, for example, than you think that you can. To do this, however, you must leave your daily language behind at least momentarily, and pay attention to your own feelings and imagination. Your language tells you that certain things are true, or facts, and that certain things are not. Many of your most vivid and moving feelings do not fit the facts of your language, so you disregard them.
quotes.translation: 在日常语言中，物体有特定名称。显然名称并非物体本身，而是它们的象征符号。然而，即便是这些符号，也将你作为感知者与其余世界分隔开来，使世界被客体化。例如，你对自己心灵本质的理解，远比你以为的要多得多。但要做到这一点，你至少必须暂时放下日常语言，转而关注自己的感受与想象。你的语言告诉你某些事物是真实的或事实，而某些事物则不是。但你许多最鲜活、最动人的感受并不符合语言的事实，于是你便忽视了它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now there are several ways of taking care of such matters. You can build form or a structure with lines, or a face, and leave it and wait for the emotion to fill it up, and then quickly with a touch here and there bring out that which has appeared, the movement that was within the form, that you barely sensed. Or you can perceive the movement, the emotion and the power first, and then paint the form that grows about it to form it.
quotes.translation: 处理这类事情有几种方法。你可以用线条构建形式或面容，然后搁置它，等待情感将其充盈，再迅速以寥寥数笔勾勒出已然显现之物——那存在于形式之中、你几乎未曾察觉的动态。或者，你也可以先感知动态、情感与力量，再描绘出围绕它生长、将其塑造成形的形式。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: If you have become afraid of emotion or the expression of feeling, or if you have been taught that the inner self is no more than a repository of uncivilized impulses, then you may have the habit of denying this deep rhythm. You may try to operate as if it did not exist, or even try to refute it. But it represents your deepest, most creative impulses; to fight against it is like trying to swim upstream against a strong current.
quotes.translation: 如果你已对情绪或感受的表达感到恐惧，或者你被教导内在自体不过是未开化冲动的仓库，那么你可能已养成否认这深层韵律的习惯。你或许试图装作它不存在，甚至试图反驳它。但它代表着你最深层、最富创造性的冲动；与之对抗，就如同逆着激流奋力向上游。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: It may outrage your intellect, and the evidence of your physical senses may shout that it is untrue, yet a belief in good without a belief in evil is actually highly realistic, since in physical life it will keep your body healthier, keep you psychologically free of many fears and mental difficulties, and bring you a feeling of ease and spontaneity in which the development of your abilities can be better fulfilled. After death it will release you from the belief in demons and hell, and enforced punishment. You will be better prepared to understand the nature of reality as it is. I understand that the concept does indeed offend your intellect, and that your senses seem to deny it. Yet you should already realize that your senses tell you many things, which are not true; and I tell you that your physical senses perceive a reality that is a result of your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 这可能冒犯你的理智，你肉体的感官证据也可能大声宣称这是不真实的，然而，相信善而不相信恶实际上是非常现实的，因为在物质生活中，这种信念会让你的身体更健康，让你心理上免于许多恐惧和精神困扰，并带给你一种轻松和自发的感受，在这种感受中，你的能力发展可以得到更好的实现。死后，它将使你摆脱对恶魔、地狱和强制惩罚的信念。你将更好地准备理解实相的本质。我明白这个概念确实冒犯了你的理智，你的感官似乎也否认它。然而，你应该已经意识到，你的感官告诉你的许多事情并非真实；我告诉你，你的肉体感官感知到的实相是你信念的结果。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Forget now that in this case such a division occurred, and imagine instead the successive thoughts and feelings that you possess. When you feel weak you are weak. When you feel joyful your body benefits and becomes stronger. Augustus's case simply shows in exaggerated form the effects of your beliefs upon your physical image. If you think, "Aha, then from now on I will only think good thoughts -- and therefore be healthy, and inhibit my 'bad' thoughts, or do anything at all with them but think them," then in your own way you are doing what Augustus did. He began by believing that some of his thoughts were so evil that they must somehow be made nonexistent. So inhibiting what you consider as negative thoughts, or assuming that they are so terrible, is no answer.
quotes.translation: 现在忘记在这种情况下发生了这样的分裂，而是想象你拥有的连续思想和感受。当你感到虚弱时，你就是虚弱的。当你感到快乐时，你的身体受益并变得更强壮。奥古斯都的案例只是以夸张的形式展示了你的信念对你肉体形象的影响。如果你认为，"啊哈，那么从现在起，我只想好的念头——因此就会健康，并抑制我的'坏'念头，或者对它们做任何事，就是不去想它们"，那么你就在以自己的方式做奥古斯都所做的事。他一开始相信他的一些念头如此邪恶，以至于必须让它们以某种方式不存在。因此，抑制你认为消极的念头，或者假设它们如此可怕，都不是答案。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: There are two ways to get at your own conscious beliefs. The most direct is to have a series of talks with yourself. Write down your beliefs in a variety of areas, and you will find that you believe different things at different times. Often there will be contradictions readily apparent. These represent opposing beliefs that regulate your emotions, your bodily condition and your physical experience. Examine the conflicts. Invisible beliefs will appear that unite those seemingly diverse attitudes. Invisible beliefs are simply those of which you are fully aware but prefer to ignore, because they represent areas of strife which you have not been willing to handle thus far. They are quite available once you are determined to examine the complete contents of your conscious mind.
quotes.translation: 有两种方法可以触及你自己的有意识信念。最直接的方法是与自己进行一系列对话。写下你在各个领域的信念，你会发现自己在不同时间相信不同的事情。通常会有明显的矛盾。这些代表了对立的信念，它们调节你的情绪、身体状况和物质体验。审视这些冲突。无形的信念会出现，统一那些看似不同的态度。无形的信念只是那些你完全意识到但宁愿忽略的信念，因为它们代表了迄今为止你不愿处理的冲突领域。一旦你决心审视你意识心智的全部内容，它们就很容易获得。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The many directions possible for the species exist now. Joseph reacted on a cellular level in one respect. The cells recognized the probable reality involved,6 and he, Joseph, felt that he was "at home" (in the Markle place), and yet consciously could not explain the feeling. In certain terms his mother will feel vindicated if Joseph buys that house, but the choice is still his and Ruburt's. If you pay more attention to what you think of as coincidences, you will discover another kind of order that underlies the recognized order you follow. This has all kinds of implications biologically as far as the species is concerned; you can perhaps understand, then, that there are also probable histories beneath your lives, individually and en masse.
quotes.translation: 物种可能的众多方向现在都存在。约瑟夫在一个方面在细胞层面上做出了反应。细胞识别了涉及的可能实相，而他，约瑟夫，感到自己"在家"（在马克尔的地方），然而有意识地无法解释这种感受。从某种角度来说，如果约瑟夫买了那栋房子，他的母亲会感到被证明是正确的，但选择仍然是他和鲁柏的。如果你更加注意你认为是巧合的事情，你会发现另一种秩序，它潜藏在你遵循的公认秩序之下。就物种而言，这在生物学上具有各种含义；那么你或许可以理解，在你的生活之下，无论是个体还是集体，也存在着可能的过往。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In the dream state and in certain other levels of reality, ideas and their symbols are immediately experienced. There is no time lag, then, between a feeling and its "exteriorized" condition. It is automatically experienced in whatever form is familiar and natural to the one who holds it. The psyche is presented with its own concepts, which are instantly reflected in dream situations and other events that will be explained shortly. If you dream of or yearn for a new house in physical life, for instance, it may take some time before that ideal is realized, even though such a strong intent will most certainly bring about its physical fulfillment. The same desire in the dreaming state, however, may lead to the instant creation of such a house as far as your dream experience is concerned. Again, there is no time lag there between desire and its materialization.
quotes.translation: 在梦境状态和某些其他实相层面，想法及其符号被立即体验。因此，在感受及其"外化"状态之间没有时间延迟。它自动以持有者熟悉和自然的任何形式被体验。心灵呈现其自身概念，这些概念立即反映在梦境情境和其他即将解释的事件中。例如，如果你在物质生活中梦想或渴望一所新房子，即使如此强烈的意图肯定会带来其物质实现，也可能需要一段时间才能实现那个理想。然而，就你的梦境体验而言，同样的渴望可能导致这样的房子立即被创造出来。再次强调，在渴望及其物质化之间没有时间延迟。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In your terms, your histories were not written by the people who worked the earth. They were created by the priests and the elite, who made up their own histories to suit their purposes -- to hold down the masses, for reasons that I will someday discuss, for they are important. Those histories never spoke of the vast, massive emotions and needs of the human beings involved, who listened, because their hearts and survival depended upon their doing so, to the voices that speak within the earth that your instruments even now cannot perceive. Those histories did not tell of the human beings who had to know what insects would crawl or fly from one end of a continent to another, so that they could be captured and roasted and eaten. They did not speak of the human beings who had to know what migrations of animals would roam through their land -- and when and where, and at what phase of the moon -- lest they starve....
quotes.translation: 用你们的话说，你们的历史不是由耕种土地的人书写的。它们是由祭司和精英创造的，他们编造自己的历史以符合他们的目的——为了压制大众，原因我将来会讨论，因为它们很重要。那些历史从未提及所涉及的人类那巨大而强烈的情感和需求，这些人倾听，因为他们的心和生存依赖于这样做，倾听地球内部的声音，你们现在的仪器甚至无法感知。那些历史没有讲述那些必须知道哪些昆虫会从一个大洲爬行或飞到另一个大洲的人类，以便它们可以被捕捉、烤熟和食用。它们没有讲述那些必须知道哪些动物迁徙会漫游过他们的土地——以及何时、何地、在月相的哪个阶段——以免他们挨饿的人类……
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2

quotes.text: "My heavyset friend was filled with the thrill of knowing that now words would spread faster. This is hard to specify, but he had the same feeling I have now about newspapers -- the daily spreading out of ideas, and the kind of tremendous power behind that ability ... I can see that corner of his shop/work area clearly in a half-light, illuminated by a candle in an enclosed mesh lantern sitting on a tabletop. This man had several apprentices, and he was a real artisan, putting ideas across in the form of movable type. I know that Gutenberg is credited with this invention, and probably rightly so; but I also feel this as one of those discoveries that appeared in several places at once, and that my beefy fellow's shop was in the general vicinity of Gutenberg's -- in Germany? I can't recall. This idea was 'shared' in many places at once, then.6
quotes.translation: 我那魁梧的朋友因预知消息将传播得更快而兴奋不已。这很难具体描述，但他当时的感觉与我此刻对报纸的感受如出一辙——思想每日铺展开来，以及那能力背后蕴藏的磅礴力量……我能清晰看见他作坊/工作区的那个角落，半明半暗间，桌上一个封闭铁丝网灯笼里的蜡烛照亮了四周。这人带着几个学徒，是个真正的工匠，用活字印刷的形式传递思想。我知道古腾堡被公认为这项发明的发明者，这大概没错；但我也觉得这是那种同时在多个地方出现的发现之一，我那壮实朋友的作坊就在古腾堡作坊附近——在德国？我记不清了。这个想法当时在许多地方同时被"共享"。

quotes.text: The more able the child is to force such an artificial identification, the greater its feelings of inner rebellion. The lack of a "suitable" father or mother image has "saved" more children than it has hurt. The psyche, with its great gifts, always feels thwarted and attempts to take countering measures. Your schools further continue the process, however, so that the areas of curiosity and learning become separated for males and females. The "she" within the male does indeed represent portions of his personality that are being unexpressed -- not because of any natural predominance of mental or emotional characteristics over others, but because of artificial specializations. The same applies to the "male" within the female. You have accepted this version of personhood, again, in line with your ideas about the nature of consciousness. Those ideas are changing, and as they do the species must accept its true personhood. As this happens, your understanding will allow you to glimpse the nature of the reality of the gods you have recognized through the ages. You will no longer need to clothe them in limited sexual guises.
quotes.translation: 孩子越是能强行达成这种人为的认同，其内心的反叛感就越强烈。缺乏"合适"的父亲或母亲形象，拯救的孩子远比伤害的多。拥有伟大天赋的心灵，总会感到受挫并试图采取对抗措施。然而你们的学校进一步延续了这一过程，使得好奇与学习的领域在男女之间被割裂开来。男性内在的"她"确实代表了他未被表达的人格部分——并非因为心智或情感特质天然优于其他，而是因为人为的专业化分工。女性内在的"他"也是如此。你们接受了这种人格版本，同样符合你们对意识本质的看法。这些观念正在改变，随着改变的发生，物种必须接纳其真实的人格。当这发生时，你们的理解将使你们得以窥见你们历代所认知的神祇实相的本质。你们将不再需要用有限的性别伪装来包裹它们。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At cellular levels the world exists with a kind of social interchange, in which the birth and death of cells are known to all others, and in which the death of a frog and a star gain equal weight. But at your level of activity your thoughts, feelings, and intents, however private, form part of the inner environment of communication. This inner environment is as pertinent and vital to the species' well-being as is the physical one. It represents the psychic, mass bank of potential, even as the planet provides a physical bank of potential. When there is an earthquake in another area of the world, the land mass in your own country is in one way or another affected. When there are psychic earthquakes in other areas of the world, then you are also affected, and usually to the same degree.
quotes.translation: 在细胞层面，世界以一种社会性交流的方式存在，其中细胞的生灭为所有其他细胞所知，青蛙的死亡与星辰的陨落具有同等分量。但在你们的活动层面，你们的思想、情感和意图，无论多么私密，都构成了内在沟通环境的一部分。这个内在环境对物种福祉的重要性与物理环境同样关键。它代表了心灵的大众潜能库，正如地球提供了物理潜能库。当世界其他地方发生地震时，你们自己国家的大地在某种程度上也会受到影响。当世界其他地方发生心灵地震时，你们同样会受到影响，而且程度通常相同。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Children experiment with the creation of joyful and frightening events, trying to ascertain for themselves the nature of their control over their own experience. They imagine joyful and terrifying experiences. They are in fact fascinated by the effects that their thoughts, feelings, and purposes have upon daily events. This is a natural learning process. If they create "bogeymen," then they can cause them to disappear also. If their thoughts can cause them to become ill, then there is no real reason for them to fear illness, for it is their own creation. This learning process is nipped in the bud, however. By the time you are adults, it certainly seems that you are a subjective being in an objective universe, at the mercy of others, and with only the most superficial control over the events of your lives.3
quotes.translation: 孩子们通过创造快乐与恐怖的事件进行实验，试图自行确定他们对自己经验掌控的本质。他们想象快乐与恐怖的经历。事实上，他们着迷于自己的思想、情感和目的对日常事件产生的影响。这是一个自然的学习过程。如果他们创造了"妖怪"，那么他们也能让它们消失。如果他们的思想能让他们生病，那么他们就没有真正理由害怕疾病，因为那是他们自己的创造。然而，这个学习过程在萌芽阶段就被扼杀了。等到你们成年时，你们当然会觉得自己是客观宇宙中的主观存在，受他人摆布，对自己生命中的事件只有最表面的控制。

quotes.text: The serf will invariably be looking at his time through a different focus than his future self could ever do. And think of the added challenges of feeling and perception where sexual changes between present and past incarnations are involved! Eroticism--and yes, outright sexual curiosity and arousal over reversed genitalia, for instance--must enter in sometimes, although in print at least these specifics of sex in connection with reincarnation seem to be a taboo subject. By contrast, there's plenty of material in the reincarnational literature on the generalized patterns of sexual behavior, from promiscuity to repression. (I wonder whether a long-term past-life sexual fantasy could be connected to a real sexual problem or challenge in a present--or future--life.)
quotes.translation: 农奴看待自己时代的方式，永远与他未来的自我所能做到的不同。再想想当涉及今生与前世之间的性别转换时，情感与感知所面临的额外挑战！情色——是的，甚至对反转生殖器的直白性好奇与性兴奋——有时必然会出现，尽管至少在印刷品中，这些与转世相关的性细节似乎是个禁忌话题。相比之下，转世文献中有大量关于性行为普遍模式的材料，从滥交到压抑都有。（我在想，长期的前世性幻想是否可能与今生——或来生——真实的性问题或挑战有关。）

source_raw.title: Even in God of Jane, which was published in 1981, Jane presented some relatively late material from Seth to show that he doesn't independently communicate with others. The idea that he'd done so can be inspiring, however. In Chapter 20 of her book, see Session 876 for August 27, 1979. After explaining how a couple of women (among others) had recently claimed that he had been in contact with them, Seth stated: "Now, I did not communicate with those women--but their belief in me helped each of them use certain abilities."

quotes.text: Now in a manner of speaking--though I see that little time has passed in this living room where I speak with Ruburt's permission--we have transcended time to some extent this evening, for in what I have said there are indeed hints and illusions--cadences--that can, if you are ready, give you a feeling for existence as it is outside of time's context. Even to try and verbally present such material necessitates alterations involving perception, for while that gridwork appears quite stable to your senses, giving you a reliable picture of reality, this is also because you have trained yourselves to pick up certain signals only. Others at other levels are (underlined) available. You can (underlined) tune into cellular consciousness, for example.
quotes.translation: 现在，可以这么说——尽管我看到在这个我经鲁柏允许说话的客厅里，时间几乎没怎么流逝——今晚我们在某种程度上超越了时间，因为在我所说的话中，确实有暗示和幻象——韵律——如果你准备好了，它们能让你感受到时间脉络之外的存在感。即使试图用语言呈现这样的材料，也需要涉及感知的改变，因为虽然那个网格结构在你们感官看来相当稳定，为你们提供了可靠的现实画面，但这同样是因为你们只训练自己接收某些特定信号。其他层面的信号是（强调）可用的。例如，你们（强调）可以调谐到细胞意识。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate. In your terms, it contains the physical history of the species in context with the probable future capabilities of the species. You choose your genetic structure so that it suits the challenges and capabilities of the species. You choose your genetic structure so that it suits the challenges and potentials that you have chosen. (Long pause.) It represents your physical reference point, your bodily framework. It is your personal physical property. It is a portion of physical matter that you have identified, filled out with your own identity. It is like a splendid ship, the body, that you have chosen ahead of time for a splendid challenging adventure--a ship that you have personally appointed that is equipped to serve as much as possible as a physical manifestation of your personhood.
quotes.translation: 你的基因结构会对你每一个念头、情绪状态和心理气候做出反应。用你们的时间概念来说，它既包含了物种的物理历史，也关联着物种可能的未来潜能。你选择自己的基因结构，使其契合你所选择的挑战与潜能。（长停顿）它代表你的物理参照点，你的身体框架。这是你个人的物理财产，是你所认同并注入自身身份的那部分物质。身体就像一艘华丽的航船，是你为一场精彩而充满挑战的冒险预先选定的——这艘由你亲自装备的航船，尽可能完美地作为你人格的物理显化而运作。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In a fashion, those stylized figures that stood for the images of God, apostles, saints, and so forth, were like a kind of formalized abstract form, into which the artist painted all of his emotions and all of his beliefs, all of his hopes and dissatisfactions. Let no one make God the Father look like a mere human, for example! He must be seen in heroic dimensions, while Christ could be shown in divine and human attributes also. The point is that the images the artists were trying to portray were initially mental and emotional ones, and the paintings were supposed to represent not only themselves but the great drama of divine and human interrelationship, and the tension between the two. The paintings themselves seemed to make the heavenly horde come alive. If no one had seen Christ, there were pictures of him.
quotes.translation: 从某种角度来看，那些代表上帝、使徒、圣徒等形象的程式化人物，就像一种形式化的抽象形态，艺术家在其中倾注了自己所有的情感、信念、希望与不满。例如，绝不能让天父看起来像个凡人！他必须以英雄般的维度呈现，而基督则可以同时展现神性与人性。关键在于，艺术家试图描绘的形象最初都是心智与情感层面的，这些画作不仅代表他们自身，更象征着神人互动的宏大戏剧及两者间的张力。这些画作本身仿佛让天国众军活了过来。即便无人见过基督，仍有他的画像流传。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "The book was based on the idea that nature was against man; and that religion was man's attempt to operate within that unsafe context. The feelings I was getting went even further, that religion or science or whatever weren't attempts to discover truth--but to escape from doing so, to substitute some satisfying tale or story instead. And I suppose that if someone persisted long enough, he or she would find the holes in the stories ... and undo the whole works. The idea of the stories was to save each man from having to encounter reality in such a frightening fashion.... The characters in the stories did this for him in their own fashion, and if you kept [searching] ... you threatened the fine framework of organization that alone made life possible...."
quotes.translation: "那本书基于这样的理念：自然与人类为敌；而宗教是人类试图在这个不安全的背景下运作的尝试。我感受到的情绪更进一步——宗教、科学或其他任何事物都不是为了发现真理，而是为了逃避发现真理，用某个令人满意的故事来替代。而且我认为，如果有人坚持得足够久，他或她就会在故事中找到漏洞……然后瓦解整个体系。这些故事的目的是让每个人免于以如此可怕的方式直面现实……故事中的角色以各自的方式替他们完成了这件事，而如果你继续[探索]……就会威胁到那个使生命成为可能的精妙组织框架……"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: "The magical approach puts you in harmony with your own individual knowledge of the universe. It puts you in touch with the magical feeling of yourself that you had as a child, and that is familiar to you at levels usually beyond your physical knowledge of yourself. It is better, then, to use the approach because you recognize it for what it is than to use it specifically in order to get something that you want, however beneficial. (All very intently:) There is no doubt at my level that use of the approach can clear up Ruburt's difficulties naturally and easily. If it is used because you recognize its inherent rightness in yourself, its inherent 'superior stance,' then it automatically puts you in a position of greater trust and faith. It opens your options, enlarges your vista of comprehension, so that the difficulties themselves are simply no longer as important--and vanish from your experience in, again, a more natural manner." (9:37.) "In a fashion, all of the material that I have given you in the annals of our relationship was meant to lead you in one way or another to a place where the true nature of reality could at least be glimpsed. You are at that point now.
quotes.translation: "魔法途径让你与自己对宇宙的个体认知和谐一致。它让你重新连接童年时那种对自己魔幻般的感觉，这种熟悉感存在于通常超越你物理自我认知的层面。因此，最好是因为你认识到它本来的面目而使用它，而不是为了得到你想要的东西而刻意使用它，无论那东西多么有益。（非常专注地：）在我的层面毫无疑问，使用这个途径可以自然而轻松地解决鲁柏的困难。如果你是因为认识到它在你内在固有的正确性、它固有的'优越立场'而使用它，那么它会自动让你处于更大的信任与信念之中。它打开你的选择，拓宽你的理解视野，使困难本身不再那么重要——并以更自然的方式从你的经验中消失。"（9:37）"从某种角度来说，我在我们交往记录中给予你的所有资料，都是为了以这样或那样的方式引导你到达一个至少能瞥见实相真实本质的地方。你现在正处于那个节点。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 5. It was inevitable that Jane's images would remind me of the note I'd written well over two months ago, on re-creating the past, or updating it, through nostalgia. In Chapter 11 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 936. Her images led me to search out the collection of battered black-and-white snapshots of her that somehow, some way, she'd managed to save from her early childhood. Along with scraps of her youthful poetry, the pictures are the only physical remnants she possesses of her first years, and studying them anew I realized just how valuable they really are. I talked of having them copied and enlarged by a professional photographer; I speculated about eventually having some of them reproduced in a book. That idea may have to wait, however: For some years Jane hasn't cared to be photographed--or have pictures of herself shown, no matter when they had been taken.

quotes.text: Your species shares with the other species a feeling of kinship for its kind. There is a great give-and-take of ideas. You end up, then, with a consensus, generally speaking, as to what a reasonable picture of agreed-upon reality is. Your system has frowned upon many experiences, considering them eccentric behavior in an adverse fashion, since your belief systems have so regimented behavior, and so narrowly defined sanity. (Long pause.) The intellect, I want to stress, is socially oriented. It is peculiarly suited, of course, to react to cultural information. (Pause.) It wants to see the world as it is seen by the minds of others. Through that kind of action it helps form your cultural environment, the civilization of which you are justly proud.
quotes.translation: 你们的物种与其他物种共享着对同类的归属感。思想之间存在着巨大的交流与反馈。因此，总体而言，你们最终会就什么是公认实相的合理图景达成共识。你们的体系一直不赞成许多经验，将它们视为反常的古怪行为，因为你们的信念体系如此严格地规范行为，并如此狭隘地定义理智。（长停顿）我要强调，智力是社会导向的。它当然特别适合对文化信息做出反应。（停顿）它希望看到的世界与他人心智所见一致。通过这种作用，它帮助塑造了你们的文化环境，以及你们引以为傲的文明。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: For much more Seth material on the counterpart concept, see Session 732 and Appendix 25 in Volume II of The "Unknown" Reality (Published by Amber-Allen Publishing, Inc., San Rafael, CA).

quotes.text: There are five steps. (Pause.) It is possible to cover all of them at once, or the process may take you some time. First however imagine your consciousness out of your body in its astral form. Next, feeling that you are in the astral form, tell yourself that from that image you will be able to see the image of the person that you were in your immediately past life. I cannot tell you whether you will succeed at once, as you may, or whether you will find it difficult. You, Joseph, being proficient with images, may find it easier than Ruburt. Now when you feel that you have this new image of your past self before you, then imagine that your consciousness is moving from your astral form into this past self. You should not do this until you see the whole form.
quotes.translation: 有五个步骤。（停顿）有可能一次性完成所有步骤，也可能需要一些时间。首先，想象你的意识以星光体形式离开身体。接着，在感受到自己处于星光体状态时，告诉自己从这个形象中你将能看到你上一世那个人的形象。我无法告诉你是否会立即成功，还是发现困难重重。约瑟，你擅长处理意象，可能比鲁柏更容易做到。现在，当你感觉面前有了这个过去自我的新形象时，想象你的意识正从星光体移入这个过去的自我。在看清整个形态之前，你不应进行这一步。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now I am only speaking because many of you have known that I was here. And it ill behooves me not to bid you welcome when you felt my presence. But in many of your discussions, there has only been surface thought and surface feelings... and we do not walk on the surface in this class. I would push your heads collectively and individually underneath... for you still do not understand that you create your physical reality! you form your blocks and shove them up upon the surface of the earth. You cannot solve the problems of your world on a surface level. If you could, you would have done it centuries ago. You must step out of your present perspective in order to see your world clearly. Here you are not to hide within the closet of 3 dimensionality and cry, "It is dark and I cannot see!" And refuse to use the inner light that can alone aid your vision.
quotes.translation: 我之所以现在开口说话，只是因为你们许多人已经知道我的存在。既然你们感受到我的临在，我不该不向你们表示欢迎。但在你们许多讨论中，只有表面的思想和表面的感受……而在这课堂上，我们并不行走于表面。我会把你们的头——集体和个体地——按到水面之下……因为你们仍然不明白，你们创造了自己的物质实相！你们形成自己的障碍，并将它们推挤到地球表面。你们无法在表面层次解决世界的问题。若能如此，你们早在几个世纪前就做到了。你们必须跳出当前的视角，才能看清自己的世界。在这里，你们不应躲进三次元的壁橱里哭喊："太黑了，我看不见！"却拒绝使用那唯一能助你视物的内在光明。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: You might even make a reasonable, and I mean reasonable, Ruburt, list of preferred circumstances such as shelves built, rooms painted and so forth, and carry them out yourselves. Whatever if anything now daily aggravates you, change these things and you will be amazed at your added peace of mind. I would have thought that you would have hit upon the entryway idea yourselves; something to keep your psychic energies contained, and again it is the feeling that the entryway gives you rather than the entryway itself, and the feeling that a desk and chair in another room gives Ruburt, rather than the desk and chair itself.
quotes.translation: 你甚至可以列出一份合理的——我指的是合理的，鲁柏——偏好情境清单，比如书架已装好、房间已粉刷等等，然后亲自去执行。无论现在每天有什么事情让你烦恼，改变这些事，你会惊讶于内心增加的平静。我本以为你们自己会想到"入口"这个主意；某种能收束你心灵能量的东西，再次强调，是入口给你的感受，而非入口本身；以及另一房间的书桌和椅子给鲁柏的感受，而非书桌和椅子本身。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: （I would like to note here that one night during the second week in December, possibly around the 11th or 12th, I awoke out of a sound sleep to become aware that both arms and hands were involved in my rather familiar sensation of enlargement and of elevation. The feeling of enlargement centers mainly on the tops of the limbs, as though they were swelling, and is not to be confused with the overall thrilling or tingling sensation I often refer to. I have now experienced both sensations many times. As far as I can consciously recall, this instances is the first I have experienced upon coming out of a sleep. I was lying upon my back at the time, and this is the position I use while trying psychological time.
quotes.translation: （我想在此说明，在十二月的第二周某夜，大约十一或十二号，我从沉睡中醒来，意识到双臂和双手都陷入了我相当熟悉的膨胀感和提升感。膨胀感主要集中在四肢顶端，仿佛在肿胀，这不应与我常提到的整体震颤或刺痛感混淆。我现在多次体验过这两种感觉。据我有意识地回忆，这次是我从睡眠中醒来后首次经历。当时我仰卧着，这也是我尝试心理时间时采用的姿势。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Subconsciously he feels that you are saying shut up, and this angers, humiliates and bewilders him. He struggles against such disclosures to begin with. Instead you see methods can be used to direct the emotions in other channels. You can get him to talk about other matters by asking for example about what he has written for the day. The subject chosen by you must be one in which he is immensely interested however. He will understand that you are merely trying to redirect him, and will not feel that you are restraining his freedom to say what he wants to say. He will understand what you are doing, but that is all right. He knows the emotional situation with your mother, and will not object.
quotes.translation: 潜意识里，他觉得你在说"闭嘴"，这让他愤怒、羞辱且困惑。他本来就挣扎着不愿透露这些。相反，你可以用方法将情绪引导到其他渠道。例如，通过询问他当天写了什么，来让他谈论其他事情。但你选择的话题必须是他极感兴趣的。他会明白你只是试图引导他，而不会觉得你在限制他表达的自由。他知道你与你母亲的情感状况，不会反对。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: At one time or another all of us on my plane give such lessons, but psychic bonds between teacher and pupils are necessary, which means that we must wait until personalities on your plane have progressed sufficiently for lessons to begin. To explain more clearly, lessons are conducted with those who are psychically bound to us, although reason is extremely important and I do not mean to minimize its value. Nevertheless, what you call emotion or feeling is the connective between us, and it is the connective that most clearly represents the life force on any plane and under any circumstances.
quotes.translation: 在我所在的层面，我们所有人都曾给予这样的课程，但师生之间的心灵纽带是必要的，这意味着我们必须等待你们层面的人格进步到足以开始课程。为了更清楚地解释，课程是与那些与我们心灵相连的人进行的，尽管理性极为重要，我并非要贬低其价值。然而，你们所谓的情绪或感受，是我们之间的连接物，也是在任何层面、任何情况下最能代表生命力的连接物。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Your present methods will simply bring you pat, manufactured results and answers. They will satisfy neither the intellect nor the soul. Since your universe springs from an inner one, and since that inner one pervades each nook and cranny of your own existence, you must look where you have not before -- into the reality of your own minds and emotions. You must look to the natural universe that you know. You must look with your intuitions and creative instincts at the creatures about you, seeing them not as other species with certain habits, not as inferior properties of the earth, to be dissected, but as living examples of the nature of the universe, in constant being and transformation.
quotes.translation: 你们目前的方法只会带来现成的、制造出来的结果和答案。它们既不能满足理智，也不能满足灵魂。既然你们的宇宙源于内在宇宙，而内在宇宙又渗透于你们存在的每个角落，你们必须看向从未看过的地方——进入自己心智和情绪的真实。你们必须看向所知的自然宇宙。你们必须用直觉和创造本能，注视周围的生物，不再将它们视为具有特定习性的其他物种，或地球可被解剖的劣等财产，而是视为宇宙本质的活生生的例证，处于持续的存在与转化之中。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I make these analogies because they are pertinent, yet I am aware that they can make you feel small or fear for your identity. You are more than a message, say, passing through the vast reaches of a superself. You are not lost in the universe. In a book we must use words, but such analogies can, if you let them, conjure up within your imagination some feeling of your intimate relationship with all other reality. To some extent, the feeling of grace is your emotional recognition of the necessity, purpose and freedom, the innate appreciation, of your rightness and your place in existence.
quotes.translation: 我举这些比喻是因为它们切中要害，然而我也意识到它们可能让你感到渺小，或为你的身份而恐惧。你不仅仅是一条信息，比如，正穿越一个超级自我的浩瀚领域。你并未迷失在宇宙中。在一本书里我们必须使用文字，但如果你允许的话，这些比喻可以在你的想象中唤起你对与所有其他实相亲密关系的某种感受。在某种程度上，恩宠感是你对必要性、目的和自由的情感认知，是对你自身正确性及在存在中位置的天生欣赏。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: (This feeling of repulsion is the reason for these notes, actually. If the beginning of movement in a piece of furniture is weird to start with, a refusal by the table to sit on the floor as one expects is much more so. This period lasted for perhaps ten minutes or more, while the three of us took turns shifting position so we could all test the pressure required to push the table down to the floor. There is no question that any of us were causing this effect; all of our movements were plain to see. During all of this the table was active to a greater or lesser degree, making it impossible to prop up a leg, say, with a shoe, etc. As interested as we were, we did not lose objectivity.
quotes.translation: （实际上，这种排斥感正是这些笔记的原因。如果一件家具开始移动本身就够奇怪了，那么桌子拒绝像预期那样坐在地板上则更加离奇。这段时间持续了大约十分钟或更久，我们三人轮流变换位置，以便都能测试将桌子压回地板所需的力量。毫无疑问，我们中没有人造成这种效果；我们所有的动作都清晰可见。在这整个过程中，桌子或多或少地活跃着，使得用鞋子之类的东西撑起一条腿等行为变得不可能。尽管我们很感兴趣，但我们并未失去客观性。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You appear in astral form in realities that are comparatively more advanced than your own. You are usually recognized because of your disorientation. You do not know how to manipulate. (Humorously): You do not know the customs. But whether you have a physical form or not, if you have emotions or feelings, these will take form. They have a reality. If you think strongly of an object, somewhere it will appear.
quotes.translation: 你以星光体的形式出现在那些相对比你自己的实相更先进的实相中。你通常因迷失方向而被认出。你不知道如何操控。（幽默地）：你不懂当地习俗。但无论你是否拥有物质形态，只要你有情绪或感受，它们就会成形。它们具有实相。如果你强烈地想着一个物体，它就会在某处出现。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: (Slowly:) In the same way, it is possible to love your fellow human beings on a grand scale, while at times hating them precisely because they so often seem to fall short of that love. When you rage against humanity it is because you love it. To deny the existence of hate then is to deny love. It is not that those emotions are opposites. It is that they are different aspects, and experienced differently. To some extent you want to identify with those you feel deeply about. You do not love someone simply because you associate portions of yourself with another. You often do love another individual because such a person evokes within you glimpses of your own "idealized" self.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）同样地，你可以在宏大尺度上爱你的同胞，同时有时又恨他们，恰恰因为他们似乎常常辜负了那份爱。当你对人类愤怒时，那是因为你爱人类。因此，否认恨的存在就是否认爱。并非那些情绪是对立的。而是它们是不同的面向，且被以不同方式体验。在某种程度上，你想与你深切感受到的人认同。你爱一个人，不仅仅是因为你将自身的部分与另一个人关联起来。你常常爱另一个人，是因为这样的人在你内心唤起对你自身“理想化”自我的惊鸿一瞥。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The emotions and the imagination, however, give you your closest contact with other portions of your own reality. They also liberate your intellect so that its powers are not limited by concepts it has been taught are true. Instead, such concepts are relatively true -- operationally true. For example, the physical laws that you are familiar with operate where you are. They are true, relatively speaking. In those terms you are one person physically objectified, staring upward in the scene just mentioned at an objectified sky. You weigh so many pounds, tilt your head at such-and-such an angle to peer upward at the skyscape, and physically speaking, you can be categorized.
quotes.translation: 然而，情绪和想象力让你与你自身实相的其他部分有最密切的接触。它们也解放你的理智，使其力量不受那些被教导为真的概念所限制。相反，这些概念是相对真实的——操作上真实的。例如，你所熟悉的物理定律在你所在之处运作。相对而言，它们是真实的。就那些术语而言，在刚才提到的场景中，你是一个物质化的个体，向上凝视着物质化的天空。你重多少磅，以某个角度倾斜头部向上凝视天空，从物质角度来说，你是可以被归类的。

quotes.text: When Ruburt's mother went to the hospital in his high-school years, he had a symbolic way of ridding the house of her psychic presence, and to add to his own sense of inner freedom. He threw open the windows, something usually denied him. This also has a therapeutic symbolism for him in connection with his grandfather, that is the feeling that cleansing nature rids the air of impurities. He also liked to have the radio blaring as a gesture of defiance and freedom, and within limitations, this would be an aid when the windows are opened. There are other reasons for the housecleaning suggestions also, involving individualistic symbols that have strong value for him.
quotes.translation: 当鲁柏的母亲在他高中时期住院时，他有一种象征性的方式将她的心灵存在从房子里清除，并增加自己的内在自由感。他打开窗户，这通常是禁止他做的。这对他而言也与他的祖父有治疗性的象征意义，即清洁的自然能净化空气中的杂质。他也喜欢把收音机开得震天响，作为一种反抗和自由的姿态，并且在限制范围内，当窗户打开时，这会有帮助。关于打扫房屋的建议还有其他原因，涉及对他具有强烈价值的个性化象征。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)

quotes.text: (3:27.) A city might be overrun by rats, for example -- a fine situation for the rats if not the populace -- but the entire picture would include unrest in the populace at large, a severe dissatisfaction with social conditions, feelings of dejection, and all of those conditions together would contribute to the problem. Rat poison may indeed add its own dangers, killing other small birds or rodents, and contaminating animal food supplies. Nor are insects invulnerable to such conditions, in such an hypothesized picture (long pause). Actually, all forms of life in that certain environment would be seeking for a balanced return to a more advantageous condition.
quotes.translation: （3点27分）例如，一座城市可能被老鼠泛滥——对老鼠来说是个好局面，但对民众则不然——但整体画面将包括民众普遍的不安、对社会状况的严重不满、沮丧的情绪，所有这些状况共同导致了问题。老鼠药确实可能带来自身的危险，杀死其他小鸟或啮齿动物，并污染动物食物供应。在这种假设的画面中（长停顿），昆虫也无法幸免于这些状况。实际上，在那个特定环境中的所有生命形式，都会寻求一种平衡的回归，回到更有利的状况。

Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: Now, using an analogy only, let me explain that your thoughts and feelings also give off shadows (intently) that we will here call hallucinations.2 They are quite valid. They have as strong a part to play in dream reality as shadows do in the physical world. They are beautiful in themselves. They add to the entire picture. A shadow of a tree cools the ground. It affects the environment. So hallucinations alter the environment, but in a different way and at another level of reality. In the dream world hallucinations are like conscious shadows. They are not passive, nor is their shape dependent upon their origin. They have their own abilities.
quotes.translation: 现在，仅用类比来解释，你的思想和情感也会投下阴影（专注地），我们在此称之为幻觉。它们是相当有效的。它们在梦境现实中扮演的角色，就像阴影在物质世界中一样重要。它们本身是美丽的。它们增添了整体画面。一棵树的阴影使地面凉爽。它影响环境。所以幻觉改变环境，但以不同的方式，在另一个现实层面。在梦境世界中，幻觉就像有意识的阴影。它们不是被动的，它们的形状也不依赖于它们的起源。它们有自己的能力。

source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Even the duration of an event or object in space or time is determined by the intensity of the thoughts or emotions that gave it birth. Duration in space is not the same as duration in time, however, though it may seem that this is the case. I am speaking in your terms now. An event or object that exists briefly in space may have a much greater duration in time. It may have far greater importance and intensity, existing in your memory, for example, long after it has disappeared in space. Such an event or object does not merely exist symbolically within your mind or memory -- but in your terms its actual reality continues as a time event.
quotes.translation: 甚至一个事件或物体在空间或时间中的持续时间，也由其产生的思想或情感的强度决定。然而，空间中的持续时间与时间中的持续时间并不相同，尽管看起来可能是这样。我现在用你们的术语来说。一个在空间中短暂存在的事件或物体，可能在时间中有更长的持续时间。它可能具有更大的重要性和强度，例如，在它从空间消失很久之后，仍存在于你的记忆中。这样的事件或物体不仅仅象征性地存在于你的心智或记忆中——而是用你们的术语来说，它的实际现实作为时间事件继续存在。

quotes.text: At Ruburt's stage of development however, he reacts as a result of emotional impetus, which is picked up rather directly, and which acts as additional energy in transmission. It is for this reason that he now and then does enjoy visitors to sessions, because he instinctively feels that he is thereby learning to manipulate and use these abilities oftentimes, and thereby learn the various subjective feelings that accompany valid perceptions, and to distinguish these from subjective feelings that turn out to be based upon invalid perceptions.
quotes.translation: 然而，在鲁柏的发展阶段，他因情感冲动而反应，这种冲动被相当直接地接收，并在传输中作为额外的能量起作用。正因如此，他偶尔确实喜欢有访客参加课程，因为他本能地感觉到，他因此正在学习经常操控和使用这些能力，从而学习伴随有效感知的各种主观感受，并将这些与基于无效感知的主观感受区分开来。

quotes.text: You may desire health, but believe implicitly in your state of poor health. You may desire spiritual understanding, while thinking that you are spiritually unworthy and opaque. (Pause.) When you set your longing against a present belief there is always conflict. Your belief will generate the proper feelings and imaginative endeavors characteristic of it. If you want to be healthy and continually contrast what you want with the present conviction in your poor health, then the belief itself, set up against the desire, will cause added difficulties. In such a case you seem to want the impossible. Desire and belief are not united but apart.
quotes.translation: 你可能渴望健康，但暗中相信你处于健康不佳的状态。你可能渴望灵性理解，同时认为自己在灵性上毫无价值且愚钝。（停顿。）当你将渴望与当前信念对立时，总是会有冲突。你的信念会生成适合它的恰当感受和想象努力。如果你想要健康，并不断将你想要的与你当前健康不佳的信念对比，那么信念本身与渴望对立，将导致额外的困难。在这种情况下，你似乎想要不可能的事。渴望和信念没有联合，而是分离的。

quotes.text: The larger pattern of human personhood demands a bisexual affiliation that allows leeway in sexual encounters, a leeway that provides a framework in which individuals can express feelings, abilities, and characteristics that follow the natural inclines of the personal psyche rather than sexual stereotypes. I am not speaking here of anything so simple as merely allowing women more freedom, or relieving men from the conventional breadwinner's role. I am certainly not talking about "open marriage" as it is currently understood, but of far greater issues. Before we can consider these, however, there are several points I would like to make.
quotes.translation: 人类人格的更大模式要求一种双性取向，允许性接触中的自由度，这种自由度提供了一个框架，在其中个体可以表达遵循个人心灵自然倾向的感受、能力和特征，而不是性别刻板印象。我在这里说的不是简单如仅仅给予女性更多自由，或让男性摆脱传统养家糊口角色的事情。我当然不是在谈论当前理解的“开放婚姻”，而是远为更大的议题。然而，在我们考虑这些之前，我想提出几点。

quotes.text: It may even improve. Since I have said that expectations are formed by the emotions, then it is obviously the basic emotions themselves that must be manipulated, since the expectations are the frameworks formed by the emotions. This is the starting point.
quotes.translation: 它甚至可能改善。既然我曾说过期待由情绪形成，那么显然必须直接操控基本情绪本身，因为期待正是情绪所构筑的框架。这便是起点所在。

quotes.text: Initially language had nothing to do with words, and indeed verbal language emerged only when man had lost a portion of his love, forgotten some of his identification with nature, so that he no longer understood its voice to be his also. In those early days man possessed a gargantuan arena for the expression of his emotions. He did not symbolically rage with the storms, for example, but quite consciously identified with them to such a degree that he and his tribesmen merged with the wind and lightning, and became a part of the storms' forces. They felt, and knew as well, that the storms would refresh the land, whatever their fury.
quotes.translation: 最初语言与文字毫无关联，事实上当人类失去部分爱意、遗忘与自然的部分认同感，以致不再理解自然之声亦属己声时，言语语言才应运而生。在远古时代，人类拥有表达情绪的浩瀚舞台。例如他们并非象征性地与风暴同怒，而是有意识地与之认同到如此程度：他们与族人融入风与闪电之中，成为风暴力量的一部分。无论风暴何等狂暴，他们都能感知并确知风暴将令大地焕然一新。

quotes.text: The intensity of the inner feeling is the dominating factor here. A sudden but intense feeling of hatred or resentment or fear may cause tragic physical circumstances, for example. A sudden and intense exaltation, however, will have the same immediate and literally astounding but opposite physical effects. That is, pure joy, even of brief duration, can literally change the direction of a life.
quotes.translation: 内在感受的强度在此处是主导因素。例如，突然而强烈的憎恨、怨恨或恐惧情绪可能引发悲剧性的物质境况。然而，突然而强烈的狂喜则会带来同样即时且惊人却相反的物质效应。也就是说，纯粹的喜悦——即便转瞬即逝——确实能改变人生的方向。

quotes.text: These brief definitions are very general: A paranoid is afflicted with systematic, logically-reasoned delusions of grandeur or persecution; the personality can be relatively stable otherwise. A schizophrenic suffers from a division between thought processes and emotions. The cause of schizophrenia is unknown, and the victim usually ends up hospitalized because of the severity of symptoms, which can include motor malfunctions, perceptual distortions involving hallucinations and delusions, strange behavior, and a withdrawal from reality. Yet the schizophrenic can also keep the use of his or her primary intellectual capacities.
quotes.translation: 这些简要定义非常概括：偏执狂患者受制于系统化、逻辑推理的夸大或被害妄想；除此之外其人格可能相对稳定。精神分裂症患者则存在思维过程与情绪的分裂。精神分裂症的病因未知，患者常因症状严重（包括运动功能障碍、涉及幻觉与妄想的知觉扭曲、怪异行为及脱离现实）而最终住院治疗。然而精神分裂症患者仍可能保留其基本智力能力。

quotes.text: You live in a physical community, but you live first of all in a community of thoughts and feelings. These trigger your physical actions. They directly affect the behavior of your body. The experience of the animals is different, yet in their own ways animals have both individual intent and purpose. Their feelings are certainly as pertinent as yours. They dream, and in their way they reason.
quotes.translation: 你生活在物质社群中，但首先生活在思想与情感的社群中。这些思想情感触发你的物质行动，直接影响你身体的行为。动物的体验虽不相同，但它们以各自的方式同样拥有个体意图与目的。它们的感受无疑与你同等重要。它们做梦，并以它们的方式推理。

quotes.text: If you deny yourself the direct experience of your own emotions, but muffle them, say, through too-strict discipline, then you can hurt others much more easily, for you project your deadened emotional state upon them--as in the Nazi war camps [men] followed orders, torturing other people--and you do that first of all by deadening your own sensitivity to pain, and by repressing your emotions.
quotes.translation: 若你否认对自身情绪的直接体验，而通过过于严苛的纪律将其压抑，那么你便更容易伤害他人——因为你将自身麻木的情绪状态投射到他人身上（正如纳粹集中营中奉命折磨他人的士兵）——而要做到这点，你首先必须麻木自身对痛苦的敏感度，并压抑自己的情绪。

quotes.text: I can only hope to evoke some feeling within you that is reminiscent of your own actual behavior at those hidden levels of dreaming activity, but they have remained highly pertinent in the development of all species with their environments, keeping the intents and purposes of one alive in the other. I have told you that in actuality, now, no genetic knowledge is gone from the earth. It does not vanish. It is retained in latent form within a kind of backup system, so that in terms of probabilities each species carries within its own genetic patterns the blueprints and specializations of each other's genetic sequence.
quotes.translation: 我只能希望能唤起你内心某种感觉，让你联想到自己在那些隐藏的梦境活动层面上的实际行为，但这些行为始终与所有物种及其环境的发展密切相关，使一方的意图和目的在另一方中保持活力。我曾告诉过你，实际上，现在地球上没有任何基因知识消失。它不会消失。它以一种潜在的形式保留在某种备份系统中，因此在概率上，每个物种在其自身的基因模式中都携带着彼此基因序列的蓝图和特化。

quotes.text: From it is woven all material of your world and mine. If you consider the wires again, then you could view them as solidified emotion, woven together, however with a strong cohesive and stiffening power of the intellect. With feeling alone, although it is the basis, you would have an inconsistent, very precarious framework. Reason is the form that disciplines and upholds these frameworks.
quotes.translation: 从它编织出你世界和我世界的一切物质。如果你再次考虑那些电线，那么你可以把它们视为固化的情感，编织在一起，然而却带有理智的强大凝聚力和硬化力量。仅凭感觉，尽管它是基础，你会拥有一个不稳定、非常脆弱的框架。理性是约束并支撑这些框架的形式。

quotes.text: Now. This will be a relatively brief session and we shall end shortly. I will here repeat my earlier suggestion that our friend read my earlier material. It is important that you all realize the ways in which your attitudes and feelings affect others through telepathic workings, and the ways in which the smallest thought affects your own Emotions, and the physical condition of your image.
quotes.translation: 现在。这将是一个相对简短的课程，我们很快就会结束。我将在此重复我之前的建议，让我们的朋友阅读我早期的资料。重要的是，你们都要意识到你们的态度和感觉如何通过心灵感应影响他人，以及最小的想法如何影响你们自己的情绪和你们形象的身体状况。

quotes.text: In certain circles now it is fashionable to deny the intellectual capacities in favor of feeling, sentiment, or intuitive actions. Intellectual concerns then become suspect, and recourse to reason is considered a failing. Instead, of course, intellectual and intuitive behavior should be beautifully blended. In the same way you have attempted to force the expression of love into a purely -- or exclusively -- sexual orientation. An affectionate caress or kiss between members of the same sex is generally not considered proper. The taboos include most aspects of the sense of touch in connection with the human body.
quotes.translation: 在某些圈子里，现在流行否认理智能力，而推崇感觉、情感或直觉行动。于是，理智的关注变得可疑，诉诸理性被视为一种失败。当然，相反地，理智和直觉行为应该优美地融合。同样地，你们曾试图将爱的表达强行纳入纯粹——或唯一——的性取向。同性之间的深情抚摸或亲吻通常被认为不合适。禁忌包括与人体相关的触觉的大部分方面。
