quotes.text: The authors instead tell you to be positive, compassionate, strong, optimistic, filled with joy and enthusiasm, without telling you what to do to get out of the predicament you may be in, and without understanding the vicious circle that may seem to entrap you. Such books, again, while sometimes of value, do not explain how thoughts and emotions cause reality. They do not take into consideration the multidimensional aspects of the self or the fact that ultimately each personality, while following definite general laws, must still find and follow his or her own way of adapting these to personal circumstances.
quotes.translation: 相反，这些作者告诉你要积极、富有同情心、坚强、乐观、充满喜悦和热情，却没有告诉你如何摆脱你可能陷入的困境，也不理解可能困住你的恶性循环。这类书籍，虽然有时有价值，但并未解释思想和情感如何创造现实。它们没有考虑自我的多维度方面，也没有考虑最终每个个体，在遵循明确的一般法则的同时，仍必须找到并遵循自己将这些法则适应个人情况的方式。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: If man paid more attention to his own subjective behavior, to those feelings of identification with nature that persistently arise, then half of the dictates of both the evolutionists and the creationists would automatically fall away, for they would appear nonsensical.4 It is not a matter of outlining a whole new series of methods that will allow you to increase your psychic abilities, or to remember your dreams, or to perform out-of-body gymnastics. It is rather a question or a matter of completely altering your approach to life, so that you no longer block out such natural spontaneous activity.
quotes.translation: 如果人类更多地关注自己的主观行为，关注那些持续出现的与自然认同的感觉，那么进化论者和创造论者的半数教条就会自动消失，因为它们会显得荒谬。这不是概述一套全新的方法，让你增强通灵能力、记住梦境或进行出体体操的问题。而是一个完全改变你对生活态度的问题，这样你就不再阻挡这种自然的自发活动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Jane held Session 900 for Chapter 5 of Dreams, in Volume 1, some 20 months ago. In Note 1 for that session I described a most vivid dream experience--one in which, Seth told me in the session itself, I had viewed the many-faceted light of my own being and of the universe. Participating in that event had been our friend, Floyd Waterman. Floyd is an extremely generous and caring individual who has helped us many times over the years; he's the contractor who converted half of our double garage for the hill house into Jane's writing room.2 Jane and I have each shared a number of psychic experiences with him.

quotes.text: In the inner world, your desires bring about their own fulfillment, effortlessly. That inner world, and the exterior one, intersect and interweave. They only appear separate. (Pause.) In the physical world, time may have to elapse, or whatever. Conditions may have to change, or whatever, but the desire will bring about the proper results. The feeling of effortlessness is what is important. It is quite proper for Ruburt's intellect to understand this, and to say, simply now, "That is not my realm. I will leave the solution to that problem where it belongs. We will use the magical approach here."
quotes.translation: 在内在世界中，你的渴望会毫不费力地自行实现。那个内在世界与外在世界相互交织、彼此渗透，它们看似分离实则一体。（停顿）在物质世界中，时间或许需要流逝，条件或许需要改变，但渴望终将带来应有的结果。这种毫不费力的感受至关重要。鲁柏的理智完全能够理解这一点，并简单地说："那不是我的领域。我会把那个问题的解决方案留给它该在的地方。我们将在此运用魔法之道。"
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: As you know the seasons are important to Ruburt. The combination of late winter and a pent-up emotional reaction to the room situation should be avoided if possible. He, Ruburt, has more psychic power than he knows, and an uncluttered feeling is important. He has an inner sense of order which may not be apparent, and a strong feeling for what he considers sacred or private functions.
quotes.translation: 如你所知，季节对鲁柏很重要。晚冬时节与他对房间状况积压的情绪反应，应尽可能避免同时出现。他拥有的心灵力量远超自身认知，而保持清爽的感受至关重要。他内在的秩序感或许并不明显，但对他视为神圣或私密的功能有着强烈感受。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now in many ways this applies to Ruburt also. The two of you seek refreshment together, then plunge into your inner realities. He is more Emotionally attuned than you, in that it is easier for him, when coming out of his private world, to rush toward you with spontaneity. You are both equally dependent however, basically, on this necessary Emotional refreshment. You both drink it in, and it sustains you for some period. It is used in your work, and in your joint work. It forms an inner framework of which you are sometimes aware. (Smile.) Without your feeling toward Ruburt and the resulting urge to Emotionally actualize yourself to another personality, your background could have prevented even artistic development. (Pause.) Rather than serve as a springboard for creativity.
quotes.translation: 现在，这在许多方面同样适用于鲁柏。你们俩共同寻求焕然一新，然后深入各自的内在实相。他在情感上比你更敏锐，当他走出私人世界时，更容易自发地冲向你。但你们俩在根本上同样依赖这种必要的情感滋养。你们汲取它，它支撑你们度过一段时间，被用于你们各自的工作与共同的工作。它构成了你们有时能觉察到的内在框架。（微笑）若没有你对鲁柏的情感，以及由此产生的在情感上向另一个体实现自我的冲动，你的背景甚至可能阻碍艺术发展。（停顿）而非成为创造力的跳板。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: What happens at the point of death? The question is much more easily asked than answered. Basically there is not any particular point of death in those terms, even in the case of a sudden accident. I will attempt to give you a practical answer to what you think of as this practical question, however. What the question really means to most people is this: What will happen when I am not alive in physical terms any longer? What will I feel? Will I still be myself? Will the emotions that propelled me in life continue to do so? Is there a heaven or a hell? Will I be greeted by gods or demons, enemies, or beloved ones? Most of all the question means: When I am dead, will I still be who I am now, and will I remember those who are dear to me now?
quotes.translation: 死亡之际会发生什么？这个问题问起来远比回答容易。从根本上说，即便在突发意外的情况下，也不存在任何特定的死亡时刻。不过，我会尝试就你所认为的这个实际问题给出实用答案。对大多数人而言，这个问题真正意味着：当我不再以物质形式活着时会发生什么？我会感受到什么？我还会是我自己吗？推动我生命前行的情感会继续存在吗？有天堂或地狱吗？我会被神明或恶魔、敌人或挚爱迎接吗？最重要的是，这个问题意味着：当我死后，我还会是现在的我吗？我还会记得现在珍爱的人吗？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In that framework, even the emotions of love and exaltation are seen as no more than the erratic activity of neurons firing (pause), or of chemicals reacting to chemicals. Those beliefs alone bring on suffering. All of science, in your time, has been set up to promote beliefs that run in direct contradiction to the knowledge of man's heart. Science has, you have noted, denied emotional truth. It is not simply that science denies the validity of emotional experience, but that it has believed so firmly that knowledge can only be acquired from the outside, from observing the exterior of nature.
quotes.translation: 在那个框架中，就连爱与升华的情感，也不过被视为神经元放电（停顿）或化学物质相互反应的随机活动。仅凭这些信念就会带来痛苦。在你们时代，整个科学体系都被建立起来，以推广与人类心灵认知直接相悖的信念。如你所见，科学否定了情感的真实性。这不仅是科学否认情感体验的有效性，更是因为它坚信知识只能从外部、通过观察自然的外在表象获得。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now these emotional confessions of Ruburt's annoy you considerably. They are not necessary. However your annoyance can be tempered with somewhat more understanding, and this in itself will tend to minimize their occurrence. When he indulges in this sort of thing he is feeling rebellious, you see, and this is a result of the built-up aggressive feelings of which I have been speaking.
quotes.translation: 现在，鲁柏这些情感上的坦白让你相当恼火。它们并非必要。然而，你的恼怒可以通过更多理解来缓和，这本身就会减少它们发生的频率。当他沉溺于这类事情时，他正感到叛逆，你看，这是我一直在说的积压攻击性情感的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: "The church was quite real to Ruburt as a child, through the priests who came (to the house) regularly, and through direct contact with the religious (grade) school, and the support offered to the (fatherless) family. Ruburt's very early poetry offended Father (Boyle), who burned his books on the fall of Rome, so he had more than a hypothetical feeling about such issues. Many of his fears originated long before the sessions, of course, and before he realized that there was any alternative at all between, say, conventional religious beliefs and complete disbelief in any nature of divinity.
quotes.translation: 对鲁柏而言，童年时的教堂因定期来访的神父、与教会学校的直接接触，以及对（失去父亲的）家庭提供的支持而显得真实。鲁柏早期诗歌曾冒犯博伊尔神父，后者烧毁了他关于罗马衰亡的书籍——因此他对这类议题的感受绝非空穴来风。当然，他的许多恐惧早在赛斯课开始前就已存在，早在他意识到传统宗教信仰与完全否定神性本质之间尚有其他选择之前。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth's comments of February 17 also reflect upon Jane's own ideas as she described them just 11 days ago; see the excerpts in Note 4. Then in this Chapter 9 of Dreams, see Note 8 for Session 920.

quotes.text: I want to make this clearer however. Suppose that you suddenly understand the concept of oneness with the universe, and that this particular inner sense of feeling concepts is to be used. You would then construct, as you construct dream images, a multitudinous variety of shapes and forms meant to represent the complicated varieties of life. You would then have the experience of entering into each of these lives. You would not think what it was like to be a bird, you would momentarily be a bird. This does involve a projection of sorts, and yet it still must be called a pseudo projection.
quotes.translation: 但我要让这点更清晰。假设你突然领悟了与宇宙合一的理念，并开始运用这种感受概念的内在感知。那么你会像构建梦境意象般，创造出千姿百态的形象来代表生命的复杂多样性。接着你会经历进入每个生命的过程——不是思考"成为鸟是什么感觉"，而是瞬间化身为鸟。这确实涉及某种投射，但仍需称之为伪投射。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Before you can be allowed into systems of reality that are more extensive and open, you must first learn to handle energy, and see, through physical materiaizationls, the concrete result of thought and emotion. As a child forms mud pies from dirt, so you form your civilization out of thoughts and emotions, and then see what you have created, and you must deal with it on its terms.
quotes.translation: 在获准进入更广阔开放的现实系统前，你必须先学会驾驭能量，并通过物质显化看见思想与情感的具体结果。如同孩童用泥土捏制泥饼，你用思想与情感塑造文明，然后目睹自己的造物，并必须按其规则与之相处。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Each person alive helps paint the living picture of civilization as it exists at any given time, in your terms. Be your own best artist. Your thoughts, feelings and expectations are like the living brush strokes with which you paint your corner of life's landscape. If you do your best in your own life, then you are indeed helping to improve the quality of all life. Your thoughts are as real as snowflakes or raindrops or clouds. They mix and merge with the thoughts of others, to form man's livingscape, providing the vast mental elements from which physical events will be formed.
quotes.translation: 每个活着的人都在协助描绘特定时代（以你们的时间观而言）的文明活画卷。做自己最好的艺术家。你的思想、情感与期待如同鲜活的笔触，勾勒出你生命风景的角落。若你在自身生命中做到最好，便确实在提升所有生命的品质。你的思想与雪花、雨滴或云朵同样真实，它们与他人的思想交融汇聚，形成人类的心灵景观，为物质事件的成形提供浩瀚的精神元素。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Over two years ago, in Note 2 for Session 801, and in the opening notes for Session 805, I described our decision to add the writing room to the house. All four of us involved in the moving were quite amused, then -- for the friends who helped Jane and me carry the furniture yesterday are the father-and-son contracting team that built the room in the first place.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: It is, again, the ego's misguided attempt to stand apart to gain stability, that makes the ego react in such a manner. It fears loss of control and of discipline. But all appearances to the contrary, the emotions are natural controlling devices that in themselves aid discipline, since they allow for the spontaneous flow of energies outward. It is only when they are denied that they become explosive or dangerous to the personality. Then indeed they result in explosive barrages, ranting and shouting, organic and psychological illnesses, and of unfortunate manifestations.
quotes.translation: 再次强调，正是自我试图通过抽离来获得稳定的错误企图，导致它做出如此反应。它害怕失去控制与纪律。但表象恰恰相反，情感本身就是自然的调控装置，能促进纪律——因为它们允许能量自发向外流动。只有当情感被否定时，才会对人格产生爆炸性危害。那时它们确实会引发狂轰滥炸般的宣泄、咆哮怒吼、器质性及心理疾病，以及不幸的显化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Either it attempts to return the emotion to a subjective state, or it attempts to objectify it further away from itself. In either case the ego is at fault for not assimilating or accepting the emotion. It is easy to see then that the ego is itself a series of actions, that it is a collection of more or less similar actions, selected from a larger mainstream of other actions.
quotes.translation: 它要么试图将情感压回主观状态，要么试图将其进一步客体化远离自身。无论哪种情况，自我都因未能同化或接纳情感而失职。由此易见，自我本身即是一系列行动，是从更庞大的行动主流中筛选出的、或多或少相似的行动集合。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: Any such communications coming through the inner senses will exist in your psychological time. I have said also that this psychological time operates during sleep and during quiet times of consciousness. Now in dreams and in the dream framework you have the feeling of experiencing many hours, or even days. These days or hours that you seem to experience in dreams are not recorded by the physical body, and are outside of your physical time camouflage. If in a dream for example you experience within the framework two days, physically you do not age for these two days.
quotes.translation: 任何透过内在感官传来的这类讯息，都会存在于你们的心理时间中。我也说过，这种心理时间在睡眠时和意识清醒的宁静时刻运作。在梦境与梦的架构里，你会有经历数小时甚至数天的感受。这些你在梦中似乎经历的时日，肉体并未记录，且处于你们物理时间幻相之外。举例来说，若你在梦的架构中经历了两天，肉体并不会因这两天而衰老。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: For one thing, you are dealing with different kinds of consciousness than your own. They are focused consciousnesses, surely, each one feeling itself at life's center. While this is the case, however, these other forms of consciousness also identify then with the source of nature from which they emerge. In a way impossible to explain, the fly and the spider are connected, and aware of the connection. Not as hunter and prey, but as individual participants in deeper processes. Together they work toward a joint kind of value fulfillment, in which both are fulfilled.
quotes.translation: 首先，你接触的是与你自身不同种类的意识。这些意识无疑是聚焦的，每个都感觉自己处于生命的中心。然而即便如此，这些其他形式的意识也会认同于它们所源自的自然本源。以一种难以解释的方式，苍蝇与蜘蛛是相连的，并且意识到这种关联——并非作为猎食者与猎物，而是作为更深层进程中的个体参与者。它们共同致力于一种联合的价值完成，在其中两者都获得圆满。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (In Note 2 for Session 801, I commented on the sociological implications represented by her correspondence.
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: (11:09.) Then, according to your beliefs, you deal with the physical dilemma as it is presented in those terms. You will react individually with your own purposes in mind. Your own unique and highly private beliefs help bring about the overall emotional condition. The pool of emotional energy into which your emotions flow is still composed of unalike charges, but generally speaking, the individual contribution of all those participating will fall into a coherent pattern that gives impetus and direction to the storm, providing the charge and the power behind it.
quotes.translation: （11点09分）接着，根据你们的信念，你们会以那些条件所呈现的方式处理物理困境。你们会带着各自的目的做出个人反应。你们独特且高度私密的信念，有助于促成整体的情绪状态。你们的情绪所汇入的情绪能量池，仍由不同的电荷组成，但一般而言，所有参与者的个别贡献会落入一个连贯的模式中，为风暴提供动力与方向，成为其背后的电荷与力量。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Ruburt was well prepared for the call by then, and for the visit. Now the visit and Ruburt's earlier feelings and thoughts were part of the same event, except that his subjective experience gave him clues as to the inner processes by which all events take place. More is involved than the simple question: Did he perceive the visit precognitively? More is involved than the question: Did he perceive his information directly from the minds of his friends, or from the letter itself, which had already been mailed, of course, and was on its way to Ruburt at the time?
quotes.translation: 到那时，鲁柏已为那通电话和那次来访做好了充分准备。如今，那次来访与鲁柏先前的感受和想法，属于同一事件的一部分，只不过他的主观经验为他提供了线索，让他了解所有事件发生的内在过程。这涉及的不只是简单的问题：他是否预知了那次来访？也不只是：他是否直接从朋友们的思想中，或从那封已寄出、正在寄往鲁柏途中的信函本身获取了信息？
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: It is this basic feeling about the book and Saratoga that suddenly activated past associations and brought on some identification with his mother. He should remember here that he is not the person who lived in Saratoga now, unless he chooses in a self limiting way to be so. The book itself, oddly enough, provides a certain protection for it informs others of his basic strength. It shocked him to know that people of the past were reading the book in his present, and seemed to draw him closer to those original associations that caused him to leave Saratoga.
quotes.translation: 正是这种对这本书和萨拉托加的基本感受，突然激活了过去的联想，并引发了他与母亲的部分认同。他应在此记住，他现在并非那个曾住在萨拉托加的人，除非他以自我限制的方式选择如此。这本书本身，奇妙地提供了一种保护，因为它向他人揭示了他的基本力量。得知过去的人们正在他当下的时空阅读这本书，令他震惊，似乎将他拉回那些最初导致他离开萨拉托加的联想。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: Let us become more practical, and see how these issues merge in your reality. Some of this requires a great honesty on your own parts, as you try to recall some feelings and daydreams that you have tried to put away or forget or disown. Why are some people, then, born with conditions that are certainly experienced as genetically defective, granting even the overall value of such variances on the part of the species? For, again, I must stress the fact that in its way nature makes no such judgments, regardless of the beliefs of your science or religions.
quotes.translation: 让我们变得更实际些，看看这些问题如何在你们的实相中融合。这需要你们自身极大的诚实，因为你们要试着回忆那些曾试图搁置、遗忘或否认的感受与白日梦。那么，为何有些人天生就带有被确认为基因缺陷的状况——即便承认这类变异对物种的整体价值？因为，我必须再次强调，大自然以其自身方式并不做此类判断，无论你们的科学或宗教信念如何。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: An analyst might consider Augustus as schizophrenic and label him neatly, but such terms are basically meaningless. If the analyst, over a period of time, should convince Augustus that his condition in the present resulted from some specific inhibited event in the past, and if the analyst was an intuitive and understanding man, then Augustus might change his beliefs enough so that some kind of "cure" was worked. He would then conveniently remember such an event and display the expected emotions as he re-experienced it. Unfortunately in his present state, powerless as it were without Augustus Two, he might also simply call on his "alter ego" to show the good doctor that he was no one to trifle with.
quotes.translation: 分析师可能会将奥古斯都视为精神分裂症患者，并轻易给他贴上标签，但这些术语基本上毫无意义。如果分析师在一段时间内能让奥古斯都相信，他目前的状况源于过去某个特定的压抑事件，并且这位分析师是个直觉敏锐且善解人意的人，那么奥古斯都可能会充分改变自己的信念，从而达成某种"治愈"。届时他会方便地记起这样的事件，并在重新体验时表现出预期的情绪。不幸的是，在他目前的状态下，由于没有奥古斯都二世的协助而显得无能为力，他可能干脆召唤他的"第二自我"来向那位好医生证明，他可不是好惹的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Fears, sometimes even seemingly irrational ones, can serve to rouse the body if you have been too lethargic, or have been in a rut psychologically or physically. If you trusted your nature you would be able to trust such feelings, and following their own rhythms and routes they would change into others. Ideally even illnesses are a part of the body's health, representing needed adjustments, and also following the needs of the subjective person at any given time. (Long pause.) They are a part of the interplay between the body and mind, or spirit.
quotes.translation: 恐惧——有时甚至是看似非理性的恐惧——可以在你过于懒散或身心陷入僵局时唤醒身体。如果你信任自己的本性，就能信任这些感受，它们会遵循自身的节奏和路径，转化为其他感受。理想情况下，即使疾病也是身体健康的一部分，代表着必要的调整，并且始终遵循着主观个体在特定时刻的需求。（长停顿。）它们是身体与心智或灵性之间相互作用的一部分。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 821, FEBRUARY 20, 1978

source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: (11:25.) This is a matter of psychological feeling. Some individuals are more at ease, more assured, and more capable working with others in this case. You could consider an analogy in which John Doe follows his kindergarten class all the way through high school. In a reincarnational situation, he would always choose to return with associates. Others, however, would rather skip from school to school, appearing alone, relatively speaking, with greater freedom, more challenge, but without the comforting framework of security chosen by the others.
quotes.translation: （11:25。）这是个心理感受的问题。在这种情况下，有些人更自在、更自信，也更有能力与他人合作。你可以设想一个类比：张三从幼儿园到高中一直跟着同一个班级。在转世情境中，他总会选择与同伴一起返回。然而，其他人则宁愿在不同学校间跳来跳去，相对而言独自出现，拥有更多自由和挑战，但缺少其他人所选择的那种令人安心的安全框架。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Objectively -- whatever the appearances -- storms, earthquakes, floods, et cetera, are quite necessary to the well-being of the earth. Both man's and nature's purposes are served, then, though generally speaking man's myths make him blind to those interactions. People's thoughts and emotions always give clear clues whenever illness is involved, yet most people ignore such information. They censor their own thoughts. Many therefore "fall prey" to epidemics of one kind or another because they want to, though they might deny this quite vigorously.
quotes.translation: 客观而言——无论表象如何——风暴、地震、洪水等对地球的福祉都是相当必要的。因此，人类和自然的目的都得到了满足，尽管一般来说，人类的神话使他们对这些相互作用视而不见。每当涉及疾病时，人们的思想和情绪总会给出清晰的线索，但大多数人忽视这些信息。他们审查自己的思想。因此，许多人"沦为"某种流行病的牺牲品，是因为他们自己想要如此，尽管他们可能会极力否认这一点。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: If you desperately try to remain young, it is usually to hide your own beliefs about age, and to negate all of those emotions connected with it. (Pause.) Whenever you refuse to accept the reality of your creaturehood, you also reject aspects of your spirit. The body exists in the world of space and time. The experiences you may encounter in your sixties are as necessary as those in your twenties. Your changing image is supposed to tell you something. When you pretend alterations do not occur you block both biological and spiritual messages.
quotes.translation: 如果你拼命试图保持年轻，通常是为了隐藏自己对年龄的信念，并否定所有与之相关的情绪。（停顿。）每当你拒绝接受自己作为生物的现实，你也在拒绝自己灵性的某些方面。身体存在于时空世界中。你在六十岁时可能遇到的经历，与二十岁时同样必要。你不断变化的形象本应告诉你一些事情。当你假装变化没有发生时，你既阻断了生物信息，也阻断了灵性信息。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Left alone, some of these people would die with a feeling of satisfaction. Kept alive through medical techniques, the physical mechanism continues its struggles to revitalize the body and bring about this second puberty -- that naturally would only come about under different conditions, with the mind far more alert and the will unimpaired. Now, to some extent (underlined) there is a connection between this innate, rarely observed second puberty and the development of cancer, in which growth is specifically apparent in an exaggerated manner.
quotes.translation: 如果任其自然，这些人中的一部分会带着满足感死去。通过医疗技术维持生命后，身体机制继续挣扎着重振活力，带来这第二次青春期——这自然只会在不同条件下发生，那时心智更为警觉，意志也未受损。现在，在某种程度上（强调）这种天生的、罕见的第二次青春期与癌症的发展之间存在联系，在癌症中，生长以夸张的方式特别明显。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche

quotes.text: "Yesterday while checking the page proofs for Mass Events I got the feeling that that book really bothered me, served as a focal point. My eye troubles started the same spring that Seth started dictating it [in 1977]; I was doing [my world-view book on William] James; and those people were picketing Prentice-Hall's offices in New York City because of the Seth material. The fact had escaped me earlier that Mass Events represented Seth's and my direct attack on official dictums--or so it seemed to me. Before, we sort of did it by inference.
quotes.translation: 昨天在核对《群体事件》的校样时，我有种感觉——那本书确实困扰着我，成了一个焦点。我的眼疾始于1977年春天，正是赛斯开始口述那本书的时候；当时我正在写关于威廉·詹姆斯世界观的书；而那些人因为赛斯资料在纽约市普伦蒂斯-霍尔出版社门口抗议。我之前没意识到，《群体事件》代表了赛斯和我对官方教条的直接攻击——至少在我看来是这样。在此之前，我们只是间接地这么做。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

quotes.text: (11:09.) The habit of not facing problems, which indeed are challenges, can be addictive. A feeling of powerlessness in one field can be transferred to others. When this happens through natural hypnosis, then even the psychic territory of power can be assailed. Here the individual becomes thoroughly aroused, threatened, and realizes for the first time perhaps the nature of belief and his or her predicament. Here you have life and death struggles in creative terms. Some miraculous cures or change-abouts in midlife occur as a result.
quotes.translation: （晚上11点09分。）不面对问题——实际上这些问题是挑战——的习惯会让人上瘾。在一个领域感到无力会转移到其他领域。当这种情况通过自然催眠发生时，甚至连力量的灵性领域也会受到攻击。这时个体被彻底唤醒、感到威胁，或许第一次认识到信念的本质以及自己或自己的困境。这里以创造性的方式呈现生与死的挣扎。一些奇迹般的疗愈或中年转变由此发生。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The relationships for the next life have to be settled upon, and this involves telepathic communication with all those who will be involved. This is a time, then, of many projections. There are those who are simply loners, who reincarnate without any great feeling for earth's historical periods. There are others who like to return when their contemporaries from some particular past historical time return again, and therefore there are group patterns that involve reincarnational cycles in which many, but not all, are involved.
quotes.translation: 下一世的关系必须确定下来，这涉及与所有相关者进行心灵感应沟通。因此，这是一个充满投射的时期。有些人只是独行者，转世时对地球的历史时期没有太多感觉。另一些人喜欢在某个特定历史时期的同代人再次转世时回来，因此存在涉及转世周期的群体模式，许多人——但不是所有人——都参与其中。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul

quotes.text: Catastrophies, such as earthquakes or floods, are not perpetuated by certain elements of nature against other portions of itself. Your feelings have as much natural validity as the tides, and they have their own kind of attraction -- mind does move matter. A ring manipulated at a seance under controlled conditions is but the most simple kind of demonstration of the great ability of mind to interact with matter. You each participate in the creation of each thunderstorm, each new spring, each flood, earthquake, and summer rain.
quotes.translation: 灾难，如地震或洪水，并非自然界的某些元素对其他部分施加的永恒惩罚。你的感受与潮汐一样具有自然的有效性，它们有自己独特的吸引力——心智确实能移动物质。在受控条件下，降神会上被操控的戒指，只是心智与物质互动强大能力的最简单展示。你们每个人都参与创造了每一场雷暴、每一个春天、每一次洪水、地震和夏雨。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: "I feel inferior because my mother hated me," or, "I feel unworthy because I was scrawny and small as a child." You may find as you work with your beliefs that a feeling of inferiority seems to stem from such episodes. It is up to you as an adult to get on top of your beliefs, to realize that a mother who hates her child is already in difficulties, and that such a hate says far more about the mother than it does about her offspring. It is up to you to understand that you are now a grown person, and not a child to be bullied.
quotes.translation: “我觉得自卑，因为我母亲恨我，”或者，“我觉得自己毫无价值，因为我小时候瘦小孱弱。”当你处理自己的信念时，你可能会发现自卑感似乎源于这些经历。作为成年人，你有责任掌控自己的信念，意识到一个恨自己孩子的母亲已经陷入困境，这种恨更多地反映了母亲自身，而非她的孩子。你有责任理解自己现在是一个成年人，而不是一个可以被欺负的孩子。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Now in order to direct this power consciously, you must again get used to the feeling of your own subjective experience.... so that you can tell subjectively when this energy is pouring through you and outward. You can use your imagination and imagine perhaps that you hold an arrow and want to direct it to a proper location. But with practice, there is a subjective knowing that you will recognize and understand. But you use the ability whether you realize you use it or not. You are a healer, whether you realize this or not.
quotes.translation: 现在，为了有意识地引导这股力量，你必须再次习惯自己主观体验的感受……这样你就能在主观上辨别这股能量何时流经你并向外散发。你可以运用想象力，或许想象自己握着一支箭，想把它射向合适的位置。但通过练习，会有一种主观的知晓，你会认出并理解它。无论你是否意识到，你都在运用这种能力。无论你是否意识到，你都是一个疗愈者。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)

quotes.text: Mark, you need discipline, but it is discipline that you will acquire in living. And here let me say that Ruburt's strong feelings are correct. Ruburt knows what he knows. You should move into your gallery as soon as possible. You will be asked to give shows of your paintings by two people whom you should refuse; you will be asked to give shows by other people, whose invitations you should accept. It will be up to you to use your inner knowledge, your intuitions, in determining which persons to refuse and which to accept.
quotes.translation: 马克，你需要纪律，但这是你在生活中会获得的纪律。在此让我说，鲁柏的强烈感受是正确的。鲁柏知道他所知道的事。你应该尽快搬进你的画廊。会有两个人请你举办画展，你应该拒绝；也会有其他人请你举办画展，你应该接受他们的邀请。你必须运用你的内在知识和直觉，来决定拒绝哪些人、接受哪些人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: You always know what you are doing, even when you do not realize it. Your eye knows it sees, though it cannot see itself except through the use of reflection. In the same way the world as you see it is a reflection of what you are, a reflection not in glass but in three-dimensional reality. You project your thoughts, feelings, and expectations outward, then you perceive them as the outside reality. When it seems to you that others are observing you, you are observing yourself from the standpoint of your own projections.
quotes.translation: 你总是知道自己在做什么，即使你并未意识到。你的眼睛知道它在看，尽管它无法看见自己，除非通过反射。同样地，你看到的世界是你自身的反映——不是玻璃中的反映，而是三维实相中的反映。你将你的思想、感受和期待向外投射，然后将它们视为外在实相。当别人似乎正在观察你时，你其实是从自身投射的角度在观察自己。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Imagine further that you actually experienced the feeling of such a furry coat, and all the other feline equipment from the inside. Purely as a spectator this would loosely represent an analogy to my traveling to other planes. It follows that I could not travel to higher planes than my own, where more acute senses than mine would instantly perceive me. This sort of thing does not as a rule go on in your plane. Even with your limited senses you would perceive my presence, though my plane is further developed than yours.
quotes.translation: 再进一步想象，你实际上从内在体验了那样一件毛茸茸的皮毛外套，以及所有其他猫科动物的装备。纯粹作为旁观者，这大致可以类比于我前往其他层面的旅行。由此可知，我无法前往比我自身更高的层面，因为那里比我更敏锐的感官会立刻察觉到我。这种事情通常不会发生在你们的层面上。即使以你们有限的感官，你们也会察觉到我的存在，尽管我的层面比你们的更为进化。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now, creativity has always been the species' closest connection with its own source, with the nature of its own being. Through creativity the species senses All That Is. Creativity goes by a different set of rules, however. It defies categories, and it insists upon the evidence of feeling. It is a source of revelation and inspiration -- yet initially revelation and inspiration do not deal with power, but with knowing. So what often happens in your society when men and women have creative bents, and good minds to boot?
quotes.translation: 创造力一直是这个物种与其自身源头、与其存在本质最紧密的连接。通过创造力，这个物种感知到"一切万有"。然而，创造力遵循一套不同的规则。它蔑视分类，坚持感受的证据。它是启示和灵感的来源——但最初，启示和灵感并不关乎力量，而是关乎知晓。那么在你们的社会中，当男女拥有创造性的倾向和良好的心智时，常常会发生什么？

quotes.text: Individuals feeling this way will be very uncomfortable when they mingle with others of a different race, creed or color, and despite themselves may be revengefully conservative in dealing, for example, with problems of a community nature. They will consider poverty as a sign of God's displeasure and so be inclined to leave the entire matter in his "hands." They may speak with seeming compassion about the plight of others, and yet all the while consider that difficulty the simple result of inferiority, of inequality.
quotes.translation: 有这种感觉的人，在与不同种族、信仰或肤色的人交往时会非常不自在，并且不由自主地，例如在处理社区性质的问题时，可能会表现出报复性的保守。他们会将贫穷视为神不悦的迹象，因而倾向于将整个问题交在神的"手中"。他们可能会带着看似同情的话语谈论他人的困境，但始终认为这种困难只是低劣、不平等的简单结果。

quotes.text: When she sensed any strong feelings that Joseph also wanted such a home, then -- in your terms now -- she began, from her different framework after death, to bring that opportunity into his experience. This is not manipulation. It does show, however, that one portion of Joseph's mother, the portion connected to her son, still relates to him in a certain fashion. It also shows that his desires for a house in Sayre (deeper and stronger) helped bring about certain events: He could have such a house if he wanted one.
quotes.translation: 当她察觉到约瑟夫也渴望拥有这样一个家时——用你们的话说——她便从死后不同的架构开始，将那个机会带入他的体验中。这不是操纵。但这确实表明，约瑟夫母亲的一部分——与儿子相连的那部分——仍然以某种方式与他联系。这也表明，他对塞耶房子的渴望（更深、更强烈）促成了某些事件的发生：如果他想要，他就能拥有这样一栋房子。

quotes.text: （While writing out this statement, I am reminded that I experienced the same sensation, one time in a milder form, about a year ago. It happened on my job at Artistic Card Co., at noon. I was alone in the art room, eating lunch at my desk, when the feeling swept over me from head to foot. There was no warning no pain, but the surprise of it doubled me over my desk. I was of course frightened momentarily, thinking of some kind of attack, but it quickly passed and did not return. I happened to be alone at the time.
quotes.translation: 写下这段话时，我忆起约一年前也曾体验过类似感受，只是程度较轻。当时我在艺术卡片公司上班，正午时分独自待在美术室，伏案用午餐时，那股感觉从头到脚席卷而来。没有预兆也无痛楚，但惊愕之下我整个人弓身趴在桌上。自然瞬间恐慌，以为是某种病症发作，所幸转瞬即逝，再未复发。当时恰巧只有我一人。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: Now. Quite practically your personal and practical interest in immortality will give the impetus, the emotional impetus that will indeed allow me to deliver what you want. This is perhaps one of the most important statements of this evenings session. Emotions are more important than you suppose. There is no fraud in me. There is no fraud in Ruburt or Joseph. They are not stupid and they are not gullible. You are not stupid nor gullible. They are not caught up in pseudo religious nonsense. This will serve us well.
quotes.translation: 现在。非常实际地说，你对不朽的个人与实用兴趣，将提供情感上的驱动力——这股情感动力确实能让我传递你所需的信息。这或许是今晚课程最重要的陈述之一。情感的重要性远超你的想象。我毫无欺诈，鲁柏与约瑟亦无欺瞒。他们既不愚钝也不轻信，你同样如此。他们并未陷入伪宗教的胡言乱语，这将成为我们良好的基础。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: They are brought into existence in a subjective manner, but they then are independent action, and as such may continue to exist in duration within the physical field, according to their original electric potential. In turn these thoughts or emotions, as electric actions, can affect other actions; and influence patterns can be set up, and are set up. The subjective habits of individuals are largely responsible for their own attractions to various types of such electric actions, and here indeed like attracts like.
quotes.translation: 它们以主观方式被创造出来，但随后成为独立行动，依据其原始电位，可在物理场域中持续存在。这些思想或情感作为电性行动，能影响其他行动；影响模式由此建立，且确实在建立中。个体的主观习性，很大程度上决定了他们会被何种电性行动吸引——在此，同类确实相吸。

quotes.text: It was a tool of your art. It was also a part of your talent. It added to the dimension of your work, but to some degree it was also adopted as a protective mechanism, to give you some feeling of separation. Your mother's Emotionalism frightened you so that you distrusted strong feeling, even while you must use it, and be vividly aware of it in order to paint.
quotes.translation: 它曾是你艺术的工具，亦是你天赋的一部分。它为你的作品增添了维度，但某种程度上也被用作保护机制，让你获得分离感。你母亲的情绪主义令你恐惧，使你即便必须运用强烈情感作画，也对其心存戒备。

quotes.text: Ruburt sensed one in particular, and did indeed react in a manner that you found annoying. Ruburt's feelings and lack of action as far as the publishing house was concerned left much to be desired, and in time his lack of action would have caused an unpleasant reaction on his own part. However he correctly, if subconsciously, interpreted your attitude toward the publishing house as being basically dangerous to you. And so it was.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏特别感知到某种情绪，确实以令你恼火的方式做出了反应。就出版社事宜而言，鲁柏的情感与不作为令人遗憾，长此以往，他的不作为本会引发自身的不快反应。然而他正确（即便出于潜意识）解读了你对出版社的态度——那本质上对你构成危险。事实确是如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: My definition of magic is this: Magic is nature unimpeded, or magic is life unimpeded. It is true that your thoughts and emotions and beliefs form the reality that you experience -- but it is also true that this creative construction is, in a manner of speaking, magically formed. That is, the construction of your body and the construction of a world (pause) are produced with the greatest combination of order and spontaneity -- an order and spontaneity that seems hidden rather than apparent (all intently).
quotes.translation: 我对魔法的定义是：魔法即不受阻碍的自然，或不受阻碍的生命。你的思想、情感与信念确实构成你所体验的现实——但同样真实的是，这种创造性建构，从某种意义上说，是以魔法方式形成的。也就是说，你身体的建构与世界的建构（停顿），是秩序与自发性最精妙的结合——这种秩序与自发性看似隐而不显（全神贯注地）。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: (Slowly:) You have not learned to use your consciousness properly or fully, so that it seems that imagination, emotions and reasoning are separate faculties, or sometimes set against each other. The mature conscious mind, once more, accepts data from the exterior world and from the interior one. It is only when you believe that consciousness must be attuned only to exterior conditions that you force it to cut itself off from inner knowledge, intuitional "voices," and the depths from which it springs.
quotes.translation: （缓慢地：）你尚未学会正确或充分地运用自己的意识，以至于想象、情感和理智看似是彼此分离的能力，有时甚至相互对立。成熟的意识心智再次强调，它既接受外在世界的资料，也接受内在世界的资料。唯有当你相信意识必须只调谐于外在条件时，你才会迫使它切断与内在知识、直觉的“声音”以及它自身所源起的深层领域的联系。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Do not place the words of gurus, ministers, priests, scientists, psychologists, friends -- or my words -- higher than the feelings of your own being. You can learn much from others, but the deepest knowledge must come from within yourself. Your own consciousness is embarked upon a reality that basically can be experienced by no other, that is unique and untranslatable, with its own meaning, following its own paths of becoming.
quotes.translation: 不要将大师、牧师、神父、科学家、心理学家、朋友——或我的话——置于你自身存在的感受之上。你可以从他人那里学到很多，但最深层的知识必须来自你内在。你的意识所投身其中的实相，本质上无法被任何其他个体所体验，它是独一无二且不可翻译的，拥有其自身意义，遵循其自身成为的路径。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Instead, such an event is a direct expression of the psyche's knowledge. It senses its quite legitimate identification with nature, exercises its mobility, and feels its own emotional power leap. Your emotions in such a case would be momentarily magnified -- raised, say, to a higher power. There are multitudinous such examples that could be given, as in each day your psyche presents evidence of its own greater being -- evidence that you are taught to overlook, or to dismiss because it is not factual.
quotes.translation: 相反，这样的事件是心灵知识的直接表达。它感知到自身与自然之间完全正当的认同，施展其流动性，并感受到自身情感力量的跃升。在这种情况下，你的情绪会被瞬间放大——比如说，被提升到更高的次幂。可以举出无数这样的例子，正如每一天你的心灵都在呈现其更伟大存在的证据——这些证据你被教导要忽视或摒弃，因为它们并非事实。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The feeling of unreality is not felt when the dream experience is being participated in by the dreamer. At that time the experience is felt to be real, and some dreams indeed are more vivid than waking experience. It is only when the personality passes out of the dream experience, or the dream universe, that the dream experience in retrospect may appear unreal. For now, again, the focus of attention and energy is in the physical universe. Reality then is a result of the focus of energy and attention.
quotes.translation: 当做梦者亲身参与梦境体验时，并不会感到不真实。在那一刻，体验被感觉为真实的，有些梦境甚至比清醒体验更为生动。只有当人格离开梦境体验或梦境宇宙时，回顾中的梦境体验才可能显得不真实。因为此时，注意力和能量的焦点再次落在物质宇宙中。于是，实相是能量与注意力焦点的结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: All of this needs to be said, you see. You are both learning at a rather amazing rate. In the past you turned some repressed violence inward against yourself. Ruburts selling jobs were very practical for a time, for they allowed him to release aggressive feelings. You become angry when you think, rightly, that Ruburt is too docile in his dealings, but this is because you are angry at your own lack of power as a child to retaliate against the atmosphere of violence that you sensed in the child's home.
quotes.translation: 所有这些都需要说明，你看。你们两人都在以相当惊人的速度学习。过去，你们将一些被压抑的暴力转向内在，针对自己。鲁柏的销售工作在一段时间内非常实用，因为这让他得以释放攻击性感受。当你正确地认为鲁柏在待人接物中过于温顺时，你会感到愤怒，但这是因为你对自己童年时无力反击在孩童家中感知到的暴力氛围而感到愤怒。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 6 of The Seth Material (Sessions 240-280)
quotes.source_file: ES-6-The Early Sessions-Book 6 of The Seth Material(240-280).json

quotes.text: Because of these feelings he reacts as if he is being attacked. The feelings however are the cause of the attack, for these feelings attract those elements that would otherwise be harmless. This is rather important.
quotes.translation: 由于这些感受，他的反应仿佛自己正在遭受攻击。然而，这些感受才是攻击的原因，因为这些感受吸引了那些原本无害的元素。这一点相当重要。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The power must fill the image, and the image then fill the painting so that the very force of the figure seems hardly contained within it. It is the emotions beneath (smile) that give meaning to the image, and yet these emotions are held and contained and given direction and force by and through the image. (Leaning forward, emphatic delivery, eyes open.)
quotes.translation: 力量必须充满意象，而意象必须充盈画作，使人物本身的力量几乎无法被画框所容纳。正是微笑之下潜藏的情感赋予了意象意义，然而这些情感又被意象所承载、约束，并通过意象获得方向与力量。（身体前倾，语气强调，双目圆睁。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: To an extent, some people in the sciences manage to blend the so-called female and male characteristics. When they do so, seeming oppositions and contradictions disappear. To whatever degree, more than their contemporaries, they do not allow sexual roles to blind them psychologically. Therefore they are more apt to combine reason and emotion, intuitions and intellect, and in so doing invent theories that reconcile previous contradictions. They unify, expand, and create, rather than diversify.
quotes.translation: 某种程度上，科学界的某些人成功融合了所谓的女性特质与男性特质。当他们做到这一点时，看似对立的矛盾便消失了。无论程度如何，他们比同时代人更少让性别角色在心理上蒙蔽自己。因此他们更善于结合理性与情感、直觉与智性，从而创造出调和先前矛盾的理论。他们统一、扩展并创造，而非分化。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your enthusiasm is excellent. However, your enthusiasm can cause waves that can go up and down. You want a quiet surface. It would help you if before bed you imagine your mind like an ocean upon which you could walk. All feelings are on the waters, you are above. Then imagine yourself walking on the water, listening with your inner ear and using inner eyes. You should hear voices and see a figure on the horizon... let the figure come closer... Do not run. Relax and let the figure come to you.
quotes.translation: 你的热情极佳。然而你的热情可能引发起伏不定的浪潮。你需要平静的水面。睡前若想象你的心智如一片可踏浪而行的海洋，将对你有益。所有情感皆浮于水面，而你立于其上。接着想象自己行走于水面，以内耳聆听，以内眼观照。你应能听见声音，望见地平线上的人影……让那人影靠近……切勿奔跑。放松，任其走向你。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Let the definition go for a moment however while I make this one point. I have said that our imaginary wires that seem to permeate our model universe are alive, and now if you will bear with me I will say that they are mental enzymes or solidified feelings, always of course in motion and yet permanent enough to form a more or less consistent framework. （Pause.） You could almost say that mental enzymes become the tentacles which form material, though I do not find that a very pleasant phrase.
quotes.translation: 暂且放下定义，容我阐明这一点。我曾说过，那些看似渗透我们模型宇宙的虚构导线是活生生的，此刻若蒙您耐心，我将称它们为精神酶或固化的情感——始终处于运动之中，却又足够恒久，足以构成大致连贯的框架。（停顿。）你几乎可以说，精神酶化为形成物质的触须，尽管我并不觉得这个说法很悦耳。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Even those of you who intellectually agree that you form your own reality find it difficult to accept emotionally in certain areas. You are, of course, literally hypnotized into believing that your feelings arise in response to events. Your feelings, however, cause the events you perceive. Secondarily, you do of course then react to those events.
quotes.translation: 即便那些在理智上认同"自己创造实相"的人，也发现难以在情感上接受某些领域。当然，你们确实被催眠般相信情感是对事件的反应。然而，正是你们的情感引发了所感知的事件。其次，你们当然会随后对这些事件做出反应。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (Five weeks ago [in the notes leading off Session 821], I wrote that Jane could resume work on The Further Education of Oversoul Seven -- or Seven Two -- at any time. She finished her first novel about Seven in July 1972, and within a month, long before it was published, she wrote the first five chapters for Seven Two. Then we became involved in so many other projects that she laid it aside until August 1976, when she wrote two more chapters. The book has hovered in the back of her consciousness ever since, waiting until she focused her attention upon it once again. "Seven's got all the time in the world," she laughed more than once. But now that her editor, Tam Mossman, has scheduled a visit to us at the hill house in a couple of weeks [on April 10, to be exact], Jane feels that she wants to study what she's done on Seven Two, go over it with Tam, and perhaps take up work on it again.)
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: I also want to stress here that all aspects of life experience not only sensations but emotional feelings. Therefore, there is a kind of innate gallantry that operates among all segments of life -- a gallantry that deserves your respect and consideration. You should have respect, then, for the cells of your body, the thoughts of your mind (pause), and try to understand that even the smallest of creatures shares with you the emotional experience of life's triumphs and vulnerabilities.
quotes.translation: 我在此也要强调：生命经验的每个面向不仅感受知觉，也体验情感。因此，所有生命片段之间都运作着一种与生俱来的勇毅——一种值得你尊重与体恤的勇毅。你应当尊重身体的细胞、心智的念头（停顿），并试着理解：即便最微小的造物，也与你共享生命胜利与脆弱的情绪体验。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health
quotes.source_file: 10-10-10-10The Way Toward Health - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: This has something to do with Ruburt's difficulty in breaking the trance state. (Pause.) From someone else here... Your figures, to get the feeling of depth and breadth, this is not to get the feeling simply of the flesh and bone within the flesh, but to suggest the personal energy and vitality that is in a riot, to be loose within the flesh.
quotes.translation: 这与鲁柏难以脱离出神状态有关。（停顿。）在座的其他人……你的画作，为了捕捉深度与广度的感觉，并非单纯描绘血肉中的骨骼，而是要暗示那奔放不羁的个人能量与生命力，在血肉中自由舒展。

quotes.text: （See the tracing of the envelope object on page 82. I felt Seth was on the track with his data, yet because ! had so little background concerning it myself the data seemed opaque to me. I found myself feeling the same way about the answers to my questions, so I did not continue to ask them. I also thought the data was mixed up with the planned visit of my parents to the apartment for Easter Sunday, April 10, and a very short reminder my mother had written in connection with this.
quotes.translation: （参见第82页的信封物件描摹图。我感觉赛斯正在追踪他的资料，但由于我对此背景知之甚少，这些资料对我而言显得晦涩难懂。我发现自己对问题的答案也有同感，因此便不再继续追问。我还认为这些资料与我父母计划于4月10日复活节周日来访公寓一事，以及母亲为此写下的简短提醒有所混淆。）

quotes.text: I do not make it a practice to observe. We are connected, however, in our psychological gestalt, and so I am aware of any intense feelings on your part, or strong reactions of any kind. This does not mean that I am necessarily aware of all events within your lives, or that I always break down the feelings I receive from you into specifics.
quotes.translation: 我并非刻意观察你们。然而，在我们的心理完形中彼此相连，因此我能感知到你们任何强烈的情感或任何形式的剧烈反应。但这并不意味着我必然知晓你们生活中的所有事件，或总能将接收到的感受分解为具体细节。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: （I spent eight or nine days in bed, the victim of what is generally called a virus. At no time however did I blame a virus, feeling that the real cause was psychic, thus permitting the virus to come to the fore. I was somewhat embarrassed at having "goofed" psychically. As time passed, and while I pored over several daily New York City newspapers plus Elmira's daily paper, I came to realize to a small extent that poor expectations on my part had much to do with my falling ill.
quotes.translation: （我在床上躺了八九天，成了所谓病毒的受害者。但我从未归咎于病毒，始终认为真正的原因是心理层面的，从而让病毒得以显现。我对自己的"心理失误"感到些许尴尬。随着时间推移，当我仔细翻阅纽约市几份日报和埃尔迈拉日报时，才逐渐意识到，我自身的负面预期与这场疾病密切相关。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)

quotes.text: At this point many variations in behavior emerge, each the result of individual background, knowledge, and habit. The surroundings in which the dead find themselves will often vary. Vivid hallucinations may form experience quite as real as any in mortal life. Now, I have told you that thoughts and emotions form physical reality, and they form after-death experience. This does not mean that the experiences are not valid, any more than it means that physical life is not valid.
quotes.translation: 此时会出现许多行为变异，每种都是个人背景、知识与习惯的结果。死者所处的环境往往各不相同。生动的幻觉可能构成与尘世生活同样真实的体验。我曾告诉你们，思想与情感构建了物质实相，也构建了死后体验。这并不意味着这些体验无效，正如不能说物质生活无效一样。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The organization of your feelings, beliefs, and intents directs the focus about which your physical reality is built. This follows with impeccable spontaneity and order. If you believe in the sinfulness of the world, for instance, then you will search out from normal sense data those facts that confirm your belief. But beyond that, at other levels you also organize your mental world in such a way that you attract to yourself events that -- again -- will confirm your beliefs.
quotes.translation: 你的感受、信念与意图的组织方式，决定了构建物质实相的焦点。这以无可挑剔的自发性与秩序运作。例如，若你相信世界充满罪恶，便会从正常感官数据中筛选出证实此信念的事实。但不仅如此，在其他层面上，你还会以某种方式组织你的心灵世界，从而吸引那些——再次——能印证你信念的事件。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now: If, for example, you hate a parent, then it becomes quite easy to hate any parents, for in their faces you see and project the original offender. In subsequent lives you may also be drawn into a family and find yourself with the same emotions, for the emotions are the problem, and not those elements that seem to bring them about.
quotes.translation: 现在：举例来说，如果你憎恨一位父母，那么憎恨任何父母就变得很容易，因为你在他们的脸上看到并投射了最初冒犯你的人。在后续的转世中，你可能会被吸引到某个家庭，发现自己带着同样的情绪，因为情绪才是问题所在，而非那些看似引发情绪的元素。

quotes.text: Emotions may even cause a color reaction. Any reality, regardless of which system it originates within, will appear to some degree within all systems. Even within your own system, though perhaps on a subconscious level, all emotions have a reality in color. They have, as you know, a chemical reaction which is sniffed by other animals.
quotes.translation: 情绪甚至可能引起颜色反应。任何实相，无论它起源于哪个系统，都会在一定程度上出现在所有系统中。即使在你们自己的系统内，尽管可能是在潜意识层面，所有情绪都有颜色的实相。如你所知，它们会产生一种化学反应，能被其他动物嗅到。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: In such a case, begin imaginatively, following through with the other decision or decisions that you might have made. At one point a shadowy effect -- grayness, or other characteristics just mentioned -- will occur. One or several of these may be involved, but again your subjective feeling is the most important clue. Imagination may bring you a clear picture, for example, that may then become fuzzy, and in that case the blurred quality would be your hint of probable action.
quotes.translation: 在这种情况下，开始运用想象力，跟随你可能做出的其他决定或选择。在某个时刻，会出现一种阴影效果——灰色或其他刚才提到的特征。可能涉及其中一种或几种，但再次强调，你的主观感受是最重要的线索。想象力可能会给你带来清晰的画面，例如，然后变得模糊，在这种情况下，模糊的特质就是你可能行动的提示。

quotes.text: Ruburt has changed since then, and so have I. And yet we are bound together, and no invasion occurs because in one way of speaking our psychic territory is the same. I will go into the construction of entities later. My own emotional feeling, you see, goes outward, which is away from Ruburt often, since basically we are tempted to think of ourselves as one, though actually our roots are merely the same.
quotes.translation: 鲁柏自那时起已经改变，我也一样。然而我们彼此相连，没有发生入侵，因为从某种角度来说，我们的心灵领域是相同的。我稍后会探讨人格实体的构建。你看，我的情感感受向外延伸，常常远离鲁柏，因为基本上我们倾向于认为我们是一体的，尽管实际上我们的根源只是相同。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)

quotes.text: You are largely over that kind of reaction -- yet it is still apt to return now and then when you fear that you have not done as well as you should have, or when you have momentarily caught yourself behaving in old ways. A feeling of self-approval is absolutely necessary for any true sense of well-being; it is not (underlined) virtuous in any way to put yourself down, or to punish yourself, because you do not feel you have lived up to your best behavior at any given time.
quotes.translation: 你很大程度上已经克服了那种反应——然而当你担心自己没有做得像应该的那样好时，或者当你一时发现自己以旧方式行事时，它仍然容易时不时地回来。自我认同感对于任何真正的幸福感是绝对必要的；在任何时候，因为觉得自己没有达到最佳行为标准而贬低自己或惩罚自己，绝不是（强调）美德。
quotes.book: The Way Toward Health

source_raw.title: Your question about transmigration (in Session 903 in Dreams) was an excellent one, and did indeed make the matter clear. It is a temptation at times to use more specific scientific terms, but these would be as confusing as the various definitions and classifications (with humor) that you read in the dictionary, so overall we try to hit a "happy medium." (With more humor.)

quotes.text: The emotions come closer than anything else to the vividness of inner data. There are of course more differences than similarities. However because of the intense quality of emotional experience this is still a good comparison. With the emotions however, there is in many cases a stimulus to action in the outside camouflage pattern.
quotes.translation: 情绪比其他任何东西都更接近内在数据的生动性。当然，差异多于相似之处。然而，由于情感体验的强烈特质，这仍然是一个很好的比较。但在情绪方面，在许多情况下，会有一种刺激，促使在外在伪装模式中采取行动。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Now in your dream you got the feeling of that kind of work, or action. It is the given power of the world, the given power of nature. It is the directed force of value fulfillment.2 In other terms it is of course the energy of All That Is. The trouble is that the rational view of life has separated man from a sense of his own power source. When he has a problem, the rational approach to its solution seems the only answer, and often, of course, it is no answer at all.
quotes.translation: 现在，在你的梦境中，你感受到了那种工作或行动的感觉。它是世界赋予的力量，是自然赋予的力量。它是价值完成的方向性能量。换句话说，它当然是“一切万有”的能量。问题在于，理性的生活观将人与自身的力量源泉分离开来。当一个人遇到问题时，理性的解决方法似乎是唯一的答案，而通常，这根本就不是答案。
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: In one sense meeting with you costs me little energy, it is true. On the other hand the effort to communicate explanations does involve very real effort on my part. And so you are not the only ones who grow weary in this respect. As I have said, feeling is action, and in my communications to you feeling plays a strong part.
quotes.translation: 从某种意义上说，与你会面消耗我的能量很少，这是真的。另一方面，为传达解释所做的努力确实涉及我这边非常真实的付出。所以，在这方面感到疲惫的不只是你们。正如我所说，感觉即是行动，在我与你们的沟通中，感觉扮演着重要的角色。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)

quotes.text: (4:36.) This leads me of course to at least mention here the cruel methods used in the slaughtering of animals and fowls for human consumption. The creatures are treated as if they possessed no feeling or consciousness of their own -- and such attitudes show a most unfortunate misreading of natural events. As a direct result, at least as many diseases develop through such procedures as would exist in a highly primitive society with unsanitary conditions. Period.
quotes.translation: （4:36。）这当然让我至少在此提及人类为食用而屠宰动物和家禽时所使用的残忍方法。这些生物被当作没有自身感觉或意识的东西来对待——这种态度显示出对自然事件最不幸的误读。直接结果是，通过这样的程序，至少会发展出与高度原始且卫生条件恶劣的社会中同样多的疾病。句号。

source_raw.title: (I talked about the first session for Jane's symptoms -- the private portion of Session 208, for November 15, 1965 -- yet she said that for her the whole thing really started the day before we went to a party in June 1966 regarding How to Develop Your ESP Power. Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: Animals also dramatize. They possess emotions. They feel a part of the drama of the seasons. They are fully alive, in those terms. Nature in all of its varieties is so richly encountered by the animals that it becomes their equivalent of your structures of culture and civilization. They respond to its rich nuances in ways impossible to describe, so that their "civilizations" are built up through the interweavings of sense data that you cannot possibly perceive.
quotes.translation: 动物也会戏剧化。它们拥有情感。它们感受到季节戏剧的一部分。就那些方面而言，它们是完全活着的。大自然以其所有多样性如此丰富地被动物所体验，以至于它成为它们文化文明结构的等价物。它们以无法描述的方式回应其丰富的细微差别，以至于它们的“文明”是通过你无法感知的感觉数据的交织而建立起来的。

quotes.text: The emotional climate, though intangible, is intimately known by each individual as it exists within himself, and it is the best indication of his physical condition, for thoughts and emotions as independent electrical actions have great influence directly upon the physical mechanism, acting indeed as electric storms which flash through the entire nervous system; or as great stabilizers as the case may be, and with of course many middle varieties of influence.
quotes.translation: 情感氛围虽然无形，但每个人都能亲密地了解它存在于自身之内，它是身体状况的最佳指标，因为思想和情感作为独立的电性活动，对物理机制有直接而巨大的影响，确实像电风暴一样闪遍整个神经系统；或者视情况而定，像巨大的稳定器，当然还有许多中间类型的影响。

quotes.text: This is not simple to explain, yet I would do poorly by you if I left you with a simple explanation. Emotional feelings are vital. Emotional identities are indestructible. Personalities that you have known in past lives will know you after transition. This does not mean that for their own purposes they have not joined in psychic mergers that you cannot presently understand. As I am joined in a psychic merger with you now, and am still myself, and you yourself.
quotes.translation: 这不容易解释，但如果我只给你一个简单的解释，那我对你就太不负责任了。情感感觉是至关重要的。情感身份是不可摧毁的。你在前世认识的人格在过渡之后会认识你。这并不意味着为了他们自己的目的，他们没有加入你目前无法理解的心灵融合。正如我现在与你进行心灵融合，而我仍然是我自己，你也仍然是你自己。

quotes.text: More and more, the quality of your lives is formed through the subjective realities of your feelings and mental constructions. Again, beliefs that foster despair are biologically destructive. They cause the physical system to shut down. If mass action against appalling social or political conditions is not effective, then other means are taken, and these are often in the guise of epidemics or natural disasters. The blight is wiped out in one way or another.
quotes.translation: 你们生命品质的塑造，越来越取决于感受与心智建构的主观实相。再次强调，助长绝望的信念具有生物破坏性，会导致身体系统关闭。若针对骇人社会或政治状况的集体行动无效，便会采取其他手段——往往以流行病或自然灾害的面貌出现。祸害终将以某种方式被清除。

quotes.text: You must therefore understand and examine your beliefs, realize that they form your experience, and consciously change those that do not give the effects you want. In such an examination you will be aware of many excellent beliefs that work for you. Trace these through. See how they were followed by your imagination and emotions. If possible, look in your own past for points where recognizable new ideas came to you and beneficially changed your experience.
quotes.translation: 因此你们必须理解并检视自己的信念，认识到它们塑造了你们的经验，并有意识地改变那些无法带来预期效果的信念。在此过程中，你们会察觉许多行之有效的卓越信念。追溯这些信念的脉络，观察它们如何被想象力与情感所追随。若有可能，回顾过往中那些带来崭新认知并有益改变经验的关键节点。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Any previous acts that had aroused feelings of natural guilt were to be avoided in the future. Because of the multitudinous courses open to the species, not only did the highly specific nature of many kinds of animalistic instinct no longer apply, but a curious balance had to be maintained. The conscious options that opened as man's mental world enlarged made it impossible to allow sufficient freedom, and yet necessary control, on a biological level alone.
quotes.translation: 任何曾引发自然愧疚感的过往行为，未来都需避免。由于物种面临无数可能性路径，不仅许多动物本能的高度特异性不再适用，更需维持某种精妙平衡。随着人类心智世界的扩展，意识选择空间的开辟，使得仅靠生物层面既无法赋予充分自由，又难以实现必要控制。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You must indeed for practical reasons pretend as if the automobile had no reality except the reality with which you are familiar, but this is not the case. I mentioned feeling sound because this is a capability that lies latent within your own physical system, but this same sort of a juggling of perspective data is what happens, generally speaking, when inhabitants of a different system perceive realities that also have an existence within your own system.
quotes.translation: 出于实际考量，你们确实必须假装汽车除了你们熟悉的实相外别无其他实相——但事实并非如此。我提及"感受声音"是因为这是你们身体系统内潜藏的能力。一般而言，当不同系统的居民感知到你们系统内同样存在的实相时，这种视角数据的转换就会发生。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)

quotes.text: However, through traveling within yourselves, you will discover the unity of your consciousness with other consciousnesses. You will discover the multidimensional love and energy that gives consciousness to all things. This will not lead you to want to rest upon the proverbial blessed bosom. It will instead inspire you to take a better hand in the job of creation; and that feeling of divine presence you will find indeed, and feel indeed, for you will sense it behind the dance of the molecules, and in yourselves and in your neighbors. What so many want is a God who walks down the street and says, "Happy Sunday, I am I, follow me." But God is hidden craftily in his creations, so that he is what they are and they are what he is; and in knowing them, you know him.
quotes.translation: 然而，通过向内探索，你们将发现自身意识与其他意识的统一性。你们将发现赋予万物意识的多维之爱与能量。这不会让你们想躺在传说中受祝福的怀抱里，反而会激励你们更积极地参与创造工程。你们确实会找到并感受到神圣临在——因为你们将在分子舞蹈背后、在自身与邻人之中感知到它。许多人渴望的是一位走在街上说"快乐周日，我是自有永有的，跟随我"的神。但神巧妙地隐藏在其造物中，以致祂即是造物，造物即是祂；认识造物，你们便认识祂。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The inner self is embarked upon an exciting endeavor, in which it learns how to translate its reality into physical terms. The conscious mind is brilliantly attuned to physical reality, then, and often so dazzled by what it perceives that it is tempted to think physical phenomena is a cause, rather than a result. Deeper portions of the self always serve to remind it that this is not the case. When the conscious mind accepts too many false beliefs, particularly if it sees that inner self as a danger, then it closes out these constant reminders. When this situation arises the conscious mind feels itself assailed by a reality that seems greater than itself, over which it has no control. The deep feeling of security in which it should be anchored is lost.
quotes.translation: 内在自体正投身于激动人心的探索，学习如何将其实相转化为物质语言。意识心如此精妙地调频于物质实相，常被所感知之物炫惑，以致误认物质现象为因而非果。自体更深层部分始终提醒它事实并非如此。当意识心接受过多虚假信念，尤其当它将内在自体视为危险时，便会封闭这些持续提醒。当此情形发生，意识心会感到被看似超越自身且无法掌控的实相所侵袭，失去本应锚定的深层安全感。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Your own value system then is built up of your beliefs about reality, and those beliefs form your experience. Suppose you believe that to be "good" you must try to be perfect. You may have been told, or read, that the spirit is perfect, and hence thought that your duty was to reproduce that perfect spirit in flesh as best you could. To this end you attempt to deny all imperfect thoughts and emotions. Your own "negative" thoughts appall you. You may believe also what I have told you -- that your thoughts create your reality -- so you become all the more frightened at mental or actual expressions of an aggressive nature. You may be so concerned about hurting someone else that you hardly dare move. Trying to be perfect all the time can be far more than a nuisance: It can be disastrous because of your misunderstanding.
quotes.translation: 你的价值体系是由你对实相的看法所构成的，而这些看法又塑造了你的经历。假设你相信要成为“好人”就必须努力追求完美。你可能被告知或读到过，灵性是完美的，因此认为你的职责就是尽最大努力将这种完美的灵性在肉身中再现。为此，你试图否认所有不完美的思想和情绪。你自己的“负面”想法让你感到惊恐。你可能也相信我之前告诉你的——你的思想创造了你的实相——因此你对任何攻击性本质的精神或实际表达变得更加恐惧。你可能过于担心伤害他人，以至于几乎不敢轻举妄动。一直试图保持完美远不止是麻烦：由于你的误解，它可能是灾难性的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Love, therefore, can contain hate very nicely. Hatred can contain love and be driven by it, particularly by an idealized love. (Pause.) You "hate" something that separates you from a loved object. It is precisely because the object is loved that it is so disliked if expectations are not met. You may love a parent, and if the parent does not seem to return the love and denies your expectations, then you may "hate" the same parent because of the love that leads you to expect more. The hatred is meant to get you your love back. It is supposed to lead to a communication from you, stating your feelings -- clearing the air, so to speak, and bringing you closer to the love object. Hatred is not the denial of love, then, but an attempt to regain it, and a painful recognition of circumstances that separate you from it.
quotes.translation: 因此，爱可以很好地容纳恨。恨可以包含爱，并被爱所驱动，尤其是被理想化的爱所驱动。（停顿。）你“恨”那些将你与所爱对象分离的事物。正是因为对象被爱，如果期望没有得到满足，它才会如此令人不悦。你可能爱一位父母，如果这位父母似乎没有回报你的爱并辜负了你的期望，那么你可能会因为爱而期望更多，从而“恨”同一位父母。恨意在本质上是想让你重新获得爱。它本应引导你进行沟通，表达你的感受——可以说，是为了澄清气氛，让你更接近所爱的对象。因此，恨不是对爱的否定，而是试图重新获得爱，并痛苦地认识到那些将你与爱分离的环境。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You can understand what is meant by saying that your consciousness fluctuates -- for each individual is aware of various intensities and concentrations. You are more alert, or, in your terms more conscious on some occasions than others. Now the same applies to these units of consciousness -- and to atoms, molecules, electrons, and other such phenomena. The world literally blinks off and on. This reality of fluctuation in no way bothers your own feeling of consistency, however. The "holes (spelled) of nonexistence" are plugged up by the process of selectivity. This process chooses significances then, again, around which experience is built, and around which "life" is felt. The very sensations of one kind of life then automatically set up barriers against other such "world-schemes" (hyphen) that do not correlate with their own.
quotes.translation: 你可以理解所谓你的意识在波动的含义——因为每个人都能察觉到不同的强度和集中度。在某些场合你更警觉，或者用你的话说，更清醒。同样的情况也适用于这些意识单位——以及原子、分子、电子和其他此类现象。世界实际上在闪烁明灭。然而，这种波动的实相绝不会干扰你自身的一致感。“不存在的空洞”通过选择性过程被填补。这个过程再次选择意义，围绕这些意义构建经历，并围绕这些意义感受“生命”。一种生命的特定感觉会自动设置障碍，以对抗其他与其自身不相关的“世界体系”。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-03-10-03The _Unknown_ Reality_ V_O - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: At the same time, EE units (see Note 3) became manifest. I have said, for example, that the universe expands as an idea does, and so the visible universe sprang into being in the same manner. The same energy that gave birth to the universe is, in those terms, still being created. The EE units contain within themselves the latent knowledge of all of the various species that can emerge under those conditions. It is according to your relative position. You can say that it took untold centuries for the EE units to "initially" combine, forming classifications of matter and various species, or you can say that this process happened at once. In your terms, each species is aware of the condition of each other species, and of the entire environment. In those terms the environment forms the species and the species form the environment. There were fully developed men -- that is, of full intellect, emotion, and will -- living at the same time, in your terms, as those creatures supposed to be man's evolutionary ancestors.
quotes.translation: 同时，EE单位（见注3）变得显化。例如，我曾说过，宇宙像思想一样扩展，可见宇宙也以同样的方式突然出现。从那些角度来看，赋予宇宙生命的同一能量仍在被创造。EE单位在自身内部包含了所有可能在这些条件下出现的各种物种的潜在知识。这取决于你的相对位置。你可以说，EE单位花费了无数个世纪才“最初”结合，形成物质和不同物种的分类，或者你也可以说这个过程是瞬间发生的。用你的话说，每个物种都能意识到其他物种的状况以及整个环境。从那些角度来看，环境塑造了物种，物种也塑造了环境。用你的话说，完全发展的人类——即拥有完整智力、情感和意志的人类——与那些被认为是人类进化祖先的生物同时存在。

quotes.text: "When I first mentioned the family name, Grunaargh (as Seth spelled it out for us in that session over three years ago), I knew that its members had something to do with printing, or the promulgation of printed material. Since at the time I was working as a typesetter,2 I figured my impression had derived from that. However, after that session my impression 'grew' in such a way that I knew this family had something to do in a more direct way with the printing process -- with the fascination of putting ideas down on paper through the use of typefaces that would, as much as the language involved, express the ideas behind the words themselves. In the plant where I worked at the time, I 'recognized' several people in the Grunaargh family -- all were printers -- and with a feeling quite as strong as the recognition I had for Sumari.
quotes.translation: “当我第一次提到这个家族姓氏Grunaargh（正如赛斯在三年前的那节课上为我们拼写的那样）时，我知道其成员与印刷或印刷材料的传播有关。由于当时我是一名排字工，我以为我的印象源于此。然而，在那节课之后，我的印象‘增长’了，以至于我知道这个家族与印刷过程有更直接的关系——与通过使用字体将思想付诸纸上的魅力有关，这些字体就像所涉及的语言一样，表达了文字本身背后的思想。在我当时工作的工厂里，我‘认出’了Grunaargh家族的几个人——他们都是印刷工——那种感觉之强烈，不亚于我对Sumari的认同感。
