quotes.text: There seems to be a division between science and religion, for even organized religion has an intuitive basis. The male scientist is often ashamed of using his intuitions, for not only do they appear to be unscientific, but female as well. It is what others will think about his masculinity that such a man is concerned with. To be "illogical" is a scientific "crime" -- not so much because it is an unscientific attribute, but because it is considered a feminine one. Science has followed the male orientation and become its epitome. Up until the present, science has consistently tried to do without the so-called feminine qualities. It has divorced knowledge from emotion, understanding from identification, and stressed sexuality over personhood.
quotes.translation: 科学与宗教之间似乎存在分歧，因为即使是有组织的宗教也有其直觉基础。男性科学家常常羞于运用自己的直觉，因为这不仅显得不科学，而且带有女性特质。这类男性关心的是别人会如何看待他的男子气概。变得“不合逻辑”是一种科学上的“罪行”——与其说是因为它是不科学的属性，不如说是因为它被认为是女性化的属性。科学遵循了男性导向并成为其缩影。直到现在，科学一直试图摒弃所谓的女性特质。它将知识与情感分离，将理解与认同分离，并强调性征而非人格。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: There were also some women who passed as monks, living lives of a solitary nature and carrying on for years. No works bear their feminine names, for they used male ones. It goes without saying that lesbian and homosexual relationships flourished in such surroundings. The Church closed its eyes as long as the relationships were sexual in nature. Only when love and devotion were diverted from the Church was there real concern. Intellect and emotions became further divided then. This resulted of course in an overemphasis upon dogma -- rules and the ritualization that had to be colorful and rich because it would be the one outlet allowed in which creativity could be handled. The Church believed that sexual experience belonged to the so-called lower or animal instincts, and so did usual human love. On the other hand, spiritual love and devotion could not be muddied by sexual expression, and so any normal strong relationship became a threat to the expression of piety.
quotes.translation: 也有女性假扮僧侣，过着独居生活并持续多年。没有作品署上她们女性的名字，因为她们用了男性的名字。不言而喻，在这种环境中，女同性恋和男同性恋关系蓬勃发展。只要这些关系本质上停留在性层面，教会便睁一只眼闭一只眼。只有当爱与奉献从教会转移时，才会引起真正的担忧。理智与情感由此进一步分裂。这自然导致了对教条——规则与仪式化的过度强调，仪式必须色彩丰富且华丽，因为它将成为允许创造力得以处理的唯一出口。教会认为性体验属于所谓的低级或动物本能，普通的人类之爱也是如此。另一方面，灵性之爱与奉献不能被性表达所玷污，因此任何正常的强烈关系都成为对虔诚表达的威胁。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: The span of a god's love can perhaps equally hold within its vision the existences of all individuals at one time in an infinite loving glance that beholds each person, seeing each with all his or her peculiar characteristics and tendencies. Such a god's glance would delight in each person's difference from each other person. This would not be a blanket love, a soupy porridge of a glance in which individuality melted, but a love based on a full understanding of each individual. The emotion of love brings you closest to an understanding of the nature of All That Is. Love incites dedication, commitment. It specifies. You cannot, therefore, honestly insist that you love humanity and all people equally if you do not love one other person. If you do not love yourself, it is quite difficult to love another.
quotes.translation: 神的爱的广度或许能同时以无限慈爱的一瞥，将所有人的存在尽收眼底，这一瞥注视着每个人，看到每个人及其所有独特的特质与倾向。这样的神会因每个人与他人的差异而喜悦。这不是一种笼统的爱，不是一种让个体消融的稀粥般的一瞥，而是基于对每个个体充分理解的爱。爱的情感让你最接近理解一切万有的本质。爱激发奉献、承诺。它具体化。因此，如果你不爱另一个人，你就无法诚实地坚持你平等地爱全人类。如果你不爱自己，爱他人就相当困难。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: When love and sexuality are artificially divided, however, or considered as antagonistic to each other, then all kinds of problems arise. Permanent relationships become most difficult to achieve under such conditions, and often love finds little expression, while one of its most natural channels is closed off. Many children give their greatest expression of love to toys, dolls, or imaginary playmates, because so many stereotyped patterns have already limited other expressions. Their feelings toward parents become ambiguous as a result of the identification procedures thrust upon them. Love, sexuality, and play, curiosity and explorative characteristics, merge in the child in a natural manner. Yet it soon learns that areas of exploration are limited even as far as its own body is concerned. The child is not free to contemplate its own parts. The body is early forbidden territory, so that the child feels it is wrong to love itself in any fashion.
quotes.translation: 然而，当爱与性被人为地分割，或被视为彼此对立时，各种问题便会出现。在这种条件下，持久的关系变得极难实现，爱往往难以表达，而其最自然的渠道之一却被关闭。许多孩子将爱的最强烈表达给予玩具、玩偶或想象中的玩伴，因为太多刻板模式已经限制了其他表达方式。由于强加于他们的认同程序，他们对父母的情感变得模糊不清。爱、性、玩耍、好奇与探索的特质，在孩子身上以自然的方式融合。然而，孩子很快便学到，即使就自己的身体而言，探索的领域也是有限的。孩子不能自由地审视自己的部位。身体很早就成为禁区，以至于孩子觉得以任何方式爱自己都是错误的。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Many of my readers are familiar with private meditation, when concentration is focused in one particular area. There are many methods and schools of thought here, but a highly suggestive state of mind results, in which spiritual, mental, and physical goals are sought. It is impossible to meditate without a goal, for that intent is itself a purpose. Unfortunately, many of your public health programs, and commercial statements through the various media, provide you with mass meditations of a most deplorable kind. I refer to those in which the specific symptoms of various diseases are given, in which the individual is further told to examine the body with those symptoms in mind. I also refer to those statements that just as unfortunately specify diseases for which the individual may experience no symptoms of an observable kind, but is cautioned that these disastrous physical events may be happening despite his or her feelings of good health. Here the generalized fears fostered by religious, scientific, and cultural beliefs are often given as blueprints of diseases in which a person can find a specific focus -- the individual can say: "Of course, I feel listless, or panicky, or unsafe since I have such-and-such a disease."
quotes.translation: 我的许多读者熟悉私人冥想，即注意力集中在某个特定领域。这里有许多方法和思想流派，但会产生一种高度暗示性的心智状态，在其中寻求灵性、心智和身体的目标。没有目标就无法冥想，因为那个意图本身就是目的。不幸的是，你们的许多公共卫生项目，以及通过各种媒体发布的商业声明，为你们提供了最可悲的大众冥想。我指的是那些列举各种疾病具体症状的冥想，其中进一步告诉个体要带着这些症状检查身体。我还指那些同样不幸的声明，它们具体指出个体可能没有明显症状的疾病，但警告说，尽管个体感觉健康，这些灾难性的身体事件可能正在发生。在这里，由宗教、科学和文化信仰所培养的普遍恐惧，常常被作为疾病的蓝图，个体可以从中找到特定的焦点——个体可以说："当然，我感到无精打采、恐慌或不安全，因为我患有某种疾病。"
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now give us a moment ... As creatures dwelling in time and space, your senses provide you with highly specific data, and with a cohesive-enough physical reality. Each person may react to the seasons in a very personalized manner, and yet you all share those natural events. They provide a framework for experience. It is up to the conscious mind to interpret sense events as clearly and concisely as possible. This allows for the necessary freedom of action for psychological and physical mobility. You are an imaginative species, and so the physical world is colored, charged, by your own imaginative projections, and powered by the great sweep of the emotions. But when you are confused or upset, it is an excellent idea to return your attention to the natural world as it appears at any given moment -- to sense its effect upon you as separate from your own projections.
quotes.translation: 现在请给我们一点时间……作为栖居于时空中的生物，你们的感官为你们提供高度具体的数据，以及一个足够连贯的物理实相。每个人可能以非常个性化的方式对季节做出反应，然而你们都共享那些自然事件。它们为经验提供了一个框架。有意识的心智有责任尽可能清晰简洁地解读感官事件。这允许了心理和身体活动所需的必要自由。你们是一个富有想象力的物种，因此物质世界被你们自己的想象投射所着色、充电，并由情感的宏大浪潮驱动。但当你们感到困惑或不安时，将注意力返回到自然世界——无论在任何特定时刻它呈现为何种面貌——是一个极好的主意，去感受它对你的影响，将其与你自己的投射分开。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: hen he originally gave us the 812th session some 16 months ago, Seth told us it would "be part of a chapter later in the book...." [See my opening notes for Session 814.] The 812th session was held following an encounter Jane and I had with an unexpected visitor, although these excerpts aren't personal at all. They fit in well with material in Mass Events, however -- indeed, as soon as Seth confirmed Jane's headings for Part 3 and Chapter 6, we knew that we'd found the place to present this material. We made our decision without consulting Seth, by the way.

quotes.text: The parents have worked to give their children such advantages, and the parents themselves are somewhat confused by their children's attitudes. The money and position, however, have often been attained as a result of the belief in man's competitive nature -- and that belief itself erodes the very prizes it produces: The fruit is bitter in the mouth. Many of the parents believed, quite simply, that the purpose of life was to make more money. Virtue consisted of the best car, or house or swimming pool -- proof that one could survive in a tooth-and-claw world. But the children wondered: What about those other feelings that stirred in their consciousnesses? What about those purposes they sensed? The hearts of some of them were like vacuums, waiting to be filled. They looked for values, but at the same time they felt that they were themselves sons and daughters of a species tainted, at loose ends, with no clear destinations.
quotes.translation: 父母们努力给予孩子这些优势，而父母们自己却对孩子的态度有些困惑。然而，金钱和地位往往是由于相信人类的竞争本性而获得的——而这种信念本身侵蚀了它所产生的东西：果实入口苦涩。许多父母简单地相信，生活的目的是赚更多的钱。美德在于最好的汽车、房子或游泳池——证明一个人能在弱肉强食的世界中生存。但孩子们想知道：那些在他们意识中搅动的其他情感呢？他们感受到的那些目的呢？他们中有些人的心像真空，等待被填满。他们寻找价值，但同时他们觉得自己是一个被玷污的物种的子女，漫无目的，没有明确的目的地。

quotes.text: The chicken cannot read a book. It cannot choose to read. The plant cannot choose to walk down the street. The chicken and the plant can choose to live or die, however -- rather important issues in the existence of any entity. They can choose to like or dislike their environment, and to change it according to their individual circumstances. It is fashionable to say that some scientific laws can be proven at microscopic levels, where, for example, small particles can be accelerated far beyond [their usual states]. But you quite studiously ignore that feeling exists on microscopic levels, that there can be psychological particles, much less come to the conclusion that all particles are psychological particles, with their own impetuses for development and value fulfillment. That is why atoms join together to form matter. They seek the fulfillment of themselves through form. They cooperatively choose the forms that they take.
quotes.translation: 鸡无法阅读书籍，也无法选择去阅读。植物无法选择在街上行走。然而，鸡和植物可以选择生存或死亡——这对任何实体的存在而言都是相当重要的事。它们可以选择喜欢或不喜欢自己的环境，并根据自身处境改变环境。如今流行说某些科学定律可以在微观层面得到证明，例如微小粒子能被加速到远超[其通常状态]。但你们却刻意忽视感受存在于微观层面的事实，忽视存在心理粒子，更遑论得出所有粒子都是心理粒子的结论——它们各自拥有发展的动力和价值完成的渴望。这正是原子结合形成物质的原因。它们通过形态寻求自我完成，并合作选择自己所采取的形态。

quotes.text: Now: The lamplight episode. Here you did as you supposed. You viewed that inner light, but the lampshades had two purposes: one, as you surmised, to give you a comforting image, literally to shade your eyes. Ruburt was correct, however, in seeing the connection between the lampshades and the Nazi experiments (in World War II) with human skin. The movie (on television last night), about cloning and Nazi atrocities, had made you wonder about the nature of life once again, and man's immortality. The connection with cloning came out in the lampshades made of (human) skins, in the old news stories--though your lampshades merely stood for those, and were of fabric. The connection was beneath, however, and also represented your feeling that even those people tortured to death did live again. They were not extinguished. Their consciousnesses were indeed like bulbs, say, turned on in new lamps. The lights connected life and death, then. The lights also represented pure knowing.
quotes.translation: 现在：台灯事件。你确实如自己所想的那样行事。你看见了那内在之光，但灯罩有两个作用：其一如你所推测，是为了给你舒适的意象，字面意义上遮蔽你的眼睛。然而鲁柏正确看出了灯罩与（二战中）纳粹用人体皮肤做实验的关联。昨晚电视上关于克隆和纳粹暴行的电影，让你再次思考生命的本质与人类的不朽。用（人）皮制成的灯罩与克隆的关联，在旧新闻报道中显现——尽管你的灯罩只是象征性的织物制品。但深层关联在于，这也代表你的感受：即使那些被折磨致死的人确实重获新生。他们并未被消灭。他们的意识确实如同灯泡，在新灯具中点亮。光明由此连接了生与死。光明也代表纯粹的了知。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Those "properties" are the faculties of the imagination, creativity, telepathy, clairvoyance, and dreaming, as well as the functions of logic and reason. You know that you dream. You know that you think. Those are direct experiences. (Pause.) Anytime you use instruments to probe into the nature of reality, you are looking at a kind of secondary evidence, no matter how excellent the instruments may be. The subjective evidence of dreaming, for example, is far more "convincing" and irrefutable than is the evidence for an expanding universe, black holes, or even atoms and molecules themselves. Although instruments can indeed be most advantageous in many ways, they still present you with secondary rather than primary tools of investigation--and they distort the nature of reality far more than the subjective attributes of thoughts, feelings, and intuitions do.
quotes.translation: 那些"属性"包括想象力、创造力、心灵感应、千里眼、做梦的能力，以及逻辑与理性的功能。你知道自己做梦，你知道自己思考。这些都是直接经验。（停顿）每当你使用仪器探究实相本质时，无论仪器多么精良，你看到的都只是次级证据。例如，做梦的主观证据远比膨胀宇宙、黑洞甚至原子分子本身的证据更"令人信服"且无可辩驳。尽管仪器在许多方面确实极为有利，但它们呈现的是次级而非首要的探究工具——它们对实相本质的扭曲程度，远超过思想、感受和直觉的主观属性。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: They are the dreams that warn of famines or of wars. Such dreams, however, can also be triggered often, as in your own times, when the conscious mind is convinced that the survival of the species is threatened--and in such cases the dreams then actually represent man's fears. Overanxiety, then, can confuse the genetic system, and in a variety of ways. The existence of each of the species is dependent upon trust, indeed a biological optimism, in which each species feels the freedom to develop the potentials of its members in relative safety, within the natural frameworks of existence. Each species comes into being not merely feeling a natural built-in trust in its own validity, but is literally propelled by exuberance in its ability to cope with its environment. It knows that it is uniquely suited to its place within life's framework. The young of all species exhibit an unquenchable rambunctiousness. That rambunctiousness is built in.
quotes.translation: 它们是预示饥荒或战争的梦境。然而在你们这个时代，当意识心确信物种生存受到威胁时，这类梦境也常被触发——此时这些梦境实际上代表了人类的恐惧。过度焦虑会以多种方式扰乱基因系统。每个物种的存在都依赖于信任，确切地说是生物性的乐观主义，使每个物种在存在的自然框架内，感受到在相对安全中发展成员潜能的自由。每个物种诞生时不仅天生信任自身有效性，更被应对环境能力的丰沛活力所推动。它知道自己独特地适应生命框架中的位置。所有物种的幼崽都展现出无法抑制的活力，这种活力是与生俱来的。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Here in Dreams Seth uses the uncertainty principle as an analogy (and an excellent one), meaning that as the positions and motions of elementary particles, say, cannot be simultaneously measured precisely, so our genetic qualities and their motions can not always be specifically determined. In Dreams he's already said (in Session 909) that the human species has an "amazing interplay between genetic preciseness and genetic freedom," and (in Session 910) that "your genetic structure reacts to each thought that you have, to the state of your emotions, to your psychological climate." Choices and probabilities apply. Thus do we avoid genetic rigidity.

quotes.text: Your stated universe emerged out of that kind of interval, emerging from a master event whose true nature remains uncaptured by your definitions--so there will be places in our book where I may say that an event known to you is true and untrue at the same time, or that it is both myth and fact. And in so doing I hope to lead you toward some psychic comprehension of a kind of event far too large for your usual categories of true and false. [Perhaps], then, you will let your imaginations play upon the usual events of your world, and glimpse at least in part that greater brilliance that illuminates them, so that it leads you intuitively to a feeling for the source of events and the source of your world. The units of consciousness that I have mentioned are (underlined) that, and they do behave as I have said. They are also in other terms entities, fragments of All That Is, if you prefer--divine fragments of power and majesty, containing (pause) all of the powers of consciousness as you think of it, concentrations without substance in your terms.
quotes.translation: 你们所宣称的宇宙正是从那种间隙中浮现，源自一个其真实本质无法被你们定义捕捉的主事件——因此在我们书中某些地方，我可能会说你们所知的事件同时为真也为假，或既是神话也是事实。如此做时，我希望引导你们对某种远超你们真假范畴的事件产生心灵理解。[或许]你们会让想象力在世界的寻常事件上嬉戏，至少瞥见照亮它们的更伟大光辉，从而直觉地引导你们感受事件的源头与世界的源头。我提到的意识单位正是（强调）如此，它们确实如我所说般运作。若你愿意，它们也可被称为"一切万有"的实体碎片——神圣的力量与庄严碎片，包含（停顿）你们所认知的意识所有力量，以你们术语而言是无实质的凝聚。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "Life as we know it is excitement; highly organized--excitement at all levels, microscopic, macroscopic, psychic. It is the result of the relationship between balance and imbalance, between organization and 'chaos.' It is excitement ever in a state of flux, forming psychic and material knots. It is explosive yet filled with order; it becomes so filled with itself that it explodes in the same way that a flower bursts; the same principle is acting in a hurricane or a flood or a murder or the creation of a poem, or the formation of a dream; in the birth and death of individuals and nations. We instinctively know that disasters mimic the birth and death of cells within our bodies--we instinctively know that all life survives death, that death is the bursting of life into new forms, hence our fascination with accidents and fires. The psyche itself leapfrogs our beliefs at usual conscious levels, and sees us as a part of all life, excitedly forming all kinds of complexes which then fill themselves to the brim, exploding, escaping the framework only to form another. The emotions themselves can sense this when we let them, and grasping that sense of excitement can show us a glimpse of the even greater freedom of our own psychic existence, which flows into us as individuals and then bursts apart that short-lived form into another, as the excitement of individuation leaps from life to life."
quotes.translation: "我们所知的生命是兴奋；高度组织化——微观、宏观、心灵各层面的兴奋。它是平衡与失衡、秩序与'混沌'之间关系的产物。它是永远处于流变状态的兴奋，形成心灵与物质的结。它充满爆发性却蕴含秩序；它如此充盈自身，以致如花朵绽放般爆发；同样的原理作用于飓风、洪水、谋杀、诗歌创作或梦境形成；作用于个体与国家的生灭。我们本能知晓灾难模仿体内细胞的生灭——我们本能知晓所有生命超越死亡，死亡是生命迸发为新形态，因此我们对事故与火灾着迷。心灵本身在通常意识层面跃过我们的信念，视我们为所有生命的一部分，兴奋地形成各种情结，这些情结满溢后爆发，挣脱框架只为形成新的情结。当我们允许时，情绪本身能感知这点，抓住那种兴奋感能让我们一瞥自身心灵存在的更大自由——它流入我们个体，然后迸裂那短暂形态进入另一形态，正如个体化的兴奋从生命跃向生命。"
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 6. In Chapter 8 of Dreams, see the opening notes for Session 915.

quotes.text: "His poetry acted in some regards as a stimulator. That breakthrough, you might say, with perhaps some exaggeration, was a lifesaver, for without some such expansion Ruburt would have felt unable to continue the particular brand of his existence. It is not possible to say in words what one person or another looks for in life, or what unique features best promote his or her growth and development. Even two plants of the same kind sometimes require completely different treatments. The sessions, then, opened the door to a particular kind of value fulfillment that was natural to Ruburt's being. Now to some extent it was that poor, unhappy sinful self, a psychological structure formed by beliefs and feelings, that was also seeking its own redemption, since even it had outgrown the framework that so defined it.
quotes.translation: 他的诗作在某种程度上起到了刺激作用。你可以说，或许有些夸张，那次突破是救命稻草，因为如果没有某种程度的扩展，鲁柏会觉得自己无法继续他那独特的存在方式。无法用言语说清一个人在生活中寻求什么，或者什么独特特质最能促进他或她的成长与发展。即使是同一种植物的两株，有时也需要完全不同的照料。因此，这些课程为鲁柏的存在自然开启了一种特定的价值实现之门。现在，在某种程度上，正是那个可怜、不快乐、有罪的自我——一个由信念和感受形成的心理结构——也在寻求自身的救赎，因为它已经超越了那个如此定义它的框架。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: 11. When Mass Events is published, see Session 835 for February 7, 1979, in Chapter 6.

quotes.text: Ironically, as individuals and nations we talk about casting off old beliefs while cherishing them as long as possible. Why have large segments of consciousness chosen to operate in such a fashion? I think we're creating a probable reality in which consciousness has the absolute freedom to explore all facets of itself--every one we can think of, and therefore create. Within our national orientations, within our religious and secular, scientific and artistic structures, we are choosing to go to the extremes of "good" and "bad," and to deal with the consequences, all stewing together in what seems like an impossible mix of reason and emotion, learning and joy, pain and violence, and life and death. Naturally, many of us don't like certain facets of our creations, yet we must deal with all of them if we are to make any sense out of our reality. Otherwise, our growing will be too limited; we'll remain slaves to our animosities.
quotes.translation: 讽刺的是，作为个体和国家，我们一边谈论抛弃旧信念，一边却尽可能长久地珍视它们。为什么意识的大片领域选择以这种方式运作？我认为我们正在创造一种可能实相，其中意识拥有绝对自由去探索自身的所有面向——每一个我们能想到的，并因此创造出来。在我们的国家取向中，在我们的宗教与世俗、科学与艺术结构中，我们选择走向"善"与"恶"的极端，并处理其后果，这一切都在理性与情感、学习与喜悦、痛苦与暴力、生与死的看似不可能的混合中一起熬煮。自然，我们许多人不喜欢自己创造的某些面向，但如果我们想从自己的实相中找出任何意义，就必须处理所有这些面向。否则，我们的成长将过于受限；我们将继续成为自身敌意的奴隶。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-08-10-08Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_Tow - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: The ordinary violence involved with these events leads me to comment upon the theological concept of privation theory, and the military one of perception theory--for again, I think the two are closely related, not only to each other but to the points I've made in this note. In mundane terms, both represent longstanding distortions in perception of the great basic creativity of All That Is. I suggest that the reader review Seth's material on the basically creative use of violence as I quoted it in Chapter 10; see Note 2 for Session 933.

quotes.text: "Any event, therefore, has an invisible thickness, a multidimensional basis. Your skies are filled with breezes, currents, clouds, sunlight, dust particles and so forth. The sky vaults above the entire planet. The invisible [vault of] Framework 2 contains endless patterns that change as, say, clouds do -- that mix and merge to form your psychological climate. Thoughts have what we will for now term electromagnetic properties. In those terms your thoughts mix and match with others in Framework 2, creating mass patterns that form the overall psychological basis behind world events. Again, however, Framework 2 is not neutral, but automatically inclined toward what we will here term good or constructive developments. It is a growth medium. Constructive or 'positive' feelings or thoughts are more easily materialized than 'negative' ones because they are in keeping with Framework 2's characteristics."
quotes.translation: "因此，任何事件都有一种无形的厚度，一个多维的基础。你的天空充满了微风、气流、云朵、阳光、尘埃粒子等等。天空穹顶覆盖着整个星球。无形的[穹顶]架构二包含了无尽的模式，它们像云一样变化——混合并融合，形成你的心理气候。思想具有我们暂且称之为电磁特性的东西。用这些术语来说，你的思想在架构二中与其他思想混合匹配，形成大规模模式，构成世界事件背后的整体心理基础。然而，再次强调，架构二并非中立，而是自动倾向于我们在此称之为善的或建设性的发展。它是一个成长媒介。建设性或'正面'的感受或思想比'负面'的更容易物质化，因为它们符合架构二的特性。"
quotes.book: The Magical Approach
quotes.source_file: 10-09-10-09The Magical Approach_ Seth Spea - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth speaks in Session 826, held for Mass Events on March 8, 1978:

quotes.text: I have told you that you create your dreams, in actuality, not in theory alone. You create an actuality, a dream universe, as real as the physical universe. It simply cannot be directly perceived within the physical universe. There is always interaction. You can perceive the dream experience, you can receive its particular intensities, because you have created the dream experience within the universe of those intensities. But the dream universe and the dream experience, like thoughts and emotions, are independent of you in existence. You influence the dream universe but you cannot stop a dream experience after you have created it, and you must create it within the small range of intensities available for its existence, for there are no other conditions or agreeable ranges of intensity within which the dream experience can exist.
quotes.translation: 我告诉过你，你创造你的梦，实际上，而不仅仅是理论上。你创造了一个实相，一个梦宇宙，与物质宇宙一样真实。它只是无法在物质宇宙内被直接感知。总是存在互动。你可以感知梦体验，你可以接收其特定的强度，因为你已经在那些强度的宇宙中创造了梦体验。但梦宇宙和梦体验，就像思想和情感一样，在存在上独立于你。你影响梦宇宙，但一旦你创造了梦体验，你就无法停止它，而且你必须在可供其存在的小范围强度内创造它，因为没有其他条件或可接受的强度范围能让梦体验存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: The spontaneous self when it did escape, you see, managed to do so only under circumstances where the explosive impulses shattered their way through. In the years between there was some considerable improvement in balance. When the situations developed which we have discussed, setting off the old conflicts, again you see, then the discipline idea was short-circuited back to the old compulsive behavior, though in different form, and with the old religious connotation of self denial. The old fear of spontaneity returned, and the methodical attempt to deny subconscious impulses; the old feeling of unworthiness was also activated, and the body duly denied. Now this self denial began in the Catholic home, and he was peculiarly prone to accept it. It was part of the old Catholic training, and he fell for it under a new guise.
quotes.translation: 你看，当自发自我确实逃脱时，它只能在爆炸性冲动冲破一切的情况下做到。在随后的几年里，平衡有了相当大的改善。当我们已经讨论过的情况发展起来，触发了旧冲突时，你看，纪律观念又短路回旧的强迫行为，尽管形式不同，并带有旧的自律宗教内涵。对自发性的旧恐惧回来了，以及有条不紊地否认潜意识冲动的尝试；旧的无价值感也被激活，身体被恰当地否定。现在，这种自律始于天主教家庭，他特别容易接受它。这是旧天主教训练的一部分，他在新的伪装下陷入了其中。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 7 of The Seth Material (Sessions 281-333)
quotes.source_file: ES-7-The Early Sessions-Book 7 of The Seth Material(281-333).json

quotes.text: The role-playing in the dream drama would be one in which you creatively worked out the problems that caused the imbalances to begin with. Dreams of a strongly aggressive nature in this context may be very beneficial to a given individual, allowing the release of usually inhibited feelings and freeing the body from tension. By such constant dream therapy, both body and mind regulate themselves to a large degree. So your flesh is affected by your dreams.
quotes.translation: 在梦戏剧中的角色扮演，将是你创造性地解决最初导致失衡的问题的一种方式。在这种背景下，具有强烈攻击性质的梦可能对特定个体非常有益，允许释放通常被抑制的感受，并使身体摆脱紧张。通过这种持续的梦疗法，身体和心灵在很大程度上自我调节。因此，你的肉体受到你梦境的影响。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In the dream world, however, each feeling or idea can be immediately expressed and experienced. The physical world has buildings in it that you manufacture -- that is, they do not spring up naturally from the ground itself. In the same way, your thoughts are "manufactured products" in the dream world. They are a part of the environment and appear within its reality, though they change shape and form constantly, as physically manufactured objects do not.
quotes.translation: 然而在梦境世界中，每个感受或意念都能立即被表达和体验。物质世界中有你建造的建筑物——也就是说，它们并非自然从地面生长出来。同样地，你的思想在梦境世界中是"制造出来的产品"。它们是环境的一部分，出现在其现实之中，尽管它们不断改变形状和形式，而物质制造物则不会如此。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: A bond of hate will unite you, but the bond was originally based upon love. In this case however you aggravate and exaggerate all those differences from the ideal, and focus upon them predominantly. In any given case all of this is consciously available to you. It requires only an honest and determined attempt to become aware of your own feelings and beliefs. Even your hateful fantasies, left alone, will return you to a reconciliation and release love.
quotes.translation: 仇恨的纽带会将你联结，但这纽带最初基于爱。然而在这种情况下，你会加剧并夸大所有与理想相异的特质，并主要聚焦于它们。在任何特定情况下，这一切都可供你有意识地获取。只需诚实而坚定地尝试觉察自己的感受和信念。即使是你充满仇恨的幻想，若任其自然，也会让你回归和解并释放爱。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: This involves, to begin with, an almost impossible task. Data from the inner senses is vivid, it is reliable, it makes an impression upon the conscious individual. It is your insistence upon translating this material into physical terms that causes your difficulty. You do not insist upon seeing, feeling or touching a psychological experience, and yet you do not say that a psychological experience does not exist because you cannot hold it in both hands.
quotes.translation: 这首先涉及一项几乎不可能的任务。来自内在感官的数据生动而可靠，会给有意识的个体留下印象。正是你坚持将这些材料转化为物质术语才造成了困难。你并不坚持要看到、触摸或感受一种心理体验，但也不会因为无法双手握住就说心理体验不存在。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Such personalities try to affect the feelings and thoughts of a civilization, for these thoughts and feelings will alone bring about events. Personalities may be born within your system and find that it does not challenge them, or it is not to their liking in any case. (Pause.) Give us a moment on this. (Pause.)
quotes.translation: 这类人格试图影响一个文明的感受和思想，因为唯有这些思想和感受才能带来事件。人格可能诞生于你的系统内，却发现它无法挑战自己，或者无论如何都不合其意。（停顿。）请给我们一点时间思考这个。（停顿。）
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: I do not mean to mislead you. Basic reality is not by nature terrifying. It is however vibrant and direct to the extreme, and you must be prepared before you experience such an ultrasonic action. A direct experience of reality involves complete use of all the inner senses to an integrated cognizance field. A very weak analogy can be found if you imagine at one time hearing the most exciting and moving music imaginable, while simultaneously smelling the strongest but not necessarily unpleasant odor, viewing the most emotionally-charged scene while feeling intense and vivid bodily sensations. In normal life you even turn down outer-sense stimuli for the sake of simplicity, and to enable you to focus upon those stimuli close at hand.
quotes.translation: 我并非有意误导你。基本实相本质上并不可怕。但它极其充满活力且直接到极致，你必须在体验这种超音速行动前做好准备。对实相的直接体验涉及完全运用所有内在感官，达到一个整合的认知场。一个非常薄弱的类比是：想象你同时听到最激动人心、最动人的音乐，闻到最强烈但不一定难闻的气味，观看最充满情感的场景，同时感受强烈而生动的身体感觉。在正常生活中，你甚至为了简化而调低外在感官刺激，以便聚焦于手边的刺激。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: I am not a physical personality. Basically, however, neither are you. Your experience now is physical. You are a creator translating your expectations into physical form. The world is meant to serve as a reference point. The exterior appearance is a replica of inner desire. You can change your personal world. You do change it without knowing it. You have only to use your ability consciously, to examine the nature of your thoughts and feelings and project those with which you basically agree.
quotes.translation: 我不是一个物质人格。但基本上，你也不是。你现在的体验是物质的。你是一个创造者，将你的期望转化为物质形式。世界旨在作为一个参考点。外在表象是内在渴望的复制品。你可以改变你的个人世界。你确实在不知不觉中改变它。你只需有意识地运用你的能力，检视你思想和感受的本质，并投射那些你基本认同的。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: After you have managed to feel yourself above your body in your astral form, you again tell yourself to see the image of the same past self, you may then imagine its image merging with your own, and its memories a part of your own consciousness. When you have succeeded, then still feeling yourself in the astral form, tell yourself that you will next see the image of the life before last: the image of the person you were, then repeat the other steps.
quotes.translation: 当你成功在星光体形态中感觉自身高于身体后，再次告诉自己看见过去自我的影像，接着想象其影像与你的影像融合，其记忆成为你意识的一部分。成功后，保持星光体形态的感觉，告诉自己接下来将看见前前世的生命影像：你曾是那个人的影像，然后重复其他步骤。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: When the ego impedes such expression, then the emotions are translated into other actions, and can turn into impediments. It is only because the ego blocks freedom of the emotional expression, out of fear, that it appears to the ego that emotions are indeed fearful characteristics. Dammed up, they are indeed.
quotes.translation: 当自我阻碍这种表达时，情绪便转化为其他行动，并可能成为障碍。正因自我出于恐惧而阻挠情绪表达的自由，才使得情绪在自我看来确实是可怕的特质。被压抑的情绪确实如此。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 4 of The Seth Material (Sessions 149-198)
quotes.source_file: ES-4-The Early Sessions-Book 4 of The Seth Material(149-198).json

quotes.text: These then form physical objects, physical matter. Matter is the shape that basic experience takes when it intrudes into three dimensional systems. Matter is the shape of your dreams. Your dreams, emotions, feelings, thoughts, are (underlined) transformed into physical matter purposefully by this inner self.
quotes.translation: 这些情绪继而形成物质客体、物质实体。物质是基本经验侵入三维系统时所采取的形状。物质是你梦想的形状。你的梦想、情绪、感受、思想，都被这个内在自我有目的地转化为物质实体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: A person may choose a great talent instead, through which he or she will perceive reality and concentrate all experience. This will serve as a formidable focus, yet by its nature it may often preclude other experiences that many individuals find quite normal. Some artists with great ability may shut out intellectual maturity, utilizing native emotional qualities to such an extent and with such intensity that the mental reasoning faculties are largely shunted aside. (Pause.) Without rational illumination, the emotional elements may be so unwieldy that the artist, for all of his spontaneous expression, cannot relate in any kind of permanent situation of an intimate nature. For reason and emotion are natural counterparts.
quotes.translation: 一个人也可能选择一项伟大的才能，通过它来感知现实并集中所有经验。这将成为一个强大的焦点，但其本质往往可能排除许多常人认为相当正常的其他经验。某些极具天赋的艺术家可能排斥心智成熟，过度且强烈地运用原生情感特质，以至于理性思维功能被大幅搁置一旁。（停顿。）缺乏理性之光的照耀，情感元素可能变得如此难以驾驭，以至于艺术家尽管拥有自发表达，却无法维系任何稳定持久的亲密关系。因为理性与情感本是天然的对等体。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Medicine men may do a rain dance. They understand the innate relationship that exists within all portions of nature. You are taught to believe that faith can move mountains, yet many of you will find it extremely difficult to accept your own relationship with the environment. Your beliefs (often as opposed to your desires) cause wars. Your feelings represent the inner reality behind what you think of as purely natural phenomena, such as weather.
quotes.translation: 巫医会跳祈雨舞。他们理解自然万物之间存在的内在关联。你被教导相信信念可以移山，但许多人却极难接受自身与环境的关系。你的信念（往往与你的欲望相悖）引发战争。你的感受代表着那些被你视为纯粹自然现象（如天气）背后的内在实相。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: The psychic freedom and burst of energy that you will experience as a direct result of intimacy with land will be released regardless of the amount of land. Some feeling of ownership is necessary. However, though you may not want to accept this, old landowner Joseph, an acre will release this energy as well as ten acres. You have little conception of the energy that would be beneficially yours, even in the development of one acre that was yours.
quotes.translation: 与土地亲密接触所直接带来的心灵自由与能量爆发，无论土地面积大小都会释放。某种所有权感是必要的。然而，尽管你可能不愿接受，老地主约瑟夫，一英亩土地与十英亩土地释放的能量同样多。你几乎无法想象，即便只开发属于你的一英亩土地，那将有益于你的能量会有多大。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The Emotionalism within the household seemed to be threatening, and in a free form. You never knew when it would erupt, and as a child you felt helpless before it. Through precise renderings you felt that you imprisoned it, and therefore controlled it. In the beginning this had quite magical connotations for you. Basically you have felt this magical commitment to realistic work, for to leave it would be to break the lines of this imprisonment, letting loose the Emotions that you feared.
quotes.translation: 家庭中的情绪主义似乎具有威胁性，且以自由形式呈现。你永远不知道它何时会爆发，作为孩子你对此感到无助。通过精确的描绘，你觉得自己囚禁了它，从而控制了它。起初，这对你而言具有相当神奇的含义。你基本上一直对这种现实工作怀有神奇的承诺，因为放弃它就会打破这种囚禁的界限，释放你所恐惧的情绪。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: Thought and emotion then, is not only generally related to the physical mechanism, but as electrical actions, thoughts and emotions act directly upon the system. Again here you see, however, that it is the individual himself who initiates his own emotional climate, and then suffers or enjoys the results.
quotes.translation: 因此，思想和情绪不仅与身体机制普遍相关，而且作为电性活动，思想和情绪直接作用于系统。然而，这里你再次看到，是个人自己启动了自己的情绪氛围，然后承受或享受其结果。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: It knows full well its own indestructibility, and that of its fellows. What happens in the case of constructive psychic energy when it is purposely denied aggressive outlets? It finds out. What does it feel like to be the brunt of aggression? It forms another life situation and discovers the answer. How strong and potent is the energy that is at its command? How can it best be used? It is quite necessary that all of these questions be answered, for the inner self is composed of energy, and in other fields of activity thoughts and emotions are instantaneously translated, their results instantly seen. There is no saving time lag, as within your own system, and there is no physical body such as you know it.
quotes.translation: 它完全知道自己及其同伴的不可毁灭性。当建设性的心灵能量被故意拒绝侵略性出口时，会发生什么？它会去发现。成为侵略的冲击对象是什么感觉？它形成另一种生命情境并找到答案。它所掌控的能量有多强大和有力？如何最好地使用它？所有这些问题的答案都相当必要，因为内在自体由能量组成，在其他活动领域中，思想和情绪会瞬间转化，其结果立即显现。没有像你们系统内那样的时间延迟，也没有你们所知的物质身体。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Now it is easier perhaps for some of you to understand the simple stories and parables of beginnings of which I have spoken. But the time has come for mankind to take several steps further, to expand the nature of his own consciousness by trying to comprehend a more profound version of reality. You have outgrown the time of children's tales. When your own thoughts have a form and reality, when they have validity even in other systems of reality of which you are unaware, then it is not difficult to understand why other systems of probabilities are also affected by your own thoughts and emotions -- nor why the actions of the probable gods are not affected by what happens in other dimensions of existence.
quotes.translation: 现在，也许你们中有些人更容易理解我所说的那些关于起源的简单故事和寓言。但人类是时候再迈出几步，通过试图理解更深刻的实相版本，来扩展自己意识的本质。你们已经超越了童话故事的时代。当你们自己的思想具有形式和实相，甚至在你们未知的其他实相系统中也具有有效性时，就不难理解为什么其他概率系统也受到你们自己思想和情绪的影响——也不难理解为什么可能之神的行为不受其他存在维度中发生之事的影响。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: My dear Joseph, one word only: I would not leave you with the impression that I am truly displeased, or that I judge you unjustly. I do not want to hurt Ruburts feelings, and I have avoided making this statement thus far; however, for purely personal reasons I have been emotionally more involved with you in past instances. And I know your capabilities so well that when I seem severe it is only because I wish so for your happiness and success.
quotes.translation: 我亲爱的约瑟，只说一句：我不想让你觉得我真的不高兴，或我对你不公正。我不想伤害鲁柏的感情，至今我一直避免说这句话；然而，纯粹出于个人原因，在过去的情境中，我在情感上与你更为紧密。我如此了解你的能力，当我显得严厉时，那只是因为我如此希望你的幸福和成功。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Since these are far more lively than ordinary shadows, and are definitely more colorful, they may be more difficult to distinguish at first. You must remember that you are wandering through a mental or psychic landscape. You can stand before the shadow of a friend in the afternoon, in waking reality, and snap your fingers all you want to, but your friend's shadow will not move one whit. It will certainly not disappear because you tell it to. In the dream world, however, any hallucination will vanish immediately as soon as you recognize it as such, and tell it to go away. It was cast originally by your own thought or feeling, and when you withdraw that source, then its "shadow" is automatically gone.
quotes.translation: 由于这些比普通阴影生动得多，且绝对更加多彩，起初可能更难区分。你必须记住，你正在穿越一个心灵或精神的风景。在清醒实相中，你可以在下午站在朋友的影子前，随意打响指，但你朋友的影子不会移动分毫。它当然不会因为你命令它消失就消失。然而，在梦境世界中，任何幻象一旦你认出它并命令它离开，就会立即消失。它最初是由你自己的思想或感受投射的，当你撤回那个源头时，它的"阴影"就自动消失了。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: SESSION 720
source_raw.chapter_title: Section 3: "The Private Probable Man, the Private Probable Woman, the Species in Probabilities, and Blueprints for Realities" -- Nine sessions devoted to the importance of dreams in the creation of "concrete" events from probable ones. This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: You are not at the mercy of your emotions, either, for they are meant to follow the flow of your reasoning. Your mind is meant to perceive the physical environment clearly, and its judgments about the environment then activate the body's mechanisms to bring about proper response. If your beliefs about existence are fearful, then the emotional reactions will be those leading to stress. Your own value judgments need examination in such a case.
quotes.translation: 你同样不受情绪的摆布，因为情绪本应跟随你推理的流动。你的心智本应清晰地感知物质环境，其对环境的判断随后激活身体的机制以产生适当的反应。如果你对存在的信念充满恐惧，那么情绪反应将导致压力。在这种情况下，你需要审视自己的价值判断。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Give us a moment ... Those intimate realizations, however, had to be counterbalanced in line with certain purposes set by your species, and even for that matter momentarily set aside so that other abilities and characteristics could emerge. The species' sense of curiosity would not allow it to stay in any home territory for long,6 and so the sense of intimacy was purposely broken. It would become highly important again, however, when the planet was populated extensively, as it is now -- only the original feeling of home area has to be extended over the face of the earth. The "absent" portions of the self are ready to emerge. The other, to you probable, lines of consciousness can now come into play.
quotes.translation: 请稍等片刻……然而，那些亲密的领悟必须根据你们物种设定的特定目标加以平衡，甚至为了那个目的暂时搁置，以便其他能力和特性能够显现。物种的好奇心不允许它长久停留在任何家园领域，因此亲密感被有意打破。然而，当行星像现在这样被广泛居住时，这种亲密感将再次变得高度重要——只是最初的家园感必须扩展到地球的表面。“缺席”的自我部分已准备好显现。对你们而言可能存在的其他意识线索现在可以发挥作用。
quotes.book: The Unknown Reality Vol. 2
quotes.source_file: 10-04-10-04The _Unknown_ Reality_ VT - Jane Roberts.json

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: Though this thought-image usually is not seen by others, it is quite possible that in the future scientific instruments may perceive it. As it is, such an image may be perceived by those who have developed use of the inner senses. Any intense mental act -- thought or emotion -- will not only be constructed in some physical or pseudophysical manner, but will also bear to some extent the imprint of the personality who originally conceived it.
quotes.translation: 尽管这个思想影像通常不被他人所见，但未来科学仪器很可能感知到它。事实上，这样的影像可能被那些发展了内在感官使用的人所感知。任何强烈的心理活动——思想或情绪——不仅会以某种物质或准物质的方式构建，还会在一定程度上带有最初构想它的人格的印记。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Parents and physicians believe, instead, that the child is a victim, ill for no personal reason, but indisposed because of elements attacking him -- either the outside environment, or [something] working against him from within. The child may be told: "You have a cold because you got your feet wet." Or: "You caught the cold from Johnny or Sally." He may be told that he has a virus, so that it seems his body itself was invaded despite his will. He learns that such beliefs are acceptable. It is easier to go along than to be honest, particularly when honesty would often involve a kind of communication his parents might frown upon, or the expression of emotions that are quite unacceptable.
quotes.translation: 相反，父母和医生相信孩子是受害者，生病没有个人原因，而是因为攻击他的因素——要么是外部环境，要么是内部与他作对的东西。孩子可能会被告知：“你感冒是因为脚湿了。”或者：“你是从约翰尼或莎莉那里传染的。”他可能被告知他感染了病毒，以至于似乎他的身体违背他的意愿被入侵了。他学会接受这样的信念。随波逐流比诚实更容易，尤其是当诚实常常涉及父母可能不赞成的沟通方式，或表达相当不可接受的情绪时。
quotes.book: The Individual and the Nature of Events
quotes.source_file: 10-06-10-06The Individual and the Nature o - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title:
SESSION 824, MARCH 1, 1978
9:40 P.M. WEDNESDAY
source_raw.chapter_title: Chapter 10: The Good, the Better, and the Best. Value Fulfillment Versus Competition

quotes.text: The experience, the subjective universe, the "mind" of All That Is, was so brilliant, so distinct, that All That Is almost became lost, mentally wandering within this ever-flourishing, ever-growing interior landscape. Each thought, feeling, dream, or mood was itself indelibly marked with all of the attributes of this infinite subjectivity. Each glowed and quivered with its own creativity, its own desire to create as it had been created.
quotes.translation: 万有之内的体验、主观宇宙、“心智”如此辉煌、如此清晰，以至于万有之内几乎迷失，在内心漫游于这片永远繁茂、不断生长的内在景观中。每一个思想、感受、梦境或情绪都不可磨灭地带有这种无限主观性的所有属性。每一个都因自身的创造力而发光颤抖，因自身渴望像被创造那样去创造而颤动。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 1
quotes.source_file: 10-07-10-07Dreams, _Evolution,_ and Value_One - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: You would find it difficult to attend to or be aware of regular outer stimuli while you dealt with the ordinary function of daily life. Is it then any wonder that the outer ego leaves the inner data to the safekeeping of the inner ego? You are truly babes in the woods, having enough trouble getting along in your own universe. And do not get your feelings hurt, I am not referring to either of you in particular, as you should know by now.
quotes.translation: 当你处理日常生活的普通功能时，你会发现很难关注或意识到常规的外部刺激。那么，外在自我将内在数据交由内在自我保管，这有什么奇怪的呢？你们真是林中的婴儿，在自己的宇宙中相处已经够麻烦了。别伤了感情，我不是特指你们中的任何一个，你们现在应该知道了。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The constructive changing around of the apartment was instinctively correct, however, as was your agreement in both of these instances. Physical activity is an excellent way of using and controlling the effect of aggressive reaction, and will prevent the buildup of aggressive emotions into unsupervised physical constructions, and also prevent the habitual piling up of such aggressions, where detrimental constructions result continually.
quotes.translation: 然而，公寓的创造性改造在直觉上是正确的，正如你们在这两件事上的共识一样。身体活动是运用和控制攻击性反应效果的绝佳方式，能防止攻击性情绪积累成不受控的身体构造，也能避免这类攻击性习惯性堆积，从而不断产生有害的构造。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 2 of The Seth Material (Sessions 43-85)
quotes.source_file: ES-2-The Early Sessions-Book 2 of The Seth Material(43-85).json

quotes.text: The body cannot act tomorrow, today. Its sense data must be clear. This resulting feeling of powerlessness to act leads to a state of hopelessness of varying degrees -- and that mood does not tie itself to specific details, but pervades emotional life if it is allowed to. To whatever degree, the condemning, critical material too often becomes self-prophesying -- for those who put merit on it (loudly) allow it to cloud their reactions.
quotes.translation: 身体无法在今天预支明天的行动。它的感官数据必须清晰。这种无力行动的感觉会导致不同程度的绝望状态——而这种情绪若不加以控制，便不会局限于具体细节，而是弥漫整个情感生活。无论程度如何，那些谴责性的、批判性的材料往往成为自我实现的预言——因为看重它们的人（大声地）允许它们遮蔽自己的反应。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Now, this is not to be a technical book, so this is not the time nor place to discuss thoroughly the action, behavior or effects of these coordinate points; nor of the electromagnetic energy units -- those natural emanations of consciousness of which I spoke. I want it known, however, that thoughts and emotions are formed into physical matter by very definite methods and through laws quite valid, though they may be presently unknown.
quotes.translation: 现在，这并非一本技术性书籍，因此现在不是也不适合深入讨论这些坐标点的行动、行为或效应；也不适合讨论我提到的电磁能量单元——那些意识的自然放射。然而，我希望大家明白，思想和情绪是通过非常明确的方法和相当有效的法则被塑造成物质实相的，尽管这些法则目前可能尚不为人所知。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: This procedure, and your consent, will give Ruburt a feeling of trust without which I do not believe he would otherwise proceed. In other words, the situations and conditions will not be out of your hands at any time. Nor shall we necessarily keep to any one procedure. I believe that certain experiments will allow us to get more complete data through censorship.
quotes.translation: 这一过程以及你的同意，将给予鲁柏一种信任感，没有这种信任，我认为他不会继续下去。换句话说，情况和条件绝不会脱离你们的掌控。我们也不一定非要固守某一种程序。我相信某些实验将让我们通过审查获得更完整的数据。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: Children's play, creativity, and dreams all involve you with the birth of events in the most direct of fashions. The games that you play or habitually observe will, of course, tell you much about the kind of organization that occurs in your own experience. Overall, you organize events around certain emotions. These can be combative, in which there will always be good teams and bad teams, salvation or destruction, winning or losing.
quotes.translation: 儿童的游戏、创造力与梦境，都以最直接的方式让你参与事件的诞生。你玩的或习惯观察的游戏，当然会告诉你很多关于你自身经验中发生的组织方式。总体而言，你围绕某些情绪组织事件。这些情绪可能是好斗的，其中总会有好团队与坏团队、救赎与毁灭、赢与输。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Your daily language deals with separations, divisions, and distinctions. To some extent your language organizes your feelings and emotions. The language of the psyche, however, has at its command many more symbols that can be combined in many more ways, say, than mere letters of an alphabet.
quotes.translation: 你的日常语言处理着分离、划分与区别。在某种程度上，你的语言组织着你的感受与情绪。然而，心灵的词汇掌握着更多符号，这些符号能以比字母表字母多得多的方式组合。
quotes.book: The Nature of the Psyche
quotes.source_file: 10-05-10-05The Nature of the Psyche_ Its H - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: I have said also that this feeling of vitality, and I prefer the term vitality, is moving and itself a part of the living stuff of the universe. Now as these wires pass seemingly from plane to plane they actually form the boundaries of each plane and become subject to the particular laws of the plane. Therefore they become subject to time on your plane. And they become subject to other laws, if not laws of time, on other planes.
quotes.translation: 我也说过，这种生命力感——我更喜欢用"生命力"这个词——是流动的，它本身就是宇宙生命质料的一部分。如今这些"导线"看似从一层平面穿越到另一层平面，实际上它们构成了每层平面的边界，并受该平面特定法则的支配。因此，在你们的层面上，它们受制于时间法则；而在其他层面上，即便不受时间法则约束，也会受制于其他法则。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: The similarity here may not be easy for you to perceive, because of the diverse emotions with which you view childhood and old age. Nevertheless the child, to an unrealized degree, is free of the environmental necessities of his existence. Even while he molds and is molded by his environment, a part of his psyche is still uninvolved. His subconscious, on an uppermost personal level, is concerned with infantile fears of course.
quotes.translation: 这里的相似之处可能不易察觉，因为你们看待童年与老年时带着截然不同的情感。然而，儿童在某种未被充分认识到的程度上，是超脱于其生存环境之必要性的。即使在他塑造环境并被环境塑造的同时，他心灵的某一部分仍保持超然。当然，他的潜意识在个人表层层面上，确实关注着婴儿期的恐惧。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 3 of The Seth Material (Sessions 86-148)
quotes.source_file: ES-3-The Early Sessions-Book 3 of The Seth Material(86-148).json

quotes.text: This is closely related to one of the inner senses, the second inner sense, and it is upon psychological time that you must try to transpose your inner visions. You can see how handicapped we both are because of the difficulties involved in trying to make you understand inner data in terms of outer data, when the two are so apart, really, even while they are so closely connected. For instance, when I tell you that the second sense is like your sense of time, while this does give you a certain understanding or feeling of what this second sense is like, nevertheless it also is confusing, I know, because you are apt to compare the two too closely.
quotes.translation: 这与内在感官之一——第二内在感官——密切相关。你们必须尝试在心理时间中转换内在视像。你们可以明白，我们双方都多么受限——因为试图用外在数据来让你们理解内在数据时，两者实际上如此迥异，即便它们紧密相连。例如，当我告诉你们第二感官类似于你们的时间感时，这虽然能让你们对第二感官有某种理解或感受，但我知道这也令人困惑，因为你们容易将两者过度类比。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 1 of The Seth Material (Sessions 1-42)
quotes.source_file: ES-1-The Early Sessions-Book 1 of The Seth Material(1-42).json

quotes.text: Very nicely in class, she personifies the feelings that each of you have to some degree, involving your inner self.... She shows her feelings in an exaggerated fashion for you to look at, and so when she speaks it is not only for herself but for everyone in this room, including Ruburt.
quotes.translation: 在课堂上，她非常恰当地将你们每个人在某种程度上都拥有的、与内在自我相关的情感具象化了……她以夸张的方式展现情感供你们审视，因此当她说话时，不仅代表她自己，也代表这个房间里的每个人，包括鲁柏。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: Physically then you perceive an approximation of an inner event. The inner event basically is not physical. (Pause.) Now, as simply as I can put this, thoughts and emotions form, of their own electromagnetic reality, vitalized physical products called atoms and molecules. This is basically what I told you in our early analogy with the mazes and the wires.
quotes.translation: 因此，在物质层面，你们感知到的是内在事件的近似物。内在事件本质上并非物质性的。（停顿。）现在，让我尽可能简单地说明：思想和情感凭借其自身的电磁实相，形成了被称为原子和分子的活化物质产物。这基本上就是我在早期用迷宫和导线作类比时告诉你们的。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 9 of The Seth Material (Sessions 422-510)
quotes.source_file: ES-9-The Early Sessions-Book 9 of The Seth Material(422-510).json

quotes.text: Even if a direct solution does not appear, rejuvenation will of itself begin to point you in the proper direction. If you are a woman in an unhappy marriage, for example, you may begin by imagining yourself with a fine suitor. Now: No Sir Galahad may appear, but if the exercise is pursued properly you will automatically begin to feel loved, and therefore worthy of love, and lovable, where before you felt rejected, unworthy and inferior. This feeling of being loved will alter your reality, drawing love to you. You will act loved. Your spouse may then find you exhibiting characteristics of a most pleasant nature, and he himself may change.
quotes.translation: 即使直接解决方案没有出现，焕发活力本身也会开始指引你走向正确方向。例如，如果你是一位身处不幸婚姻中的女性，你可以先想象自己有一位优秀的追求者。注意：可能不会有完美的骑士出现，但如果正确进行这项练习，你会自动开始感受到被爱，因而觉得自己值得被爱、可爱，而在此之前你感到被拒绝、不值得、低人一等。这种被爱的感觉会改变你的实相，将爱吸引到你身边。你会表现得像被爱着的人。你的配偶随后可能会发现你展现出最令人愉悦的特质，而他本人也可能因此改变。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: In an odd way this made it even more difficult for those who did go into the next two, less extensive wars, for the country was not behind either one. Any sense of powerlessness on the part of individual fighting men was given expression as before, this time in a more local blood bath, but the code itself had become shaky. This release was not as accepted as it had been before, even within the ranks. By the last war (in Vietnam), the country was as much against it as for it, and the men's feelings of powerlessness were reinforced after it was over. This is the reason for the incidents of violence on the part of returning servicemen.*
quotes.translation: 奇怪的是，这反而让那些确实参与了接下来两次规模较小的战争的人更加艰难，因为国家并不支持其中任何一场战争。战斗人员个人的无力感像以往一样得到了表达，这次是在更局部的血腥屠杀中，但行为准则本身已经动摇。这种宣泄不再像以前那样被接受，即使在军队内部也是如此。到最后一场战争（越战）时，国家支持与反对的声音旗鼓相当，而战后士兵们的无力感反而加剧了。这就是返乡军人暴力事件频发的原因。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: Many psychological intangibles, including dreams, have such an electric reality, and do exist as electric actions. They are to be reckoned with. The connections between the emotional climate and physical health have never been clearly understood, because emotions were not known to have such electrical reality. They were not known to directly affect the system. More is understood as far as the chemical relationships here.
quotes.translation: 许多心理上的无形之物，包括梦境，都具有如此电性的真实，并且确实作为电性活动而存在。它们必须被认真对待。情绪氛围与身体健康之间的联系从未被清楚理解，因为人们不知道情绪具有这样的电性真实。它们被认为不会直接影响身体系统。在这方面，人们对化学关系的了解相对更多。

quotes.text: The whole psychological structure is therefore entirely different. It is also more demanding. The emotions and intuitions are the basis of experience, and the means of getting knowledge in most other systems. They have their own in quotes "reasoning processes" of which you are little aware, since at your point of development you use the intellect as a rule in its place, and make a clear distinction between the two areas.
quotes.translation: 因此，整个心理结构完全不同。它也要求更高。情绪和直觉是经验的基础，也是大多数其他系统中获取知识的手段。它们有自己所谓的"推理过程"，而你对这些过程几乎毫无察觉，因为在你的发展阶段，你通常用理智来取代它们的位置，并在两者之间做出明确区分。

quotes.text: Because of my own writing experience, I'm also well aware of the process involved in translating unconscious material into conscious reality. It's particularly obvious when I'm working on poetry. Whatever else is involved in Seth's book, certainly some kind of unconscious activity is operating at high gear. It was only natural, then, that I found myself comparing my own conscious creative experience with the trance procedure involved in Seth's book. I wanted to discover why I felt that Seth's book was his, as divorced from mine. If both were coming from the same unconscious, then why the subjective differences in my feelings?
quotes.translation: 由于我自己的写作经验，我也很清楚将无意识材料转化为意识实相所涉及的过程。在我创作诗歌时尤其明显。无论赛斯书的创作还涉及什么，某种无意识活动肯定在高强度运作。于是很自然地，我发现自己将意识层面的创作经验与赛斯书涉及的出神过程进行比较。我想发现为什么我觉得赛斯书是他的，而不是我的。如果两者都来自同一个无意识，那么为什么我的感受会有主观差异？
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

source_raw.title: Seth began dictating the book in our next Session 511, January 21, 1970, and finished it in Session 591, August 11, 1971. The intervening sessions did not all involve book dictation, however. Some were devoted to personal matters, some given for specific people who needed help, and some were in answer to philosophical questions not connected with the book. I also took several "little vacations." Despite such layoffs, Seth always picked right up, precisely where he left off dictation.

quotes.text: The mother could not consciously control the bodily processes that lead to birth. In the truest sense, the birth magically happens, as miraculous in those terms as the so-called initial emergence of life upon the planet itself. Scientific analysis of the brain will tell you nothing about the power that moves your thoughts, or hint at the source of the brain's abilities. However, the constant activity between Frameworks 1 and 2 is constantly apparent in the very existence of your world, and in the relationships involving your imagination, feelings, and beliefs, and those private and shared events that compose your experience.
quotes.translation: 母亲无法有意识地控制导致分娩的身体过程。从最真实的意义上说，分娩是神奇地发生的，就像所谓的生命最初在地球上出现一样神奇。对大脑的科学分析不会告诉你是什么力量在驱动你的思想，也不会暗示大脑能力的来源。然而，框架一和框架二之间的持续活动在你世界的存在本身中，以及在你想象、感受和信念之间的关系中，在构成你经验的私人事件和共享事件中，始终显而易见。

quotes.text: The girl will not be involved in an airplane accident. The boy is using daydreams concerning Australia to avoid facing present problems. There will be a return to Australia, but not for some time. (Long pause) Ruburt is bothered by traffic this evening, and it interferes. I do not want to attempt information on the friends in California until conditions are better. There is however uneasiness, as you know, in the family, and the boy operates in such a manner that he picks up the inner feelings of the others, and is left to handle them as best he can. The mother is more important in the family group than our friends realize.
quotes.translation: 这个女孩不会卷入飞机事故。这个男孩正在利用关于澳大利亚的白日梦来逃避面对当前的问题。他会回到澳大利亚，但不是近期。（长停顿）鲁柏今晚被交通困扰，这干扰了进程。在条件好转之前，我不想尝试提供关于加州朋友的信息。然而，如你所知，家庭中存在不安，这个男孩以这样的方式运作，他接收了其他人的内在感受，并只能尽力处理它们。母亲在家庭群体中的重要性超过了我们朋友的认识。

In any traveling Wendell might have done from his home in Edgewater, NJ, to New York City, trains and cars could very well have been involved.

quotes.text: Usually his aggressive feelings are automatically sublimated into his work. He grapples with ideas and with words. The destructive energy however found no outlet. It therefore took the point of least resistance, and he quite subconsciously of course projected it upon his pet. It was not that he actually focused the energy purposely upon the cat, merely that the cat's spongelike psychic nature received it full force.
quotes.translation: 通常他的攻击性情绪会自动升华到工作中。他与思想和文字搏斗。但破坏性能量找不到出口，于是选择了阻力最小的路径——他完全下意识地将它投射到宠物身上。并非他刻意将能量聚焦于猫，而是猫海绵般的灵性本质全盘接收了这股力量。

quotes.text: (A one-minute pause.) Each natural element had its own key system that interlocked with others, forming channels through which consciousness could flow from one kind of life to another. Man understood himself to be a separate entity, but one that was connected to all of nature. The emotional reaches of his subjective life, then, leapt far beyond what you think of as private experience. Each person participating fully in a storm, for example, still participated in his or her own individual way. Yet the grandeur of the emotions was allowed full sway, and the seasons of the earth and the world were jointly felt.
quotes.translation: （停顿一分钟。）每种自然元素都有与其他元素环环相扣的关键系统，形成意识能在不同生命形态间流动的通道。人类理解自己是独立的个体，却与整个自然相连。主观生命的情感疆域远超出你们所谓的私人体验。例如每个人都在以自己的方式全然参与风暴，但情感的壮阔得以充分展现，大地的季节与世界的脉动被共同感知。

quotes.text: Basically you create your experience through your beliefs about yourself and the nature of reality. Another way to understand this is to realize that you create your experiences through your expectations. Your feeling-tones are your emotional attitudes toward yourself and life in general, and these generally govern the large areas of experience.
quotes.translation: 根本上，你通过对自己和实相本质的信念创造体验。另一种理解方式是：你通过期待创造体验。你的感觉基调是你对自身和生命整体的情感态度，这些态度通常主导着经验的主要领域。

quotes.text: The period should result in a feeling of refreshment. Your energies afterwards however should be directed outward. You are doing very well indeed, and not overdoing it. There is a good balance here. I simply want to point out that here certain characteristics are being well used. They could also be ill-used, and so the energy should then be directed outward, as indeed it is since the supper hour so shortly follows.
quotes.translation: 这段时间应带来焕然一新的感受。之后你的能量应向外释放。你做得非常好，没有过度消耗。这里保持着良好平衡。我只想指出某些特质正在被善用，但它们也可能被误用，因此能量应转向外界——正如晚餐时间紧随其后时实际发生的那样。

quotes.text: You did not consider it refined, in terms of process, enough. Neither did you find yourself at home however with the acrylics, that you felt were over refined, and too far away from basics. Your problem being your overall attitude toward the Emotion from which your art springs, for your attitude is then projected outward upon your mediums. To some extent the present dilemma was initiated by your parents' condition.
quotes.translation: 你认为它在过程层面不够精致。同时你发现自己在丙烯画中也不自在，觉得它过于精致，离本源太远。问题在于你对孕育艺术的情感所持的整体态度——这种态度会投射到你的媒介上。某种程度上，当前困境始于你父母的状态。

quotes.text: I am speaking generally here, for remember that your individual beliefs, thoughts, and emotions cause your reality, so no person dies ahead of his or her time. The individual chooses the time of death. It is true, however, that many cancers and conditions such as AIDS result because the immunity system has been so tampered with that the body has not been allowed to follow through with its own balancing procedures.
quotes.translation: 我在此泛泛而谈，因为请记住，你个人的信念、思想和情绪造就了你的实相，所以没有人会提前死亡。个体选择死亡的时间。然而，确实许多癌症和诸如艾滋病之类的状况，是因为免疫系统受到严重干扰，以至于身体无法完成其自身的平衡程序。

Our friends wanted to celebrate the publication of her first "psychic" book, her first mention of Seth, but she read poetry at the party and wouldn't talk about the ESP book -- too embarrassed. A strange, protective way to behave.

quotes.text: With your growing population it becomes more difficult to discharge this energy in what I would call natural ways; that is, by letting it spill over into the nonidentity molecular realm. Privation naturally leads to great aggression of feeling. Where there is great privation there will be a cruel climate, but the climate does not cause the privation. The emotional aggression caused by privation caused the climate.
quotes.translation: 随着人口的增长，以我所谓的自然方式释放这种能量变得更加困难；也就是说，让它溢出进入非认同的分子领域。匮乏自然会导致强烈的情感攻击。在极度匮乏的地方，会有残酷的气候，但气候并不导致匮乏。由匮乏引起的情感攻击导致了气候。

quotes.text: If you continue, you will indeed discover that you are aware of some future events, when such knowledge is not available in usual terms. If you persist, then over a period of time you will discover that you do very well in certain areas, while in others you may fail miserably. There will be associative patterns that you follow successfully, leading toward "correct" precognitions. You will also discover that the emotions are highly involved in such procedures: You will perceive information that is significant to you for some reason. That significance will act like a magnet, attracting those data to you.
quotes.translation: 如果你继续下去，你确实会发现，当这种知识在通常意义上不可得时，你却能意识到一些未来事件。如果你坚持，那么在一段时间后，你会发现自己在某些领域做得很好，而在其他领域可能惨败。会有一些联想模式，你成功遵循它们，导向“正确”的预知。你还会发现，情绪在此类过程中高度参与：你会感知到因某种原因对你重要的信息。那种重要性会像磁铁一样，吸引那些数据到你身边。

quotes.text: There is no other valid way of changing physical events. It might help if you imagine an inner living dimension within yourself in which you create, in miniature psychic form, all the exterior conditions that you know. Simply put, you do exactly this. Your thoughts, feelings and mental pictures can be called incipient exterior events, for in one way or another each of these is materialized into physical reality.
quotes.translation: 没有其他有效的方法可以改变物质事件。如果你想象自己内在有一个活生生的维度，在其中你以微缩的心灵形式创造你所知的所有外在条件，这可能会有所帮助。简单地说，你正是这样做的。你的思想、感受和心理画面可以被称为初期的外在事件，因为每一种都以某种方式被物质化到物质实相中。

quotes.text: You understand now what space is, or is not, in physical terms. In psychic terms also there is something that can be loosely explained in terms of spatial relationships, but having to do with intensities. A strong intensity of a particular nature can give you the feeling of clutter or lack of space. This is the nearest I can come at this moment in telling you how this particular sort of transformation can occur.
quotes.translation: 你现在从物质角度理解了空间是什么，或不是什么。从心灵角度来说，也有某种东西可以粗略地用空间关系来解释，但涉及强度。某种特定性质的强烈强度会让你感到杂乱或空间不足。这是我此刻能告诉你这种特定转化如何发生的最接近的方式。

quotes.text: Joy and the spontaneous expression of it will always bring increased strength and resiliency to the personality. It also brings deep and abiding satisfaction to the subconscious, which is much more joyful than it is given credit for. The subconscious, for example, the personal subconscious, takes great pleasure in its manipulation of the physical fibers in locomotion. The expression of joy also makes the ego more resilient, less fearful, less resentful of diverse conditions when they occur. The emotion itself is an automatic signal that unites the conscious and subconscious in shared experience.
quotes.translation: 喜悦及其自发表达总会给个性带来增强的力量和韧性。它还给潜意识带来深刻而持久的满足，潜意识比人们通常认为的要快乐得多。例如，个人潜意识在操纵运动中的物质纤维时获得极大乐趣。喜悦的表达也使自我更具韧性，不那么恐惧，对发生的各种状况不那么怨恨。这种情绪本身是一个自动信号，将意识和潜意识在共享体验中联合起来。

quotes.text: Your reactions the other night, Joseph, had to do with two things; this sense of unjustified inferiority with your own ability to deal with the outside world, hence your physical immobility and back spasm; and with a superficial, rationalizing and false protective measure that operates intellectually in your case, making you think that outside conditions are so stupid that you refuse to do anything to alleviate them, feeling that the situation is so ridiculous that nothing you could do would change it. This is a rationalization to cover up the underlying, completely false sense of inferiority.
quotes.translation: 约瑟，你前几晚的反应涉及两件事：一是对自己应对外在世界的能力产生无根据的自卑感，导致身体僵硬和背部痉挛；二是你理性化、虚假的自我保护措施在智力层面运作，让你认为外界状况愚蠢至极，拒绝采取任何改善行动，觉得局面荒谬到无论做什么都无法改变。这是掩盖深层完全虚假自卑感的合理化借口。

quotes.text: There are purposes not nearly as easy to describe, however, intents of a psychological nature, yearnings toward satisfactions not so easily categorized. Man experiences ambitions, desires, likes and dislikes of a highly emotional nature -- and at the same time he has intellectual beliefs about himself, his feelings, and the world. These are the result of training, for you use your mind as you have been taught.
quotes.translation: 然而存在一些不易描述的意图——心理层面的意图，对难以归类满足感的渴望。人类体验着高度情绪化的抱负、欲望、好恶，同时对自己、感受和世界持有理性信念。这些信念源于训练，因为你按所学方式运用心智。

Remember, these are all adjacent levels, going out horizontally.
quotes.translation: 第一种状态A-1-a对你最实用也最易达成，但通常你仍需对A-1层级保有良好感受，才愿迈出下一步。不过它在局限内允许极大扩展。运用它，你能发现例如"若我做了这个或那个"会如何发展。记住，这些都是横向延伸的相邻层级。

quotes.text: Shadows can be pleasant and luxurious, and on a hot sunny day you are certainly aware of their beneficial nature. So some dream hallucinations are beautiful, comforting, refreshing. They can bring great peace and be sought after for themselves. You may believe that God exists as a kindly father, or you might personify him as Christ or Buddha. In your dreams you might then encounter such personages. They are quite valid, but they are also hallucinations cast by your own thoughts and feelings. Dreams of Heaven and Hell alike fall into the same category, in those terms, as hallucinations.
quotes.translation: 阴影可以是愉悦奢华的，在炎热晴日你定能体会其有益本质。同样，某些梦的幻觉美丽、抚慰、清新，能带来极大安宁，令人主动追寻。你可能相信上帝如慈父存在，或将祂人格化为基督或佛陀。梦中或会遇见此类人物——他们真实有效，却也是你思想情感投射的幻觉。就此而言，天堂与地狱之梦同属幻觉范畴。

This section also includes discussions on the True Dream-Art Scientist, the True Mental Physicist, and the Complete Physician, as well as material on subatomic particles and the spin of electrons in relationship to perceived reality.

quotes.text: The intensity is the core about which the electromagnetic energy units form. In your terms, the more intense the core, the sooner the physical materialization. This would apply whether the mental image was a fearful one or a joyful one. Now there is a very important problem here: If your turn of mind is highly intense and you think in vivid mental emotional images, these will be swiftly formed into physical events. If you are also of a highly pessimistic nature, given to thoughts and feelings of potential disaster, then these thoughts will be quite faithfully reproduced in experience.
quotes.translation: 强度是电磁能量单位凝聚的核心。以你们的时间观，核心越强烈，物质化速度越快——无论心象是恐惧还是喜悦。这里有个重要问题：若你思维高度激烈，善于生动的情感意象，这些意象将迅速转化为现实事件。若你同时极度悲观，惯于思考潜在灾难，这些念头便会忠实再现于经历中。

quotes.text: The differences between any of those systems of thought and Christianity may be so apparent that the similarities escape you. You may follow one of the schools of Buddhism in which great stress is laid upon the denial of the body, discipline of the flesh, and the avoidance of desire. These elements are quite characteristic of Christianity also, of course, but they may appear more palatable, exotic, or reasonable coming from a source foreign to your childhood education. So you may leap from one to the other, shouting emancipation and feeling yourself quite free of old limiting ideas.
quotes.translation: 这些思想体系与基督教之间的差异可能如此明显，以至于相似之处反而被忽略了。你或许会追随某个佛教流派，其中极力强调否定身体、克制肉体、避免欲望。这些特质当然也是基督教的典型特征，但若源自与你童年教育相异的源头，它们可能显得更易接受、更具异域风情或更合理。于是你从一个体系跳到另一个，高喊着解放，感觉自己完全摆脱了旧有的限制性观念。
quotes.book: The Nature of Personal Reality
quotes.source_file: 10-02-10-02The Nature of Personal Reality_ - Roberts, Jane.json

quotes.text: You are intuitively aware that you form your image, and that you are independent of it. You do not realize that you create your larger environment and the physical world as you know it by propelling your thoughts and emotions into matter -- a breakthrough into three-dimensional life. The inner self, therefore, individually and en masse, sends its psychic energy out, forming tentacles that coalesce into form.
quotes.translation: 你直觉地意识到你形成了自己的形象，并且你独立于它。你并未认识到，你通过将自己的思想和情感推进物质——一次突破进入三维生命的跃进——创造了你所知的更大环境和物质世界。因此，内在自体，无论是单独还是集体，向外发送其心灵能量，形成触须，这些触须凝聚成形态。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: He, as I explained earlier, has a strong spontaneous self, which he had been taught to fear. It was because of distrust of this spontaneous self that he accepted your suggestions so readily and without argument. This did indeed result in further lack of mobility, with strong Emotional blockages because of his feeling for you. This explains some of the difficulties incidentally in your intimate life together.
quotes.translation: 正如我之前解释的，他有一个强烈的自发自体，而他被教导要害怕它。正是由于对这种自发自体的不信任，他才如此轻易且毫无争辩地接受了你的建议。这确实导致了进一步的活动力缺失，并因他对你的感受而产生了强烈的情感阻塞。这顺便解释了你亲密生活中一些困难的原因。
quotes.book: The Early Sessions: Book 8 of The Seth Material (Sessions 334-421)
quotes.source_file: ES-8-The Early Sessions-Book 8 of The Seth Material(334-421).json

quotes.text: You cooperate together to form the physical reality that you know, telepathically, through ways and means that are unknown to you. You weave webs of psychic reality that then coalesce into physical reality. You do not necessarily weave them alone, but together. Your thoughts intertwine with those of others. You are responsible for your own thoughts. You need to learn the power of thought and emotion, but this should fill you with the joy of creativity. Once you realize that your thoughts form reality, then you are no longer a slave to events. You simply have to learn the methods.
quotes.translation: 你们合作，通过你们未知的方式和手段，以心电感应形成你们所知的物质实相。你们编织心灵实相的网，然后这些网凝聚成物质实相。你们不一定独自编织，而是共同编织。你们的思想与他人交织在一起。你们对自己的思想负责。你需要学习思想和情感的力量，但这应让你充满创造的喜悦。一旦你认识到你的思想形成实相，你就不再是事件的奴隶。你只需学习方法。
quotes.book: Seth Speaks: The Eternal Validity of the Soul
quotes.source_file: 10-01-10-01Seth Speaks_ The Eternal Validity of the soul - Jane Roberts.json

quotes.text: "His ideas as he tried to explain them to you earlier this evening are excellent. They revive him psychologically, particularly with your help, as he discusses them. The sinful-self material is 'timed' in its own fashion so that although there is a good deal of material already written, its effects are periodic--that is, they are clued to spring into even greater insight, which may not be apparent at any one given time. It is important that he tells you when he does feel panicky. However, the feeling itself does not last long. Relaxation, again, is a part of the creative process.
quotes.translation: "他今晚早些时候试图向你解释的想法非常出色。这些想法在心理上使他焕发活力，尤其是在你的帮助下，当他讨论它们时。'罪孽自体'的材料以它自己的方式'定时'，因此尽管已有大量材料写就，其效果是周期性的——也就是说，它们被设定为能激发更深的洞见，这种洞见在任何特定时刻可能并不明显。重要的是，当他确实感到恐慌时，他告诉你。然而，这种感觉本身不会持续太久。放松，再次，是创造过程的一部分。
quotes.book: Dreams, Evolution, and Value Fulfillment Vol. 2

source_raw.title: 7. In Chapter 2 of Dreams, in Volume 1, see Note 1 for Session 885, which we held on October 24, 1979. Through a series of misunderstandings, the people at Ankh-Hermes had published an abridged translation of Seth Speaks without having permission for the cutting from Jane and me. We still feel regret that the company had to go to all of that extra expense in order to publish a second edition of the book. I also wrote that "all concerned must wait for at least another year before a full-length version of Seth Speaks will be published in the Dutch language." Actually, almost two years passed before we received our copies of the new edition.

quotes.text: When you leave the physical system after reincarnations, you have learned the lessons, and you are literally no longer a member of the human race in those terms, for you elect to leave it. Only the conscious self dwells within it in any case, and it is other portions of your personality who simultaneously dwell within the other training systems. In other more advanced systems, thoughts and emotions are automatically and immediately translated into action, into camouflage, into whatever approximation of matter there exists. Therefore the lessons must be taught and learned well.
quotes.translation: 当你转世后离开物质系统时，你已经学到了功课，从那些角度来说，你实际上不再是人类种族的一员，因为你选择离开它。无论如何，只有意识自体居住其中，而你人格的其他部分同时居住在其他训练系统中。在其他更先进的系统中，思想和情感会自动且立即转化为行动，转化为伪装，转化为任何存在的物质近似物。因此，功课必须被教导并学好。

quotes.text: "It all gives me this feeling of great hilarity that I often have about these ideas. And the thought of families of consciousness merging for different reasons -- even while I accept that all of this is put in very limited terms -- seems to have such perfect inner logic and delightful playfulness about it that I launch into the mergings notion with all kinds of questions, and impressions exploding outward.
quotes.translation: 这一切都带给我一种经常与这些观念相伴的极度欢愉感。即便我明白所有表述都受限于有限词汇，但想到意识家族因不同缘由融合——这种想法本身具有如此完美的内在逻辑与令人愉悦的嬉戏性，以至于我带着各种疑问与向外迸发的印象，一头扎进融合的概念中。
